parts.description: Bloodroot Flowers are inert when directly ingested or touched.

parts.name: Bloodroot Roots
parts.description: Bloodroot Roots can be chewed to reduce disease-type damage by 2L wounds (per injury)

parts.name: Bobotuber Pus
parts.description:
Bobotuber pus, if touched, will produce psychedelic effects that last approximately 2 hours. Specifically, the one exposed will experience life-like nightmarish visions. For every 15 minutes of that time, the one exposed should roll 1d4 (no bonuses or penalties apply) taking the following amount of anxiety-type damage respective of the die value:
1. 2L
2. 1M
3. 1H
4. 2H

parts.name: Boomberries
parts.description: Boomberries are highly temperamental berries known to explode with ease.
parts.raw_effects: If one attempts to handle boomberries, they must attempt the Preparation and Proper Handling of Boomberries Proficiency. If this proficiency is not known to the individual handling the boomberries or if the proficiency is failed, the individual takes 1 heavy wound of explosive damage.
parts.effect_in_potions: The boomberry's effect in potions is only somewhat known. Its explosive quality is obvious, but many theorists suspect additional effects can be found.

parts.name: Boomberry Leaves
parts.description: Paradoxically, boomberry leaves help heal eruptive damage (even though boomberries themselves can be used to create explosives). Packing an eruptive type wound with boomberry leaves can heal 4H wounds of eruptive damage (once per injury).

parts.name: Borage Flowers
parts.description: Eating Borage Flowers can reduce depressive damage by 1M wound (once per injury).

parts.name: Bouncing Bulb Vines
parts.description: Bouncing Bulb Vines are safe to touch and ingest.

parts.name: Bouncing Bulbs
parts.description:
Bouncing Bulbs that are removed from their plant are still aggressive. However, removing them makes them weaker. They will attempt to bounce aggressively onto their aggressor causing 1L wounds of blunt force damage (set value 5).
If you make an herbology roll of 5, you can deftly constrain their movement without taking damage.
Once a bouncing bulb is removed from a plant for 6 hours, it will die (and is no longer aggressive).

parts.name: Bulbadox Leaves
parts.description:
Touching Bulbadox Leaves will cause one to break out into boils (which remain for three days). Ingesting them will cause severe internal bleeding leading to 1H wound per day (damage over time; for 10 days).
To successfully handle Bulbadox Leaves, make an herbology roll of 7 or higher.

parts.name: Bulrush Fibers
parts.description: Bulrush Fibers on their own treat snake bites (1M wound per hour) when rubbed on the bite. Many researchers have aimed to find a more potent concoction.

parts.name: Bulrush Roots
parts.description: These roots are inert when ingested or touched.

parts.name: Bursting Mushroom Caps
parts.description: Even once they are properly extracted, Bursting Mushroom Caps can still explode. Those transporting must make repeated herbology checks of 12 or higher. If the caps explode, the holder takes 1 medium wound of eruptive damage.

parts.name: Sliced Bursting Mushrooms
parts.description: Bursting Mushrooms, when sliced, help to protect against a potion exploding. If using volitile ingredients, you may want to add this in.

parts.name: Bell Peppers
parts.description: A tasty pepper easily extracted from capsicum. Muggles may extract this without effort as well.

parts.name: Beans
parts.description: These are beans
parts.raw_effects: You can eat em. They're nutritious.

parts.name: Cayenne Pepper
parts.description: Many believe that cayenne can help one who is suffering from over-imbibations to recover. Others believe that it may help with outright detox from addictions.

parts.name: Cedarwood Bark
parts.description: Cedarwood Bark is inert when chewed or touched.

parts.name: A Centaury Stem
parts.description: Centaury Stem is inert when directly ingested or touched.

parts.name: Centaury Flower
parts.description: Centaury Flowers are inert when directly ingested or touched.

parts.name: Chelidonium Secretion
parts.description:
Chelidonium Secretion is a mild sedative. If one touches it, they will become slightly slower and more fatigued (but won't automatically sleep). It also heals anxiety-type damage by 1M wound.
When ingested, these effects are magnified (and anxiety-type damage is healed by 1H wound).

parts.name: Cherries
parts.description: Cherries are a stabilizing agent. They also help a little with headaches.

parts.name: Cherry Tree Bark
parts.description: Cherry Tree Bark is inert when chewed or touched.

parts.name: Chili Pepper
parts.description: Chili pepper makes one hiccup (either on its own or when added to a potion).

parts.name: Chinese Chomping Cabbage Leaves
parts.description: Chinese Chomping Cabbage Leaves are edible and are sometimes included in high-end dining experiences (in salads) as something of a flex... It's like a "Look at us, we're so elite that we'll go kill this aggressive magical cabbage just so you can eat a little of it. Aren't we so cool?" kinda thing.

parts.name: Cinnamon Bark
parts.description: Chewing Cinnamon can ward off any illness damage 2L wounds or less. This does not "stack" with other illness preventatives.

parts.name: Cinquefoil Flowers
parts.description: Cinquefoil Flowers are mostly inert to ingest or touch. When one is near death, ingesting Cinquefoil Flowers stabilizes the heart temporarily. If one received a mortal wound, they may roll 1d4. Rolls of 1 mean the consumer dies. Any other roll keeps the consumer alive for another 5 seconds.

parts.name: Whole Cloves
parts.description: Cloves are mostly inert when sucked on or ingested. They produce no effects when touched, either.

parts.name: Cobra Lily Tongues
parts.description: Cobra Lily Tongues causes 4H wounds when ingested. It is safe to touch when removed from its plant.

parts.name: Coffee Bean
parts.description: Coffee Bean is inert to ingest or touch. If you eat copious amounts of coffee beans, you may experience increased alertness and anxiety.

parts.name: Common Cabbage Leaves
parts.description: Cabbage is a basic food that is safe to ingest and touch.

parts.name: Common Mushroom Caps
parts.description: Common Mushrooms are fairly tasteless and entirely (magically) useless when directly ingested. These things are just tasty when you dump em into a stew.

parts.name: Common Rue Cuttings
parts.description: Rue Cuttings are inert when ingested or touched.

parts.name: Coneflower Petals
parts.description: Coneflower is inert when directly ingested.

parts.name: Cornflower Petals
parts.description: Cornflower Petals are a common stabilizing agent.

parts.name: Cowbane Flowers
parts.description: Cowbane is a powerful poison causing 5H wounds to any who ingest it. It is particularly poisonous to cattle or cattle-like species (causing 10H wounds to those species).

plants.name: Creeper
plants.description: Creepers are plants with long, thick arms that stretch in many directions. They tend to "creep" toward magical objects and locations.

parts.name: Creeper Vines
parts.description: Creeper vines will pulsate when in the vicinity of magical objects (threshold 12). No clear record exists to document the effects of ingesting these vines directly. It is inert to the touch.

parts.name: Daisy Flowers
parts.description: Daisy Flowers are a very mild pain reliever when directly ingested. This can heal 1L wound of any physical damage or psychological damage that occurred as a result of pain. Can be used once per three hours for a maximum healing of 1H wound. In most cases the stinger/piercing object must be removed prior to attempting to heal piercing damage, but since this spell works by relieving pain, it does heal piercing damage even if the piercing object is still inside the target.

parts.name: Daisy Roots
parts.description: Daisy Roots are inert when directly ingested.

parts.name: Dandelion Petals
parts.description: Dandelion Petals are inert to ingest and touch.

parts.name: Datura Leaves
parts.description: The direct ingestion of these plants typically causes little effect unless ingested in copious amounts. In smaller animals, very young children, or when ingested in copious quantities, the leaves produce an 36 hour incapacitation due to delirium.

parts.name: Severed Death Caps Heads
parts.description: This is a powerful poison. On its own, it causes 7H wounds per cap when ingested.

parts.name: Devil's Snare Vine
parts.description: These vines are technically edible, but they are disgusting. They are safe to touch.

parts.name: Dittany Stems
parts.description: Dittany stems, when directly ingested (chewed), can heal 1M wound of disease/toxic/poison damage.

parts.name: Dogbane Leaves
parts.description: Dogbane Leaves are poisonous to all animals and creatures. Eating the leaves produces 3H wounds of poisonous damage.

plants.name: Enchanted Creeper
plants.description: Only one instance of this plant is known to have existed. It was enchanted by an unknown wizard and nearly overtook Hogwarts castle in the school year 2000-2001.

parts.name: Enchanted Creeper Vines
parts.description: Creeper vines will pulsate when in the vicinity of magical objects (threshold 15). No clear record exists to document the effects of ingesting these vines directly. It is inert to the touch.
parts.effect_in_potions: When added to potions, the potion appears to have some effect on vitality and possibly aging. Very few studies have been conducted on the substance.

parts.name: Fanged Geranium Fangs
parts.description: Fanged Geranium Fangs are safe to touch. They will produce 2H wounds of vital damage if ingested.

parts.name: Fig Leaves
parts.description: Typical fig leaves

parts.name: Figs
parts.description: Figs are tasty to eat! They are magically inert to ingest and touch.

parts.name: Fire Seed Embers
parts.description:
Fire seed embers must be handled with protective gloves (by most creatures). Eating a fire seed ember is an awfully stupid decision and will cause 4H wounds immediately (and 1H wound per second thereafter).
These embers will burn continuously for 25 days after they are removed from their bush. If you get too close to them (without proper protection), you will suffer 2L wounds of burn damage. If you touch them, you will experience 1H wound per second until you are no longer making contact. If one’s clothes or fur catch fire, the damage will continue.

parts.name: Fire Seeds
parts.description:
Fire seeds must be handled with protective gloves (by most creatures). Eating a fire seed is an awfully stupid decision and will cause 4H wounds immediately (and 1H wound per second thereafter).
They will burn continuously for 3 months after they are removed from their bush (after which time the seeds cannot sprout). If you get too close to them (without proper protection), you will suffer 2L wounds of burn damage. If you touch them, you will experience 1H wound per second until you are no longer making contact. If one’s clothes or fur catch fire, the damage will continue.

parts.name: Flitterbloom Tentacles
parts.description: Flitterbloom Tentacles are inert to touch and ingest. They do, honestly, taste pretty gross though.

parts.name: Fluxweed Stems
parts.description: Fluxweed produces a +1 bonus to transfiguration casting when ingested (once per day; effects last all day). It produces no effects when touched.

parts.name: Flesh of the Fly Amanita
parts.description: Directly ingesting this flesh leads to 1H wound of sanity shaking damage per unit consumed.

parts.name: Foxglove Bulbs
parts.description: Floxglove is inert when directly ingested or touched.

parts.name: Garlic Clove
parts.description: Garlic is inert when directly ingested or touched. However, it will repel vampires or any creature with significant vampiric bloodlines.

parts.name: Germander Leaves
parts.description: Germander can slightly reduce a fever when directly ingested (curing 1L wound of vital damage when one is near death).

plants.name: Ghost Orchid
plants.description: Ghost Orchid is a rare plant connected with the spirit realm. It can often be spotted growing near areas that spirits inhabit.

parts.name: Ghost Orchid Blooms
parts.description: These blooms must be captured at midnight exactly, or they are rendered magically inert.
parts.effect_in_potions: Typically impacts some aspect of the spirit realm.

parts.name: Ghost Orchid Vapors
parts.description: These vapors are emitted by the Ghost Orchid and can be collected in a phial.

parts.name: Clump of Gillyweed
parts.description: Once removed from the root, a Clump of Gillyweed can be ingested to allow the consumer to breath underwater for approximately 1 hour. Swimming skill is also substantially enhanced (water agility: 10; water speed 10).

parts.name: Gillyweed Slime
parts.description: Gillyweed on its own can be used to breath underwater for about 1 hour. Gillyweed slime, however, just helps treat coughs.

parts.name: Ginger Root
parts.description: Ginger Root is safe to chew on in small doses and may calm an upset stomach. Larger doses will lead to 1M wound of vital damage per overdose. It is safe to touch.

parts.name: Golden Apples
parts.description: Golden Apples are beautiful, but few uses have been found for them other than the Thick Golden Potion. Some researchers believe that Golden Apples can be used in advanced curse-containing concoctions.

parts.name: Goosegrass Blades
parts.description: Goosegrass Blades are inert when directly ingested or touched.

parts.name: Green Ti Plant Leaves
parts.description: Green Ti Plant appears inert to ingest or touch. It is used in some rituals.

parts.name: Greenheart Bark
parts.description: This bark produces no direct effects when ingested, chewed, or touched

parts.name: Gurdyroot Bulbs
parts.description: Gurdyroot as a fish repellant. In its raw form, it will only repel and damage plimpies. However, other small fish and sea creatures may be repelled or harmed by large concentrations of the bulbs.

parts.name: Hellebore Sap
parts.description: Hellebore Sap is the undiluted sap of the hellebore plant and it is a poison (3H wounds if ingested; 2H wounds if touched). One must make an herbology roll of 12 or higher anytime they are handling this substance. Failing this roll will lead to a moderate paralysis lasting approximately 12 hours.

parts.name: Leaves of Hemlock
parts.description: Hemlock alone is a potent poison (8H wounds per leaf).

plants.name: Henbane
plants.description: Henbane is a historic magical plant. Its seeds, stems and leaves all produce psychotropic effects.

parts.name: Horseradish Roots
parts.description: Chewing Horseradish Roots at the moment that one attempts to place a curse on you will block many curses (set value 12). However, the effect wears off quickly (after about 5 minutes) and can't be reused for 5 days.

parts.name: Henbane Seeds
parts.description: Henbane seeds have a psychoactive seed.
parts.raw_effects: These highly potent seeds can produce a perception-altering effect. This is inert at low doses, but if many seeds are consumed, the individual will take ever-increasing sanity shaking damage (instantaneous and non-lasting) and vital damage (which may persist for a few days). The degree of damage is dose-dependent and determined by the headmaster.

parts.name: Henna Oil
parts.description: Henna Oil promotes hair growth when directly ingested.

parts.name: Horseradish Roots
parts.description: Chewing Horseradish Roots at the moment that one attempts to place a curse on you will block many curses (set value 12). However, the effect wears off quickly (after about 5 minutes) and can't be reused for 5 days.

parts.name: Hydrangea Flowers
parts.description:
Hydrangea Flowers are particularly accepting recipients of placed curses. Essentially, one can use artificing to place a curse on these flowers. The artificing roll will receive a +5 bonus. As a result, many in the magical community have learned to be very careful about approaching or touching Hydrangea Flowers.
Many artificing courses utilize Hydrangea Flowers as a practice tool.

parts.name: Jewelweed Gasses
parts.description: Jewelweed gasses, when directly inhaled, will kill fungal infections completely (including spattergroit infections).

parts.name: Kelp Blades
parts.description: Kelp Blades when directly ingested, improve hearing and sight (perception checks +2) for about 10 minutes.

parts.name: Knotgrass Knots
parts.description: Knotgrass Knots can be eaten raw to improve magical creatures checks (+2).

parts.name: Lady's Mantle Seeds
parts.description: These seeds can be chewed to calm one's stomach. This will reduce disease/toxic/poisonous damage by 1H wound per injury.

parts.name: Lady's Mantle Leaves
parts.description: Lady's Mantle is inert when touched or ingested.

parts.name: Gloops of Lakeweed
parts.description:
These are safe, but gross, to touch. Ingesting them isn't harmful... it's just weird! Like, what are you doing just chomping on a bunch of slimy weeds you found in a lake.
You do you... but, ew?

parts.name: Lavender Flowers
parts.description: These flowers can be smashed and rubbed together in one's hand, then breathed in to significantly reduce anxiety type damage (1H wound per injury).

parts.name: Lavender Roots
parts.description: Lavender root produces no effects when ingested, chewed, or touched.

parts.name: Formerly Leaping Toadstools
parts.description: Eating formerly leaping toadstools by themselves allows the ingester to use an additional agility die when rolling an agility check.

parts.name: Lemons
parts.description: Lemons are safe to eat and touch. They are just super sour and tart!

parts.name: Lemongrass Blades
parts.description: Lemongrass Blades, enhance night vision and perception when chewed by a magical creature (including wizards).

parts.name: Lily Bulbs
parts.description: Lily Bulbs can be chewed to provide a significant boost to history checks (+4).

parts.name: Ling Ovaries
parts.description: Ling ovaries, on their own, are inert.

parts.name: Lovage Flowers
parts.description: Lovage Flowers, when smoked, can counteract 1M wound of disease-related damage due to bladder problems or gastrointestinal issues.

parts.name: Maguey Sweet Sap
parts.description: Safe to ingest and touch

parts.name: Mallowsweet Cuttings
parts.description: Mallowsweet can be ingested safely and is slightly sweet to taste.

parts.name: Mandrake Root
parts.description: Injesting mandrake root causes 3H wounds of poison damage.

parts.name: Whole Harvested Mandrake
parts.description: Mandrakes will bite you if you try to eat them (causing 1M wound of blunt force damage; set value 6). To touch them, you must make an herbology roll of 5 (without crit failing). If you fail this roll, they will bite you (same values as listed above).

parts.name: Manjistha Roots
parts.description: Manjistha Roots, on their own, are inert to chew or touch.

parts.name: Mayflower Petals
parts.description: Mayflower Petals are inert when directly ingested.

parts.name: Mercurialis Annua Flowers
parts.description: Mercurialis Annua Flowers can be scattered to repel dogs, werewolves, wolves, and other similar canine-like creatures. Neither ingesting or touching the flowers will produce any effects.

parts.name: Mimbletonia Stinksap
parts.description: If you can convince a creature to ingest a stinksap (which most will refuse to do), it can serve as a powerful healing agent for most creature-injuries (1H wound per stinksap, up to 5 stinksaps per day). Many creatures refuse to injest this because it smells so bad! Dogs don't mind and will always eat these.

parts.name: Minted Oils
parts.description: Minted Oils can heal 1L wound of vital damage per injury.

parts.name: Mistletoe Berries
parts.description: Mistletoe berries are thought to be useful in longing-related potions. In other potions, it can serve as a stabilizing agent.

parts.name: Dried Mistletoe
parts.description: Dried Mistletoe is thought to have the potential to improve cheeriness and increase feelings of affection in potions.

parts.name: Freshly Hanging Mistletoe
parts.description: Freshly Handing Mistletoe is thought to support romance and love potions.

parts.name: Moly Bulbs
parts.description: Moly Bulbs, when touched to bare skin, will strip away all enchantments. Rubbing them on magical objects may damage the object if the enchantment has a set value between 11 and 20. It will remove the enchantment if the set value is 10 or below.

parts.name: Moly Stems
parts.description: Moly Stems are inert when ingested or touched.

parts.name: Moondew Flowers
parts.description: Moondew Flowers provide a +1 bonus to divination checks.

parts.name: Moondew Nectar
parts.description: Moondew Nectar produces a +1 bonus to astronomy checks.

parts.name: Moonseeds
parts.description: These seeds, if actively chewed while confronting frightening or sanity-shaking situations, can help the consumer to stay calm and grounded to reality.

parts.name: Moonseed Leaves
parts.description: Moonseeds help the consumer stay alert (blocking fatiguing effects for 3 hours). It's also a relatively weak poison (1L wound per dose).

parts.name: Moonseed Vines
parts.description: Moonseed Vines produce no effects when directly ingested or touched.

parts.name: Morning Glory Seeds
parts.description: Ingesting the seeds of the plant will cause one to hallucinate. Roll 1d10. A roll of 1 indicates a very bad trip and one takes 2H wounds of sanity shaking damage. A roll of 10 heals either anxiety-related damage or depressive damage completely.

parts.name: Morning Glory Nectar
parts.description: Typically safe to consume in small doses. Very large doses produce effects similar to Morning Glory Seeds.

parts.name: Motherwort Leaves
parts.description: Motherwort Leaves are a mild muscle relaxant. Ingesting these leaves prior to taking blunt-force damage reduce the damage taken by 2L wounds.

parts.name: Neem Bark
parts.description: Prevents malaria when chewed regularly.

parts.name: Neem Leaves
parts.description: Neem cures 1M wound of illness damage when chewed. It is inert to touch.

parts.name: Neem Seeds
parts.description: Neem Seeds are safe to ingest (in typical doses) and touch.

parts.name: Nettle Leaves
parts.description: Nettle Leaves are nutritious and safe to eat. Eating these leaves will immediately resolve most incapacitating effects.

parts.name: Nettle Needles
parts.description: These Needles must be boiled before eating. If they are not, the ingestor will take 1M wound per 30 minutes up to 6 heavy wounds.

parts.name: Gleaming Leaves of Niffler's Fancy
parts.description: The leaves, themselves, produce severe constipation if ingested (3H wounds of vital damage). They are safe to touch.

parts.name: Nightshade Peppers
parts.description: Nightshade peppers are poisonous. If directly ingested, they cause 2H wounds of poisonous damage per 2 hours (up to 14H wounds).

parts.name: Orchid Flowers
parts.description: Orchids Flowers are inert when ingested on their own.

parts.name: Oregano Leaves
parts.description: Oregano is great in pasta sauces. Ingesting this (within an hour prior to infection) can prevent very minor (1L wound) infections from damaging the consumer.

parts.name: Parsley Leaves
parts.description: Parsley is magically inert and fairly tasteless.

parts.name: Peony Flowers
parts.description: Peony flowers can slightly relieve pain (and can heal 2L wounds of psychological damage that came about as a result of pain).

parts.name: Peppermint Oil
parts.description: Peppermint Oil can heal 2L wound (per injury) of slash/gash, puncture/piercing, gouge, burn, disease/toxic/poison, or crushing/blunt force damage when ingested. It also produces good breath. In order to heal piercing damage, the stinger/piercing object must be removed first.

parts.name: Polypody Leaves
parts.description: Polypody can be chewed as an appetite suppressant.

parts.name: Milk of the Poppy
parts.description:
Treats pain. Highly addicting. It's basically opium. You really don't know what Milk of the Poppy is?
Can temporarily remove all psychological damage related to pain. However, the damage will recur if the underlying cause of pain is not fixed.

parts.name: Oozy Substance from the Pod of a Pritcher's Porritch
parts.description: This ooze is powerful and dangerous. If handled improperly (herbology rolls below 15) the ooze will cause serious boiling and swelling (3H wounds per hour).

parts.name: Puffapods
parts.description: Puffapods cause dizziness and disorientation to any in near proximity to them (set value 10). They are inert to ingest or touch.

parts.name: Pungous Onion Secretion
parts.description: Pungous Onion Secretion is a more potent variant of whole pungous onions. The slimy secretion will cause 1 medium wound per minute of toxic damage while in its vicinity.

parts.name: Whole Pungous Onions
parts.description: These onions, when removed from their stems, are still just as potent, causing 2L wounds per minute of toxic damage while in their vicinity. If ingested, the damage increases to 1 heavy wound per minute up to 12 total heavy wounds.

parts.name: Red Ti Plant Leaves
parts.description: Red Ti Plant is used in some rituals. It appears inert to ingestion and touch.

parts.name: Rose Bud
parts.description: Rose Buds are romantic in nature and create +2 to any rolls aimed at wooing another.

parts.name: Rose Thorn
parts.description: Rose Thorns required an herbology roll to successfully handle. Crit fails result in taking 1L wound of cut damage.

parts.name: Rose Petals
parts.description: Rose Petals are commonly used in love and beautifying potions. Though it is used in the Hate potion as well, it's believed this is because one cannot truly hate without some kind of love, infatuation, or other investment.

parts.name: Rosemary Needles
parts.description: Rosemary Needles require an herbology roll anytime they are handled. Crit fails cause 1L wound of piercing damage.

parts.name: Sage Root
parts.description: Sage root repels spirits when burned.

parts.name: Chamomile Flowers
parts.description: Chamomile helps one sleep (when added to potions). There is some belief that chewing Chamomile Flowers after apparating eases the nausea that often ensues.

parts.name: Leaves of Scurvy Grass
parts.description: Eating Leaves of Scurvy Grass can protect against up to disease damage over time. If you've already eaten these leaves prior to coming into contact with a disease, the disease will have no effect on you (so long as its set value is less than 7).

parts.name: Sea Lavander Flowers
parts.description: Sea Lavendar affects one's skin when added to a potion. Its other uses appear random in nature with little theory to support its use.

parts.name: Dried Seaweed
parts.description: Dried seaweed is mildly effective in healing vital damage (2L wound total per injury).

parts.name: Shrivelfigs
parts.description: Shrivelfigs can shrivel into a remarkably small size once pruned. However, they retain all of their original nutritional value, potency, and water content making them appear like raisins but bursting with juiciness much like grapes. They are tasty to eat but produce no immediate magical effects.

parts.name: Silver Leaves
parts.description: Silver Leaves are beautiful and valuable. They are inert to touch and ingest.

parts.name: Silverweed Flowers
parts.description: Silverweed can be eaten to heal 1M wound of vital damage.

parts.name: Snarfalump Jawbone
parts.description: Snarfalump Jawbones have no magical effects when touched.

parts.name: Snargaluff Tubers
parts.description: These tubers can be easily extracted from snargaluff pods.

parts.name: Snargaluff Tentacles
parts.description: Snargaluff Tentacles still have their thorns attached. So anyone handling them must make an herbology roll. Crit fails result in 1L wound of cut damage.

parts.name: Sneezewort Pollen
parts.description: Sneezewort pollen will make you sneeze when breathed in through the nose.

parts.name: Snowdrop Bulb
parts.description: Snowdrop Bulbs when directly ingested heal sanity-shaking damage (up to 3H Wounds per injury).

parts.name: Snowdrop Roots
parts.description: Snowdrop Roots, if chewed at the very moment a memory charm is cast, will completely block the memory charm (Set value 25)

parts.name: Snowdrop Flowers
parts.description: Potions using Galanthus Nivalis (AKA Snowdrop) appear to treat depressive symptoms

parts.name: Sopophorous Bean
parts.description: Sopophorous Bean, when ingested directly, produces mild memory loss for a temporary period of time.

parts.name: Beans
parts.description: These are beans
parts.raw_effects: You can eat em. They're nutritious.

parts.name: Soy Plant Leaves
parts.description: Soy is nutritious to eat, but produces no magical effects.

parts.name: Spattergroit Spore Sac
parts.description: Illegal to possess unless given special exceptions from the local government. These spore sacs lead to significant infections if improperly handled (1L wound per day up to 5 heavy wounds).

plants.name: Sphagnum Moss
plants.description: A plant known for its grounding properties and ties to earth energies, it helps attune the user’s senses to the subtleties of the natural world.

parts.name: Mass of Sphagnum Moss
parts.description: A glob of moss from the sphagnum moss plant.

parts.name: Spikey Bush Thorns
parts.description: Spikey Bush Thorns cause 3H wounds of vital damage if ingested. They are safe to touch (as long as you don't recklessly scrape the thorny part along your skin... obviously).

parts.name: Spleenwort Petals
parts.description: Spleenwort appears inert to consume in typical quantities. Larger quantities cause 1M wound of vital damage. It can exacerbate menstrual symptoms.

parts.name: Isolated Squill Bulbs
parts.description: Squill Bulbs are toxic to any creature size three or smaller. Size 1 creatures will take 1 heavy wound of toxic damage per bulb consumed. Size 2 creatures take 1 medium wound per bulb consumed. Size 3 creatures take 1 light wound per bulb consumed.

parts.name: St John's Flower
parts.description: St. John's Flower heals 1L wound of depressive damage by touching it. Ingesting it, whole, cures 1H wound of depressive damage.

parts.name: Star Grasses
parts.description: Star Grass, when chewed or rubbed on a wound, is a healing agent for explosive-type damage (2L wounds per wound). It also treats colic and jaundice.

parts.name: Sugar Shrub Oil
parts.description: Sugar Shrub Oil is a delicious sweet treat. It also heals 2L wounds of depressive damage (1x per week max).

parts.name: Thaumatagoria Petal
parts.description: Thaumatagoria, when ingested, improves one's awareness of spirits and the undead. +5 to perception checks (specifically for spotting spirits).

parts.name: Thistle Spine
parts.description: Thistle Spines, themselves, are inert when ingested. If you crit fail an herbology roll while handling them, you take 1L wound of puncture damage.

parts.name: Whole Thorn Apples
parts.description:
Touching thorn apples causes 1 medium wound of poisonous damage per minute, up to 3 heavy wounds. If the individual incurs more than 2 heavy wounds of damage, they will also experience intense hallucinations.
Ingesting an entire thorn apple incurs 7 heavy wounds of poisonous damage.

parts.name: Cut Thyme Herb
parts.description: Cut Thyme can be smashed and rubbed on a cut or abrasion to heal 2L wounds of damage. It can be ingested to heal up to 1H wound of blunt force damage.

parts.name: Toad Eating Plant Roots
parts.description: Toad Eating Plants have no known medicinal use when directly ingested or touched.

parts.name: Tormentil Extract
parts.description: Tormentil Extract can be rubbed into wounds of slash or gouge type to heal 1H wound per injury.

parts.name: Tormentil Roots
parts.description: Tormentil Roots are inert when touched or chewed.

parts.name: Turmeric Roots
parts.description: Turmeric when chewed may make the consumer hiccup. However, it appears not to have any other significant effects.

parts.name: Mass of Tyus Moss
parts.description: Tyus Moss can be gathered into masses.

parts.name: Bloomed Umbrella Flower Bulb
parts.description: Umbrella Flower appears inert to ingest or touch.

parts.name: Valerian Root
parts.description: Valerian Root is inert on its own.

parts.name: Valerian Sprigs
parts.description: Valerian Sprigs when ingested, help one sleep. It's not powerful enough on its own to put one to sleep, fully/forcibly. However, if the consumer is already tired, they will fall asleep and sleep more deeply. Can remove 1L of disease-related damage once per illness.

plants.name: Venomous Tentacula
plants.description: Venomous Tentacula attacks with a roll value of 17; Causing 1M wound of disease damage per hour until healed.

parts.name: Venomous Tentacula Venom
parts.description: Venomous Tentacula Venom is a highly deadly poison. It is very difficult to extract, but potent once properly extracted. When ingested directly, the ingester will almost immediately begin to vomit and will take 2 heavy wounds of poison damage per minute until dead.

parts.name: Vervain Fluid
parts.description: Vervain fluid will heal 1H wound of cut damage.

parts.name: Wartcaps
parts.description:
Wartcaps, when ingested directly, are highly potent and very dangerous. Limited accounts exist of individuals eating the caps directly. However, it's believed that the ingester will suffer 5 heavy wounds of vital damage due to their internal organs encrusting. The effect does wear off after a few days, but many die during this time due to suffering additional ailments and malnutrition.
Touching the wartcaps themselves (contact with the skin), will lead to taking 1 heavy wound of vital damage.

parts.name: Welwitschia Gasses
parts.description: If the welwitschia plant feels it is under attack, it will engage a natural protective response. They will stop secreting their oils (for 30 days) and begin secreting a mild poisonous gas instead. The poisonous gas will cause 2L wounds per day of damage over time if inhaled. It can be captured in a vial.

parts.name: Welwitschia Leaf Oils
parts.description:
These oils are exceptionally difficult to extract, requiring an herbology roll of 20. The user must boil copious amounts of crystallized potioning water and dip the leaf (while still attached to the plant) in the water. If the water is too hot, it will boil the leaf and all the oils will be lost. If it is too cool, the oils will not secrete and cannot be extracted.
To add to the difficulty, Welwitschia plants have a natural protective response such that if they detect that someone is attempting to harm them, they will stop secreting their oils and begin secreting a mild poisonous gas instead. Thus, critical failures (rolling a 1 on the die) while attempting to extract these oils will, instead result in the extractor being gassed (2L wounds per hour until healed).
The Welwitschia plant will not begin secreting the oils again for another 30 days once its defenses are activated.

parts.name: Wiggentree Bark
parts.description: Wiggentree Bark is inert on its own.

parts.name: Wiggentree Twigs
parts.description: Wiggentree Twigs are highly effective for Xylomancy (a form of divination) providing a +10 to this check when this is used.

parts.name: Witch's Ganglion Heart Bulb
parts.description: The Heart Bulb is the central, throbbing part of the witch's ganglion plant. It appears to be inert when touched or ingested.

parts.name: Witch's Ganglion Leaves
parts.description: These leaves are an essential ingredient in the potion of all potential. They appear to be useful for this potion and no other (known to date). Researchers have little idea why this ingredient is so important to the formula and have been unable to replace it or use it in any other potion.

plants.name: Witches Reed
plants.description: The most common plant on earth.

parts.name: Witches Wax
parts.description: Wax extracted from the Witches Reed plant.

parts.name: Wolfsbane Flowers
parts.description:
Wolfsbane Flowers on their own repel werewolves who cannot come within 15 feet of the flowers while in wereform. The flowers themselves are also highly toxic to touch. If the flowers are touched, the individual will experience a significantly painful rash (taking 1M wound of disease damage per new site of contact).
If ingested, the individual will experience 1 heavy wound of poisonous damage per dose.

parts.name: Aconite Flower Petals
parts.description: Aconite makes one sweat. Oh, it also helps reduce the symptoms of lycanthropy. Yeah… Those are the two things it does. Sweating. And, anti-lycanthropy. Muggles mistakenly believe it helps with pain.

parts.name: Leaves of Wormwood
parts.description: These leaves a mildly toxic when directly ingested (1L wound of poisonous damage immediately). Organisms size 2 or smaller take an additional 1M wound of poisonous damage per hour up to 5 hours.

parts.name: Wormwood Oil
parts.description: Wormwood Oil, when directly ingested, can help fight some basic diseases (up to 2L wounds per dose; max 5 doses per illness must be spaced out by at least one hour). Taking more than this will cause a poisonous effect (1M wound per minute for 15 minutes then 1L wound per hour for 2 days).

parts.name: Yarrow Flowers
parts.description: Yarrow Flowers are inert to touch or ingest.

potions.name: Accip's Subterfuge Draught
potions.raw_effect: This potion helps to conceal one's subterfuge. If they are in conversation and the other individual attempts a social skills check to check whether the drinker has been lying, this potion guards against that check up to a value of 20.

potions.name: Aging Potion
potions.raw_effect: Each dose of the aging potion makes the drinker 1 year older. This effect lasts 2 hours plus 1 hour per 1 over the difficulty.

potions.name: Alihotsy Draught
potions.raw_effect: Alihotsy Draught is a potion made from the Alihotsy plant. Drinking it or inhaling its fumes induces hysterical laughter.

potions.name: Amortentia
potions.raw_effect: A powerful love potion which causes the drinker to fall hopelessly in love with anyone whose hair was dissolved in the potion before they drank it. The love produced is not true love, will always be empty, and almost never brings happiness. Lasts until an antidote is administered.

potions.name: Angel's Trumpet Draught
potions.raw_effect: The Angel's Trumpet Draught will help one be more attuned to their surroundings. Any trace detection checks are at +3 and any perception checks are at +5. Six doses per bottle.

potions.name: Anti-Paralysis Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Anti-Paralysis Potion is a Healing potion, which acts as an antidote to paralysis, including reversing the effects of the Full Body-Bind Curse.

potions.name: Antidote to Common Poisons
potions.raw_effect: The Antidote to Common Poisons (or Antidote for Common Poisons) is a potion and antidote which counteracts ordinary poisons, such as creature bites and stings.

potions.name: Antidote to Uncommon Poisons
potions.raw_effect: Despite its name, the Antidote to Uncommon Poisons also heals most diseases and toxins in addition to poisons 2H wounds per dose max 1 dose per day; may use repeatedly).

potions.name: Antidote to Veritaserum
potions.raw_effect: Reverses the effects of veritaserum.

potions.name: Babbling Beverage
potions.raw_effect: Causes the drinker to babble incoherently for 1 hour plus 5 minutes per 1 over the difficulty. Spells must be cast non-verbally.

potions.name: Bacopa Potion
potions.raw_effect: A powerful anxiolytic. This will stave off (guard against prophylactically) anxiety-causing damage up to 3H wounds and will heal anxiety-causing damage up to 6H wounds. Any DoT anxiety-producing effects are nullified. If the drinker takes this potion long term, their natural alarm system (fight/flight/freeze) will dampen. They will become unperturbed by seemingly alarming events. They will still fight and defend themselves if need be, but their overall reaction to worrisome events will be significantly muted. It takes two months for the full effects of long term use to fully wear off.

potions.name: Baruffio's Brain Elixer
potions.raw_effect: A potion that increases one's intelligence by 2 until the potion wears off (approximately five minutes or two combat rounds).

potions.name: Beautification Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Beautification Potion makes the drinker considerably more attractive for about 6 hours.

potions.name: Blood Replenishing Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Blood-Replenishing Potion heals wounds of the eruptive, gouge, and gash/slash type by replenishing lost blood 2L wounds once per injury.

potions.name: Bloodroot Poison
potions.raw_effect: The Bloodroot Poison is a potion made from bloodroot. It is poisonous, although its precise effects are unknown.

potions.name: Boil-aid
potions.raw_effect: Boil-aid (also known as simply Boil Cure) is a potion which removes boils.

potions.name: Bruise-Removal Paste
potions.raw_effect: This is a very weak treatment for blunt-force damage (1L per hour for ten hours).

potions.name: Bulgeye Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Bulgeye Potion is a potion that affects one's eyes, causing them to swell. It is emerald-green in colour.

potions.name: Bundimun Pomade
potions.raw_effect: A potion used to style one's hair. Can improve persuasion checks in situations where the target may be impressed with particularly nice hair.

potions.name: Burn-Healing Paste
potions.raw_effect: The Burn-healing paste is a healing potion in the form of a thick orange paste, which is used to heal burns completely.

potions.name: Burning Bitterroot Balm
potions.raw_effect: Burning bitterroot balm is a soothing balsam made from the bitter root plant. It is soothing to one's gastrointestinal system and reverses the fire-breathing spell.

potions.name: Calming Draught
potions.raw_effect: A Calming Draught is a potion that is used to calm a person down after they have had suffered a shock, trauma, or emotional outburst. Mostly treats anxiety (2H wounds), but can be somewhat helpful for sanity-shaking damage (1M wound).

potions.name: Caxambu Style Borborygmus Potion
potions.raw_effect: A potion which causes one's stomach to growl. Few think it is worth bothering to make given the danger and expense associated with acquiring pogrebin scalp.

potions.name: Cerci's Lion Enchantment
potions.raw_effect: This potion, when consumed, will turn the drinker into a lion. The effect will last approximately one hour and cannot be repeated for six months.

potions.name: Cerci's Pig Enchantment
potions.raw_effect: This potion, when consumed, will turn the drinker into a pig. The enchantment will last for about 10 days and cannot be repeated for one year.

potions.name: Cerci's Wolf Enchantment
potions.raw_effect: This potion will turn the drinker into a wolf temporarily (approximately six hours). The effect cannot be repeated for one month.

potions.name: Chelidonium Miniscula
potions.raw_effect: Reduces pain and eases physical suffering (which can be used to prevent sanity-shaking damage from pain; will not reverse sanity-shaking damage).

potions.name: Concentration Capsules
potions.raw_effect: Concentration Capsules are pills that enhance one's concentration skills (+5 to final grades; +1 to perception checks).

potions.name: Confusing Concoction
potions.raw_effect: The Confusing Concoction or Befuddlement Draught causes confusion. In non-combat situations, this plays out as them having little understanding of what is going on around them and little understanding of what they are doing. In combat situations, the drinker loses two turns in battle.

potions.name: Cornflower Meal
potions.raw_effect: Ground cornflower for use in potions.

potions.name: Cough Potion
potions.raw_effect: Cough potion is a general purpose disease/poison/toxic potion healing 1H wound once per day for up to three uses (3H wounds max).

potions.name: Crystalized Water
potions.raw_effect: A concoction used in potions.

potions.name: Cupid Crystals
potions.raw_effect: Cupid Crystals are a brand of Love Potion from the WonderWitch line by Weasleys' Wizard Wheezes.

potions.name: Daisyroot Draught
potions.raw_effect: An alcoholic beverage made from daisies.

potions.name: Death Potion
potions.raw_effect: The potion has several functions. First, extracted memories can be shown visible for all to see. Second, while one's memories are visible, they are usually transfixed by them. Finally, any who come into contact with the potion will die. No known supply of the drug is known to be in existence and the formula may be lost to history.

potions.name: Death-cap Draught
potions.raw_effect: The Death-cap Draught is a poison made of Death-Caps. It causes 5 heavy wounds per gulp.

potions.name: Decrustrative Skin Potion
potions.raw_effect: This potion will reverse the effects of any skin encrustations that have occurred regardless of their source (including wartcap exposure).

potions.name: Deflating Draught
potions.raw_effect: The Deflating Draught is a potion which causes anything swollen by magical means to shrink back to normal size. This is the opposite to a Swelling Solution and acts as an antidote to it.

potions.name: Delirium Potion
potions.raw_effect: The one who ingests this potion will become incapacitated through delirium for three days.

potions.name: Developing Solution
potions.raw_effect: Developing solution was a potion that - when used in the developing of photographs - allowed the pictures to move. It will apparently even work with Muggle film, as the Muggle-born wizard Colin Creevey learned during his first year, much to his delight.

potions.name: Dizziness Draught
potions.raw_effect: Dizziness Draught is a potion that provokes a sensation of lightheadedness and loss of balance on the drinker. In larger doses, it may be temporarily incapacitating.

potions.name: Dogbane Potion
potions.raw_effect: Poisons dogs.

potions.name: Dogbreath Potion
potions.raw_effect: Gives the drinker fiery breath.

potions.name: Doxycide
potions.raw_effect: Doxycide is a black-coloured foul smelling solution used to spray Doxies. It paralyses them for a period of time, allowing the user to safely remove the Doxies without fear of being bitten.

potions.name: Dr. Ubbly's Oblivious Unction
potions.raw_effect: Heals sanity shaking damage. A single dose can heal 3 heavy wounds. However, if one reaches 7 heavy wounds of sanity shaking damage, they are permanently unhealable.

potions.name: Dragon Dung Fertiliser
potions.raw_effect: Dragon dung fertilizer helps advanced magical plants (those with a skill level of more than 5) to grow. Many can only survive if given dragon dung fertilizer. One bag is enough to supply 10 plants through a full growing season.

potions.name: Dragon Poison
potions.raw_effect: A highly potent poison

potions.name: Dragon Tonic
potions.raw_effect: Cures sick dragons

potions.name: Dragon-Gash Healing Paste
potions.raw_effect: Gashed by a dragon? No problem! Just bring one of the dragon's claws to your nearest trusted potioneer and they can whip up this simple concoction in no time! Applying the paste to the wound will completely health wounds of the slash/gash type (even if it wasn't caused by a dragon). The wound completely within just three hours!

potions.name: Draught of Living Death
potions.raw_effect: The Draught of Living Death is an extremely powerful sleeping draught, sending the drinker into a deathlike slumber. Its effects are similar to suspended animation. Waking one from this sleep is exceptionally difficult.

potions.name: Draught of Peace
potions.raw_effect: This potion stabilizes one's sanity and decreases anxiety.

potions.name: Drowsiness Draught
potions.raw_effect: The Drowsiness Draught is a potion whose fumes can be used to induce drowsiness, tiredness and fatigue in the drinker. The potion produces a light pinkish-purple steam.

potions.name: Egyptian Cure for Jaundice
potions.raw_effect: This Ancient Egyptian potion can be easily developed to cure jaundice.

potions.name: Elixer of Euphoria
potions.raw_effect: The Elixir to Induce Euphoria is a sunshine-yellow potion that induces a sense of inexplicable, irrational happiness upon the drinker. This will counteract depressive-based DoT, but will not reverse damage already done.

potions.name: Emerald Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Emerald Potion is often called the Drink of Despair (though this is a misnomer, because its effects are anxiety inducing). It produces a fearful delirium and extreme thirst (1H wound and 1M wound per drink). Touching this potion with your hands produces a serious burn to the hand (2 heavy wounds). No spells work on this potion. It also cannot be removed in any way other than by drinking it. There is no other way to affect its nature in any way. It can only be drunk. As such, this potion is designed to produce something incredibly important.

potions.name: Erumpent Potion
potions.description: A highly explosive potion that can produce a massive explosion (enough to destroy a building). Anyone in the immediate vicinity is killed immediately. It is highly volitile.
potions.raw_effect: A highly explosive potion that can produce a massive explosion (enough to destroy a building). Anyone in the immediate vicinity is killed immediately. It is highly volitile.

potions.name: Everklena
potions.raw_effect: A purported cleaning potion that actually creates more mould and grime (delayed reaction).

potions.name: Exploding Potion
potions.raw_effect: An explosive potion that can be thrown at enemies to cause damage. 1M wound if hit directly. 2L if nearby. 1L if somewhat close. Roll finesse to throw. If your finesse roll is greater than the opponent's finesse roll, you get a direct hit. If you fail by 2 or less, you get a nearby hit. If you fail by four or more, you get a somewhat close roll. Otherwise, you just failed. These come in a pack of 20.

potions.name: Exstimulo Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Exstimulo Potion is a potion that produces an exceptional enhancement of magical power for a single spell cast by the drinker (+5 to power for the next cast only). There is only enough for one dose in the bottle.

potions.name: Fatiguing Infusion
potions.raw_effect: The Fatiguing Fusion is a potion used to induce fatigue. Inhaling the potion's fumes is enough to start feeling the effects.

potions.name: Felix Felicis
potions.raw_effect: Felix Felicis, also called "Liquid Luck", is a magical potion that makes the drinker lucky for a period of time, during which everything they attempt will be successful. It turns an ordinary day into an extraordinary one. It is meant to be used sparingly, however, as it causes giddiness, recklessness, and dangerous overconfidence if taken in excess. Felix is highly toxic in large quantities and is also a banned substance in all organised competitions. It is very difficult to make and if made incorrectly could have long-last magical effects with no known cure. As an added wrinkle, when made incorrectly, it often looks identical to the correct concoction. Those under the effects of Felix Felicis may re-roll any die they cast (taking the better of the two rolls) until the effects of the potion wear off. One dose per vial.

potions.name: Fergus Fungal Budge Treatment
potions.raw_effect:
Fergus Fungal Budge is an ointment useful for treating abrasions and cuts (2H wounds per hour and can be used repeatedly on wounds of these types). 

Typically, one would soak a medical dressing in the product. This will cause some damage to house elves (2L wounds per hour).

potions.name: Fire Protection Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Fire Protection Potion, also known as the Ice Potion, is a potion used to move through flames unscathed. When drunk, it induces a sensation of ice and frigidity in the drinker, and provides protection from most (but not all) magical fires.

potions.name: Fire Whiskey
potions.raw_effect: Firewhisky is an alcoholic drink that is consumed by wizards and witches. Those under the age of seventeen are not allowed to buy or drink it. It is known to cause a burning sensation when drunk and for filling one's body with courage.

potions.name: Fire-breathing Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Fire-Breathing Potion is a dangerous potion that can make a person breathe fire (which they can then use to cause 1H of damage per drink).

potions.name: Flesh Eating Slug Repellent
potions.raw_effect: Flesh-Eating Slug Repellent was a pesticide used to get rid of Flesh-Eating Slugs. It can be purchased in Knockturn Alley, and its slogan was "with slugs out, mandrakes sprout."

potions.name: Forgetfulness Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Forgetfulness Potion is a potion which causes an unknown degree of memory loss in the drinker.

potions.name: Frog Parts Mixture
potions.raw_effect: A mixture used in some potions

potions.name: Fungiface Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Fungiface Potion is a potion used to cause the drinker's face to break out in fungi.

potions.name: Garrotting Gas
potions.raw_effect: Garrotting Gas is emited by its associated potion (which has no effect if drunk). Anyone exposed to the gas will suffer 1M wound of Vital damage in combat as they find themselves choking and as a result. Outside of combat, they will suffer 1M wound per minute, but may simply leave the area to prevent further damage.

potions.name: Girding Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Girding Potion is a potion that gives the consumer prolonged endurance. The maximum dosage is two vials. It has a rather foul odour. This can help to protect against extreme fatigue and is a favorite of students cramming for their NEWTs. Taking this continuously for more than 3 days will cause ever increasing sanity damage... I mean really, kid... Just go to bed! You need sleep!

potions.name: Gregory's Unctuous Unction
potions.raw_effect:
Gregory's Unctuous Unction is a potion that was invented by Gregory the Smarmy. It causes the drinker to believe that whomever gave the potion to him or her is their best friend (effects wear off after 12 hours).

1 dose per brew

potions.name: Hair-Raising Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Hair-Raising Potion is a potion that causes the drinker's hair to stand on end. One of the known ingredients is rat tails.

potions.name: Hate Potion
potions.raw_effect: When the drinker consumes this potion, they will experience a considerable hatred toward the person who gave them the potion. This will be temporary (usually lasting about 3 days).

potions.name: Heartbreak Teardrops
potions.raw_effect: Heartbreak Teardrops are sold in Weasley's Wizard Wheezes. No one but Fred and George know the formula. The potion is known to decrease one's heartbreak but can also decrease one's embarrassment. It's is believed to work because drinking the tears of other heartbroken souls reminds one that they are not alone in their pain.

ingredients.name: Tears of one who is heartbroken

potions.name: Herbicide
potions.raw_effect: Herbicide Potion damages plants. It has the ability to kill any plant under level 3. It will harm or weaken any plant under level 6. Higher level plants remain uneffected. Fungi are unaffected.

potions.name: Hiccuping Solution
potions.raw_effect: A potion that induces incessant hiccuping for 1 hour.

potions.name: Invigoration Draught
potions.raw_effect: Invigoration draught improves one's power by 1 for 15 minutes (or 9 combat rounds).

potions.name: Invisibility Potion
potions.raw_effect:
The Invisibility Potion makes the drinker invisible for a short time. The potion will wear off at some random time (recommended that the headmaster roll to determine the wearing off time with higher quality concoctions lasting longer).

3 Doses per Brew

potions.name: Jawbind Potion
potions.raw_effect: A potion that induces lockjaw

potions.name: Kissing Concoction
potions.raw_effect: A weaker and cheaper alternative to the love potion.

potions.name: Laxative Potion
potions.raw_effect: A potion to induce diarrhea.

potions.name: Leech Juice
potions.raw_effect: This is a juice made from leeches that is sometimes used in other potions as the primary fluid.

potions.name: Liquid Mirth Formula 017
potions.raw_effect: This is an antidepressive potion that is more effective (2H wounds per drink) than chewing Alihotsy leaves or ingesting raw St.John's-wort. There are 3 doses per vial.

potions.name: Liquid Mirth Formula 215
potions.raw_effect: This is a powerful antidepressive potion that will remove all depression.

potions.name: Madame Glossy's Silver Polish
potions.raw_effect: Madame Glossy's Silver Polish is a magical cleaning solution often used by squibs.

potions.name: Magi-me-more
potions.raw_effect: Magi-Me-More pills help the consumer with concentration. However, there are considerable transfigurating side-effects.

potions.name: Mandrake Draught
potions.raw_effect: Mandrake Restorative Draught is a healing potion which restores one who has been transfigured, cursed or petrified back to their original state. The restoration process is very slow, with the full effects not resolving for approximately 1 month. 5 doses per bottle.

potions.name: Mandrake Stew
potions.raw_effect: This is a stew made from havested mandrakes that can be used in other potions. No one is sure what benefits or risks are associated with the raw stew. Few dare to try it. However, it's said to be quite delicious.

potions.name: Manegro Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Manegro Potion is a potion used to cause the hair on the drinker's head to grow out rapidly.

potions.name: Maximum Turbo Farts Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Maximum Turbo Farts Potion was a potion that causes the drinker to rapidly develop flatulence.

potions.name: Mayan Guidance of the Dead
potions.raw_effect: Ingesting this potion allows the faintest interaction with the spirit of one who has recently passed on. In so doing, the spirit will have the ability to communicate their knowledge and emotions, but only briefly (usually about 90 seconds). Very very rarely (rolls of 60 or higher) have been shown to either sustain the connection longer or to reconnect with the spirit a second time.

potions.name: Memory Potion
potions.raw_effect: A Memory Potion is a potion which improves the drinker's memory, boosting their mental cognition. (History checks by +5).

potions.name: Moonseed Poison
potions.raw_effect: The Moonseed Poison is a weak poison (2L per hour for three hours).

potions.name: Mrs. Scower's All-purpose Magic Mess Remover
Muffling Draught
potions.raw_effect: The Muffling Draught is a potion used to affect the target's ability to speak. Inhaling its fumes are enough to start feeling its effects. It is exceptionally expensive owing to the fact that no wizard to date has found a way to make the potion without the use of Nundu Claw.

potions.name: Murtlap Essence
potions.raw_effect: Murtlap Essence is a solution of strained and pickled tentacles of Murtlaps. Heals abrasions and burns 3H wounds once per injury.

potions.name: Noxious Potion
potions.raw_effect:
This potion when dumped onto the ground will release a noxious gas into the air that will force others to flee the area for at least one hour. Those with an alternative breathing source or a counteracting potion may be able to withstand this potion's effects.

10 Doses per Brew

potions.name: Oculus Potion
potions.raw_effect: Restores normal functioning to one's eyesight. It is incredibly expensive because of the need to use Ground Unicorn Horn which can only be harvested from Unicorns that died of natural causes. This is a cure for blindness.

potions.name: Olmec Conjuration Potion
potions.raw_effect: This Mayan-era potion utilizes Agarin as its primary ingredient. The result of ingesting the potion is the conjuration of four sprite-like creatures (per dose) that each cause 2L wound of cutting damage per attack. Each has a heavy wound capacity of 1. These are conjured within one turn and can begin attacking immediately. They may be reconjured once all initial sprites have died.

potions.name: Penelope's Antinox Potion
potions.raw_effect: A powerful potion which offers magical protection against venoms (up to 2H wounds) and poison gasses (protection value = 25; one hour of protection). Offers magical resistance of 10 for one hour.

potions.name: Penelope's Prolonging Potion
potions.raw_effect: Extends the life of a living creature that is near death (and can bring a plant back from the brink of death).

potions.name: Pepperup Potion
potions.raw_effect: A potion which provides some healing for disease/toxic/poison damage (2L per drink). It also warms up the recipient. It has the side-effect of causing steam to gently emit from the drinker's ears for hours afterward.

potions.name: Peruvian Instant Darkness Powder
potions.raw_effect: When this powder is thrown into the air, it blankets the entire area in a magical darkness that cannot be penetrated by magical or muggle light.

potions.name: PolyJuice Potion
potions.raw_effect: Polyjuice Potion is a potion that allows the drinker to assume the form of someone else. It is a highly complicated, challenging and time-consuming potion that even adult witches and wizards struggle to brew correctly. The potion is incredibly advanced and has two separate parts that contain steps for each part, when brewing is complete the potion has to stew for a month before usage. Its use is strictly prohibited by the ministry.

ingredients.name: A bit of the person one wants to turn into

potions.name: Pompion Potion
potions.raw_effect: Pompion Potion is a potion that when drunk, temporarily turns the drinker's head into a pumpkin.

potions.name: Potion for Dreamless Sleep
potions.raw_effect: Potion for Dreamless Sleep induces drowsiness in small doses and a deep restful sleep with full doses.

potions.name: Potion Number 07
potions.raw_effect: Potion № 07 is a deadly poison. It produces 2H wounds per hour for three days.

potions.name: Potion of All Potential
potions.raw_effect: This potion ensures that the next cast will succeed as if the drinker has rolled a 10. It appears to sparkle slightly in its phial.

potions.name: Rat Spleen Mixture
potions.raw_effect: A mixture of rat spleen used in potions

potions.name: Rat tonic
potions.raw_effect: A Rat tonic is a healing potion for rats (1L wound per dose. Max once per day). It is most often used to treat ageing rats. Rats will grow if they drink too much.

potions.name: Raya's Enhanced Welwitschia Potion
potions.description:
Invented by Raphael Raya. One batch of ingredients makes nine doses.

Raya does not know the ingredients.
potions.raw_effect: Cures one disease one time completely. But, can only be used once per lifetime. That disease will never reinfect the target.

potions.name: Raya's Standard Welwitschia Potion
potions.description: Invented by Raphael Raya. One batch of ingredients makes three doses.
potions.raw_effect: Cures one disease one time completely. But, can only be used once per lifetime. That disease will never reinfect the target.

potions.name: Raya's Welwitschia Potion
potions.description: One batch of ingredients makes six doses. This potion is technically slightly harder to cast, but mostly its difficulty is mitigated by requiring a more purified version of welwitchia extract.
potions.raw_effect: Extends the life of a living creature that is near death (and can bring a plant back from the brink of death).

potions.name: Regeneration Potion
potions.raw_effect: This potion is an "old piece of dark magic" that restores a wizard whose body has become maimed and disfigured to their true and whole bodily form.

ingredients.name: Flesh of a servent willingly given

potions.name: Regerminating Potion
potions.raw_effect: Revives dead flitterblooms and aids plants in germinating. If you're trying to plant a plant with seeds (or fungi with spores) you are required to make an herbology check. Using this potion adds 2 to that herbology check. There are enough doses in this for 10 uses.

potions.name: Replenishing Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Replenishing Potion is a potion used to replenish empty potions. The more difficult the potion is to make, the less likely this potion is to be effective (headmaster's discretion). There are 4 doses in each vial. Drinking this potion does nothing.

potions.name: Restoration Potion
potions.raw_effect: Removes spell effects when drank by a beast or when poured on an object or animal.

potions.name: Revival Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Revive Potion is a potion used to awaken an unconscious person.

potions.name: Rudimentary Body Potion
potions.raw_effect: This potion is used to prepare a rudimentary body for a non-corporeal wizard from an intact horcrux.

potions.name: Scintillation Solution
potions.raw_effect: A potion that makes the drinker sparkle slightly.

potions.name: Shrinking Solution
potions.raw_effect: Shrinking Solution, also known as a Shrinking Potion, is a potion that causes the drinker to shrink to a smaller form. It is bright green when brewed correctly and, if prepared incorrectly, it can be poisonous. Roll 1d10 when you drink the first dose to determine if the bottle is poisonous. If the roll is less than 3, the batch is poison and the drinker takes a medium wound per drink. They are also incapacitated for the rest of that day.

potions.name: Shrivelfig Paste
potions.raw_effect: Shrivelfig paste is the most commonly used paste in potioncrafting. While the shrivelfig does not specifically have any magical effect, the fact that the shrivelfig paste shrivels means that the overall volume of most potions and pastes is smaller. The invention of shrivelfig paste, was a revolution in potionmaking because it allowed for potioneers to create larger batches that could be more easily transported to markets throughout the world. The shrivelfig itself is commonly available and its powder produces an easy-to-work-with paste. It provides clear utility to commercial and hobbyist potionmakers.

potions.name: Skele-gro
potions.raw_effect: Skele-Gro mends broken bones. Multiple doses is required to regrow bones.

potions.name: Skin Restoration Potion
potions.raw_effect:
This simple potion will cure boils, blemishes, skin tags, and jinxes that have affected the skin. Essential it returns the skin to its normal state. 

Does not work on acne.

potions.name: Sleeping Draught
potions.raw_effect: The Sleeping Draught is a potion that causes the drinker to fall almost instantaneously into a deep but temporary sleep. A standard Sleeping Draught differs from the Draught of Living Death, which is much more powerful, long-lasting, and difficult to brew.

potions.name: Snuffling Potion
potions.raw_effect: This potion enhances one's sense of smell and helps them better discern the source of certain spells.

potions.name: Standard Ingredient
potions.raw_effect: A concoction used in potions.

potions.name: Star Grass Salve
potions.raw_effect: Treats damage of the explosive type (1M wound per drink) with 10 doses per bottle.

potions.name: Strength Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Strength Potion is a potion which is orange in colour and gives the drinker increased strength (thereby improving their power by 1 until the potion wears off).

potions.name: Strengthening Solution
potions.raw_effect: This potion grants the drinker considerable physical strength (+5) for the next hour.

potions.name: Swelling Solution
potions.raw_effect: The Swelling Solution is a potion which causes whatever it touches to swell in size.

potions.name: Ten-second Pimple Vanisher
potions.raw_effect: The Ten-Second Pimple Vanisher removes pimples.

potions.name: Thick Golden Potion
potions.raw_effect: Contains curses and slows their effects. Whatever DoT rate the curse would typically take is significantly decreased (headmaster's discretion). When combined with certain spells (it's possible that only Snape knew those spells), curses can be cordoned off to certain areas of the body.

potions.name: Tincture of Bloodroot
potions.description: Combine with Vinegar to make Tincture of Bloodroot which can be used to relieve a cough.

potions.name: Tincture of Thyme
potions.raw_effect: Thyme dissolved in alcohol to be used in other potions.

potions.name: Tincture of Tormentil
potions.raw_effect: Tormentil dissolved in alcohol has the ability to stop bleeding preventing some DoTs from progressing.

potions.name: Tolipan's Blemish Blitzer
potions.raw_effect: Tolipan Blemish Blitzer is a potion that treats acne.

potions.name: Tonic for Trace Detection
potions.raw_effect: The Tonic for Trace Detection is a potion that can be used to increase an individual's mental perception of Trace magic in the world. If a trace has been placed on an area, this potion enhances the user's ability to perceive it (+5 to perception to the next check solely for a trace).

potions.name: Veritaserum
potions.raw_effect: Veritaserum is a truth potion. The drinker will be forced to answer direct questions honestly (to the best of their knowledge).

potions.name: Vitamix Potion
potions.raw_effect: Vitamix Potion is a potion that gives the drinker 'a burst of energy', greatly sharpening one's reflexes (+5 to finesse). It's affects are very short lived. This must be brewed in a copper cauldron.

potions.name: Volubilis Potion
potions.raw_effect: Volubilis Potion is a potion that alters the drinker's voice. It will also restore their voice if they have lost it, and thus will end the effects of the Silencing Charm.

potions.name: Weakness Potion
potions.raw_effect: Weakness Potion is a potion whose fumes induce weakness, both physically (-5 to power checks) and mentally (-5 to intelligence checks). Weakness Potion fumes can also induce weakness, both physically (-3 power) and mentally (-3 intelligence).

potions.name: Weedosoros
potions.raw_effect: This is a highly potent poison that causes 1 heavy wound per day of vital damage for 7 days.

potions.name: Wideye Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Wideye Potion is also referred to as the Awakening Potion. The potion keeps the drinker awake until the potion wears off (two days). The potion serves as an antidote to the Draught of Living Death.

potions.name: Wiggenweld Potion
potions.raw_effect:
Cures 1H wound of cut/scratch, abrasion/scrape, slash/gash, puncture/piercing, gouge, burn, crushing/blunt force, eruptive/explosive or vital damage. In order to successfully heal the puncture/piercing wound, the stinger or puncturing object must first be removed.

Can only be used once per injury.

potions.name: Wit-sharpening Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Wit-Sharpening Potion is a potion which allows the drinker to think more clearly. Due to this, it acts an antidote to the Confundus Charm. If the drinker is not under any the effects of any spell or potion, it acts to increase perception (+5) and Intelligence (+5 for the next spell cast).

potions.name: Wolfsbane Potion
potions.raw_effect: The Wolfsbane Potion treats (but does not cure) lycanthropy. The potion is rather dangerous, and the drinker must roll a straight d10 every time they drink the potion. If the roll is less than 5, the entire bottle is poisonous and the drinker takes 1H wound per drink). The drinker must drink the potion once per day in the week leading up to the full moon. They must drink the potion successfully at least once on each of these days or the potion is rendered useless.

potions.name: Wound-cleaning Potion
potions.description:
Heals one medium wound per injury of any of the following types

1. Cuts/Scratches
2. Abrasions/Scrapes
3. Slash/Gash
4. Puncture/Piercing (if the piercing item like a stinger has been removed)
5. Gouges
6. Burns
7. Eruptive/Explosive
potions.raw_effect: The Wound-Cleaning Potion (or Wound Cleaning Potion) is a healing potion that is used to sterilise wounds as a powerful antiseptic. It smokes and stings when applied.

preparations.name: Agapanthus Solution
preparations.description: Soak Agapanthus Flowerheads in water to create the resulting solution.

preparations.name: Agarin
preparations.description: Through a combination of boiling, colling, and bathing this flesh, one can extract Agarin from the flesh of the fly amanita. This is safer to consume (only causing 1L wound per unit ingested). The resulting Agarin is useful in potions and historical rituals.

preparations.name: Armadillo Bile Solution
preparations.raw_effect: This is a corrosive substance that appears to be effective in dissolving some magical objects.

preparations.name: Baked Apple Core
preparations.description: The core of an apple is removed then baked.

preparations.name: Baked Spattergroit Spore Sacs
preparations.description: Baking these spore sacs is an effective way of rendering them inert. This is an illegal, highly valuable delicacy that is usually only served in underground establishments. It's said to be quite delicious.

preparations.name: Barley Cereal
preparations.description: A hearty meal
preparations.effect_in_potions: May greatly enhance the buffing qualities of any potion intending to provide health or protective benefits to the drinker.

preparations.name: Billywig Sting Slime
preparations.description: Extracted from freshly harvested stings. This creates a dried sting as a byproduct as well.

preparations.name: Bitterroot Syrup
preparations.description: Bitterroot Syrup is often added to teas or potions to produce a voice-quality producing effect. Essentially performers and singers drink this to ensure strong, accurate voice.

preparations.name: Bloodroot Powder
preparations.description: Powder Bloodroots to create a powder. This powder can be burned. The fumes produce romantic effects.

preparations.name: Boomberry Juice
preparations.description: Boomberry juice is an explosive substance that must be handled with care (Potions roll of 10 or higher). Failing this roll will result in taking eruptive-type damage of 1H wound. Critical failures result in permanent magical scarring even after the wound has healed.
required_proficiencies: Preparation and Proper Handling of a Boomberry

preparations.name: Bulbadox Essence
preparations.raw_effect: Bulbadox powder is a joke product that caused the victim's skin to break out in boils.

preparations.name: Bulbadox Juice
preparations.description:
Bulbadox Juice can be macerated from bulbadox leaves. This juice strips away all of the dangerous skin-affecting components of the leaf. As such, using bulbadox juice in potions can create even more potent arithmetic and trace detection effects.
Drinking straight bulbadox juice provides a +2 bonus to one's next attempt to potioncraft or spellcraft.

preparations.name: Bulbadox Powder
preparations.description: Bulbadox Powder is more potent than bulbadox leaves themselves. Not only will touching this powder lead the toucher's skin to break out in boils, these boils will be painful (causing 1L wound of depressive damage per hour), and will cause 1M wound of vital damage every hour for three days.

preparations.name: Bulrush Sugar
preparations.description: Drying Bulrush Syrup will produce Bulrush Sugar which is sweet and tasty!

preparations.name: Bulrush Syrup
preparations.description: Adding Bulrush Roots to water can create a sweet and tasty syrup.

preparations.name: Bundimum Solution
preparations.description: The ooze needs to be diluted.

preparations.name: Cornflower Meal
preparations.description: Ground cornflower for use in potions.
preparations.raw_effect: Ground cornflower for use in potions.
preparations.effect_in_potions: Ground cornflower for use in potions.

preparations.name: Crushed Flint
preparations.description: Crushed form of flint

preparations.name: Crystalized Water
preparations.description: A base for many complex potions
preparations.effect_in_potions: Serves as a base for more complex potions

preparations.name: Diluted Bloodroot
preparations.description: Diluted bloodroot is mostly inert. In a potion, it can help with digestion.

preparations.name: Diluted Bundimum Secretion
preparations.description: Bundimum secretion is first extracted in its pure form then is diluted to be more useful in potions.

preparations.name: Dragon Blood Resin
preparations.description: Creation of a resin from Dragon's blood
preparations.effect_in_potions: Varied, mostly used in alchemical elixirs.

preparations.name: Dried Beans
preparations.description: Dried out beans.

preparations.name: Dried Thyme
preparations.description: Safe to ingest. No know effects at even large doses. Consumer may feel slightly queasy.

preparations.name: Erumpent Horn Chalk
preparations.description: Can only be extracted safely after the fluid has been removed from the horn first. Erumpent Horn Chalk appears to be a stabilizing agent for potion ingredients that may otherwise be explosive. By increasing the amount of chalk, the volitility of the potion will be decreased. By decreasing or removing it, the potion will become more explosive.

preparations.name: Erumpent Horn Fluid
preparations.description: It requires a Magical Creatures roll of 15 to extract this fluid—failure to do so results in an explosion which can instantly cause between 6 and 8 heavy wounds.

preparations.name: Essence of Dittany
preparations.description: Essence of Dittany is a concentrated broad (any wound type) healing agent (1M wound per injury). It is particularly effective for splinching specific damage (curing up to 3H wounds of splinching damage).

preparations.name: Fermented Apple Cores
preparations.description: Fermented Apple Cores are usually used to make a hooch particularly popular among the ne're-do-wells of Britain
preparations.raw_effect: Fermented Apple Cores are usually used to make a hooch particularly popular among the ne're-do-wells of Britain
preparations.effect_in_potions: Fermented Apple Cores are usually used to make a hooch particularly popular among the ne're-do-wells of Britain

preparations.name: Frog Parts Mixture
preparations.description: This is a complex mixture of multiple frog parts.

preparations.name: Ground Coffee Bean
preparations.description: Ground Coffee Bean smells lovely. If directly ingested, it can increase alertness and anxiety.

preparations.name: Horklump Juice
preparations.description: Horklump juice is a magical substance extracted from Horklumps. It can be used in a range of healing potions as the primary fluid but is useless on its own.
preparations.raw_effect: Horklump juice is a magical substance extracted from Horklumps. It can be used in a range of healing potions as the primary fluid but is useless on its own.
preparations.effect_in_potions: Horklump juice is a magical substance extracted from Horklumps. It can be used in a range of healing potions as the primary fluid but is useless on its own.

preparations.name: Mandrake Stew
preparations.description: This is a stew made from harvested mandrakes that can be used in other potions.
preparations.effect_in_potions: The exact effect of the stew in potions is unknown. However, it does appear to be an essential part of many brews.

preparations.name: Manjistha Oil
preparations.description: Manjistha Oil is a common beauty product used throughout the wizarding world (when applied to the skin). It also can be consumed as a powerful curse containment agent (set value 17). In some cases, the curse may be too powerful to completely cure and manjistha oil can help to contain it (temporarily) in portions of the body (set value 24).

preparations.name: Murtlap Essence
preparations.description: A solution of strained and pickled tentacles of Murtlaps. Acts as a healing solution when applied directly onto injuries.

preparations.name: Neem Oil
