preparations.description: Neem seeds can be smashed to collect the oil. This oil treats many skin conditions. It also helps heal some magical scars, slightly. Finally, it can be rubbed on some human-form transfigurations to make the transfiguration last a little longer.

preparations.name: Peruvian Darkness Powder
preparations.description:
This fine powder is jet black and has such intense darkness binding qualities that it absorbs all light.
To counteract, a light source must be generated at a higher value than the quality of this preparation.
preparations.raw_effect:
Ingestion of This powder is dangerous. Symptoms of poisoning include convulsions and severe internal pain. Take 3H wounds per day for 2 days. Then 2H wounds per day for 3 days.
Then 1H wound per day for 5 days.

preparations.name: Phoenix Feather Ash
preparations.description: Ash from the feathers of a phoenix

preparations.name: Pickled Murtlap Tentacles
preparations.description: Pickling Murtlap Tentacles, then consuming them provides a temporary resistance to jinxes.

preparations.name: Powdered Basalt
preparations.description: A powdered form of basalt

preparations.name: Powdered Common Rue
preparations.description: A simple powder made from common rue cuttings

preparations.name: Powdered Dry Billywig Stings
preparations.description: Grind dried stings into a powder.

preparations.name: Powdered Garlic
preparations.description: Powdered Garlic is tasty in foods. It is mostly inert when directly ingested or touched. However, it will repel vampires or any creature with significant vampiric bloodlines.

preparations.name: Powdered Graphorn Horn

books.name: Trollbinding Hexcraft
books.description: First-year hexes that fetter stone-skins and dim troll rage—cautionary study.

books.name: Tummo’s Flora Codex
books.description: Catalog of heat‑generating botanicals used in inner‑fire (Tummo) yogas for living wards.

books.name: Twelve Fail-Safe Ways to Charm Witches
books.author: River Latham

books.name: Umbra’s Inception: Foundations of the Dark Arts
books.description: Introduces controlled exploration of dark energy. Drawing on time‑honored practices of occult restraint, this text trains students to harness and balance shadow forces ethically and effectively.

books.name: Unbroken: Mastering Dream-forged Defensive Magic
books.description: Finalizes defense training with elite-level protective spells, unyielding focus, and the seamless integration of dreamcasting methods into high-stress defensive scenarios.

books.name: Understanding the Night Sky
books.description: Mid level Astronomy text

books.name: Unflinching Darkness: Mastery and Responsibility in Dreamcasted Dark Arts
books.description: Teaches full mastery and responsibility in Dark Arts, covering advanced dreamcasting techniques for ethically confronting and controlling dark forces.

books.name: Unfogging the Future
books.author: Cassandra Vablatsky
books.description: This is a general Divination text offering an introduction into its many branches.

books.name: Unyielding Forms: Principles of Dreamcasting Transfiguration
books.description: A comprehensive, authoritative guide outlining the meticulous discipline needed to achieve transfiguration through dreamcasting, focusing heavily on mental precision and symbolic transformations.

books.name: Veiled Rituals: The Secret Transmission of Magic
books.description: Examines how occult knowledge—spanning practices like Dangun Protection and Saju Divinatory Arts—was covertly passed down through clandestine societies. This work highlights the methods by which esoteric techniques were preserved and transmitted, shaping the evolution of practical and theoretical magic.

books.name: Veiled Rituals: Transmission of Occult Lore
books.description: Explores how secret societies and mystery schools clandestinely preserved esoteric knowledge. It details the passage of ritual texts and practical rites that shaped the evolution of hidden, practical magic.

books.name: Veiled Strength: Disciplined Approaches to Dark Spelluse
books.description: Deepens students’ disciplined practice of Dark Arts, with rigorous mental control and responsible use of dream and trance-based dark magic.

books.name: Venom & Vine: The Basics
books.description: Safe extraction of frog toxins, curare bark, and foundational antidote brews.

books.name: Vices and Villainy
books.description: An early, hefty book introducing one to a number of Dark Arts.

books.name: Vicious Magic
books.description: A high level Dark Arts text.

books.name: Visions of the Salars: Advanced Divination
books.description: Guides students through complex divinatory rituals and symbolic readings, deepening their capacity to decipher prophetic images borne from the flats’ shimmering reflections.

books.name: Voice and Vapour: Intermediate Charm‑casting with Aromatic Amplifiers
books.description: Guides students in pairing spoken spells with controlled plumes of scented steam to extend range or soften impact; stresses timing and breath control.

books.name: Voice of Brass: Advanced Projection Charms
books.description: Long‑range and layered spells reinforced by bronze resonance plates.

books.name: Voices of the Canopy
books.description: Communication, care, and minor spirit bargains with monkeys, macaws, and poison frogs.

books.name: Voices of Yurupary
books.description: Introduction to sacred flute chants used in traditional Amazonian rituals to maintain social and ecological harmony.

books.name: Void Lattice Unsealing
books.description: Final‑year treatise on dismantling spatial prisons and null curses.

books.name: Volatile Reductions: High‑Potency Brews and Safety Protocols
books.description: Covers pressure‑sealed reductions, flash‑distillation of essential oils, and strict lab‑safety drills for highly reactive spice extracts.

books.name: Wandlore
books.author: Sahil Page
books.description: History textbook. This is the go-to source about Wandlore. Note, most of the information is out of date given that no one has bothered to update it since the days of Harry, Dumbledore, Voldemort, and Draco.

books.name: Wards of the Sky Spear
books.description: Intermediate defensive rituals based on Nilotic rainmakers' spear rituals, focused on weather-based countermeasures.

books.name: Waters of Memory
books.description: Huastec river myths, cave‑portal lore, and early silver‑vein brewing records.

books.name: Webbreakers of Fate
books.description: High seiðr counter-spells for severing hostile destiny-threads without backlash.

books.name: Wellward Invocation
books.description: Advanced water‑blessing charms incorporating Tobar Beannaithe rituals.

books.name: Well‑Rune Concord
books.description: Mastery of Tobar Beannaithe water runes for enduring restorative enchantments.

books.name: Where Theres a Wand, There's a Way
books.author: Rafe Salter
books.description: A supportive book about taking care of yourself in tough tmes. Removes psychological damage the first time you read it.

books.name: Whispering Current Forms
books.description: Spoken rune chains tuned to the waterfall’s pulse; echo‑locking technique.

books.name: Windogrand's Wonderous Water Plants
books.author: Nadia Short
books.description: Herbology textbook.

books.name: Winds of Time: Early Chronicles of Broom Flight
books.description: Explores the origins of magical flight, tracing the evolution of broomcraft through ancient texts and early experimental flight techniques recorded by pioneering wizards.

books.name: Wings of the Falls
books.description: Cliff‑swift messengers, water sprites, and blind cave‑fish scouts.

books.name: Winti Bestiary: Spirits of the Wild
books.description: Explores regional magical fauna by linking native creatures to ancestral spirits, using Winti ritual insights to reveal hidden magical bonds.

books.name: Witch’s Brew Compendium
books.description: Master‑level anti‑Spectral Evidence infusions, blending sacred herb essences to dispel Familiar Spirits.

books.name: Wolf-Breath Maledictions
books.description: Offensive gale-curses channelled through berserker breath-work.

books.name: Wood to Spirit: Introductory Mask Shifts
books.description: Voice and eyesight alterations using simple Gelede masks.

books.name: Xapiripë Rune Mastery
books.description: Expert rune techniques to invoke and communicate with Yanomami spirits.

books.name: Your First Little Addition
books.author: Greyson Powder
books.description: A parenting book about newborns.

books.name: Zagovary's Iron Index
books.description: Advanced Zagovary Defense Text

books.name: Ōmine
books.description: Stamina draughts brewed under running mountain water.

proficiencies.name: A Cure for Spattergroit Infection
proficiencies.description: A healer from the middle ages had a theory that "the only remedy is to take the liver of a toad, bind it tight about your throat, stand naked by the full moon in a barrel of eels’ eyes".

proficiencies.name: Abacomancy
proficiencies.description: When dust is present, the diviner can investigate to reveal the destination of those who traveled through this area. This will not help them know who passed through.

proficiencies.name: Acacia Tree Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Acacia Tree Proper Care and Safe Handling
Acromantula Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to track, identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Acultomancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner flicks sewing needles into a shallow bowl and studies the skein they weave. This will allow the practitioner to discern the approximate location of a nearby magical energy or may point one in the direction of an important magical target (as in the destination they seek relative to a quest). Headmaster sets the target value required to acquire the information desired.
proficiencies.history: Springing from Romani and Near‑Eastern needle‑casting, acultomancy (a variant of acutomancy) reads the arabesque patterns made when seven – twenty‑one sewing needles are dropped into water, flour, or ash; broken lines augur journeys, parallel ranks presage lucre, and vertical spurs chart destiny’s straight road.

proficiencies.name: Advanced Anthomancy
proficiencies.description: The practitioner can scatter daisy flower petals on the head of a victim of psychological damage (sanity shaking, depressing, or anxiety-related) to understand their troubles, predict their recovery and heal 5H wound of psychological damage once per day per target.

proficiencies.name: Advanced Anthropomancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner fashions a foul effigy—wax shell, pork viscera, a strand of the muggle’s hair—then slashes the mock entrails while intoning the name. The named target, no matter the distance, immediately suffers 3H wound of disease/toxic damage. They will continue to take 2H wound for per day for seven days. Once this is cured, no Anthropomancy attempts are ever successful on this target again.
proficiencies.history: In Akkadian omen‑lists, royal haruspices dissected prisoners to read the “tablet of the gods” writ upon warm viscera, believing each lobe and sulcus mirrored cosmic provinces. Hellenistic warlords occasionally turned to the grisly rite when ovine livers proved inconclusive, though philosophers like Plutarch decried it as tyrannical hubris. Early Byzantine chronicles condemn errant bishops who read a slave’s heart for auguries of plague, prompting imperial edicts that conflated anthropomancy with outright sorcery. Medieval grimoires preserve instructions for crafting wax homunculi stuffed with animal offal as a bloodless substitute, a concession to tightening Church proscriptions. By the seventeenth century the practice lingered only in whispered necromantic circles, its visceral lexicon absorbed into later anatomical “science of correspondences.”

proficiencies.name: Advanced Catoptromancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner unveils a lustrous hand‑mirror and reads fleeting images in its depths. For the rest of the evening, the target is immune to any mental spell‑effects and cannot take anxiety-related, depressive, or sanity-shaking damage. This can also be used to heal 1H wound of any anxiety-related, depressive, or sanity-shaking damage
proficiencies.history: Ancient Greek seers lowered a polished bronze mirror into a temple well; the face that shimmered on its surface foretold life or death. Roman matrons peered into hand‑mirrors by moonlight to glimpse would‑be lovers, insisting a true match left a rosy halo round the reflection. Medieval thieves’‑catchers used blackened glass: the first glint of a culprit’s likeness named the pilferer to be seized at dawn. Elizabethan magicians such as Dr Dee prized obsidian “shew‑stones,” claiming fallen angels whispered through the dark glass. By the nineteenth century parlour mystics sold small scrying mirrors, keeping the art alive wherever a flicker of candle met polished silver.

proficiencies.name: Advanced Leacanach
proficiencies.description: By dripping oil, water, or any other fluid into other fluids, a the practitioner can heal 3H wound of any type of damage caused by hexes, jinxes, curses, or poisons. Once per day per target.
proficiencies.history: Also called Lecanomancy. From Greek lekane “basin,” lecanomancy reads ripples, oil‑slicks, or floating objects in a shallow dish of water. Babylonian seers used it to unmask hidden guilt; medieval magi prized it for discovering thieves and sorcerous afflictions.

proficiencies.name: Advanced Muggle Cooking
proficiencies.description: This proficiency allows one to prepare a world-class meal using muggle approaches

proficiencies.name: Aeromancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner can read wind and weather patterns such that they are more aware of the exact trajectory of any projectiles (not magical blasts, but physical objects). As such they can inform the party who can take +2 to any attempt to dodge or block projectiles of any kind for the rest of the battle.

proficiencies.name: Agalmatomancy
proficiencies.description:
The seer crafts a small idol (out of literally anything and with whatever quality) of any creature or human that they have met in person ever. The seer then examines the interplay of light and shadows upon the surface of the idol. While holding in mind the creature or human they wish to examine, they may glean any one bit of information:
1. Last set of eyes. A description of the physical location in which another human last saw this target (e.g., Troy was last seen inside a cave, we know nothing of the one who saw him or what they discussed, but we can describe the physical environment in which they were seen).
2. Skill of Greatest Power. The exact base value of the highest skill as it currently appears on the target's character sheet (e.g., they are able to roll a base 28 in Dark Arts).
3. Current Motivation. The seer may get basic information about the target's current drives or motivations.
This may be attempted once per play session.
proficiencies.history: From Greek agalma “statue,” agalmatomancy divines omens in idols—cracks in marble, sweat on bronze, or the way a wax effigy melts. Ancient Greeks watched temple statues that wept or bled; medieval magi cast soft figurines into water, reading the fissures to diagnose hidden malefice.

proficiencies.name: Agapanthus Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Agapanthus Proper Care and Safe Handling
Agrimonia Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Agrimonia Proper Care and Safe Handling
Ailuromancy
proficiencies.description: The augur can observe the behaviors and movements of cats as a means of identifying nearby magical beings, creatures, objects, or locations. Transfigured/Conjured cats can be used. Once this proficiency is successfully used performed, the practitioner takes +5 to perception checks for anything related to something of a magical source.
proficiencies.history: From Greek ailouros “cat,” ailuromancy divines by feline comportment—tail flicks at dusk, the hearth‑side orientation of a dozing mouser, or a stray’s moonlit yowl. Sailors eyed ship’s cats to augur tempests; hearth‑seers foretold visitors and fortunes from the first paw over a threshold.

proficiencies.name: Airline Usage
proficiencies.description: This proficiency allows muggles to appropriately and effectively navigate an airport and plane ride.

proficiencies.name: Alectormancy
proficiencies.description: Interpreting the eating patterns of roosters, diviners can gain insights into the motivations of creatures granting +2 to any magical creatures roll to any target they choose including themselves.
proficiencies.history: From Greek alektōr “rooster.” Seers scattered alphabet‑marked grain in a circle and let a cock peck out letters that spelled the gods’ reply—an oracle prized from Rome’s camp‑tents to medieval barnyards.

proficiencies.name: Aleuromancy
proficiencies.description: Practitioners use flour to form symbols that gain insights into deep magical realities. This grants any target of this proficiency +5 to ancient runes.
proficiencies.history: Greek aleuron “flour.” Seers embedded lots in dough or traced flour‑whorls on bronze to augur plenty, plague, or famine.

proficiencies.name: Alihotsy Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Alihotsy Proper Care and Safe Handling
Aloe Vera Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Aloe Vera Proper Care and Safe Handling
Alomancy
proficiencies.description: Practitioners hold salt in their hands while thinking of two others. They then cast the salt into a fire. Doing so makes either one more likely to convince the other. The one doing the convincing gets a +5 to their next social skills check.

proficiencies.name: Alphitomancy
proficiencies.description: Practitioners can sprinkle barley cereal upon an injured individual or creature to get a reading on the exact nature of their injuries. This will allow anyone they share this information with to heal the individual for 1H wound more than their regular heal would typically allow.

proficiencies.name: Alveromancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner attunes to surrounding echoes—clapping, snapping, or humming until resonances divulge hidden stresses. Each target gains +5 to their next perception roll that involves detecting traps, unstable structures, or concealed magic.
proficiencies.history: Across Pharaonic Egypt and Hellenistic porticoes, hierophants held that phōnē was a direct conduit to the numina; they listened to the boom of cataracts, the crack of temple doors, and the hush between thunderclaps for auguries of empire. Medieval Arab geomancers refined the craft, mapping caravan fates from the echo‑time of camel bells in canyon hollows. By the Renaissance, itinerant peddlers hawked “oracle‑boxes” whose hidden reeds emitted tones said to name a thief or predict plague, a practice ecclesiastics decried as chicanery. Alpine shepherds later gauged avalanches from distant gun‑like reports, folding the lore into local alveromantic charts. Though eclipsed by modern acoustics, the art survives wherever a whisper in a cave or the ocean’s susurrus is thought to carry god‑tiding.

proficiencies.name: Amathomancy
proficiencies.description: The practitioner gains a deep resonance with the particulates in the air. They stifle any and all spell casts of the environmental subtype for the rest of a battle (or 10 minutes, whichever comes first) in the immediate area.
proficiencies.history: Sprung from the Greek amathos (“sand”), amathomancy bid ancient seers cast sand or grave‑ash upon marble to read the errant whorls, claiming each dune‑like ridge mirrored the coils of fate. Hellenistic manuals tally portents in drifting silt—forked lines presaged quarrel, concentric rings a safe voyage—while late‑Roman necromancers prized the cremains of nobles, believing the dead whispered by way of their own soot. Byzantine penitentials still condemn “dust‑letters” traced at crossroads to discover thieves, hinting the craft survived beneath ecclesiastical censure. Renaissance occultists revived the art in alchemical ateliers, sieving pulverized bone onto black glass to chart planetary conjunctions. Even modern mythic lore cites artists like Jackson Pollock, whose paint‑spatter “readings” echoed the old dust sigils.

proficiencies.name: Ambulomancy
proficiencies.description: Observing the tracks left in snow, mud, or dirt allows the practitioner to learn about a single traveler who has traveled through this area recently. They may learn their approximate age, gender-identity, basic characteristics and skill-related strengths.
proficiencies.history: From Latin ambulare “to walk,” ambulomancy divines fate by watching the cadence, direction, and happenstance of passers‑by. Romans took auguries from the first stranger to cross a threshold at dawn; later wayfarers traced stray‑dog tracks and errant footprints to choose auspicious roads.

proficiencies.name: Amniomancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner unfurls a preserved (or conjured) birth‑caul and scrutinizes its whorls. The target of this proficiency can mitigate 2H wounds of vital damage. If anyone or anything attempts to inflict the target with vital damage, that damage is simply ignored (as if this proficiency acts as a sort of shield).
proficiencies.history: Since antiquity, midwives prized the caul—the translucent amniotic veil sometimes sheathing a newborn’s head—as a numinous chart of the child’s destiny. Roman matronae preserved it in salt, reading minute folds to foretell prosperity or untimely peril, while sailors bought dried cauls as talismans against drowning, believing the membrane that once cocooned life could still ward the sea. Medieval cunning women and Scottish “wise wifies” consulted cauls to diagnose infant maladies and divine a household’s fortunes for the coming year. Renaissance physicians like Paracelsus wrote of signatura hidden in the caul’s marbling, linking its patterns to humoral imbalances. Though later denounced as superstition, the practice endured in rural Europe well into the nineteenth century, whispered of as “veil‑scrying.”

proficiencies.name: Anemoscopy
proficiencies.description: Reading the wind patterns gives the reader a bonus to their flying abilities. The reader themselves can take +6 to their flying. They can also inform others about these patterns and those others can take a +3 bonus to their flying abilities. Effects wear off after six hours.

proficiencies.name: Angel's Trumpet Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Angel's Trumpet Proper Care and Safe Handling
Anglican Elemental Guidance
proficiencies.description: Through the use of a particular Anglican celestial reading, the practitioner may guide others to the precise weaknesses in a spirits defenses. This provides a +3 boost to any spell casts against a spirit.

proficiencies.name: Anglican Meteoric Blade Rite
proficiencies.description: This practice doubles any cutting damage (no other damage types) delivered by allies to outsiders for the remainder of battle.

proficiencies.name: Anthroposcopy
proficiencies.description: By reading a person's general aura, the practitioner makes the receiver more susceptible to influence for the next 1 hour (they take -1 on any social skills checks for the next hour.
proficiencies.history: Rooted in classical physiognomica ascribed to Aristotle, anthroposcopy gauges destiny from the planes of cheekbones, the cant of eyebrows, and the rhythm of gait. Hellenistic physicians married it to humoral theory, reading sallow complexions as melancholic fates and sanguine flushes as auguries of triumph. Tang‑dynasty “face‑reading” manuals refined the art, mapping fortunes to sixty‑four ling points upon the countenance, while medieval European clerics alternately prized and proscribed the practice as a window to hidden sin. Renaissance savants such as Giambattista della Porta collated nose shapes, lip lines, and even ear‑lobes into elaborate taxonomies that promised insight into courage, cupidity, or treachery. By the nineteenth century it morphed into phrenology’s craniometric excesses before retreating to occult parlors as a discreet technique for reading motives at a glance.

proficiencies.name: Apantomancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner understands the deep and vital resonance of a bonded pet and can, once per the lifetime of the creature, grant it +1H wound capacity for the rest of its life. This can only be attempted once per creature per lifetime. This can be attempted on pets of allies.
proficiencies.history: Greek magoi coined apantomancy from apanthanein, “to chance upon,” discerning omens in whatever creature or object first crossed one’s path. Roman augurs weighed the portent of barking dogs, one‑eyed men, or stray cats at dawn, their note‑books bristling with graded auspices. Medieval wayfarers watched the road’s first passer‑by—monk, crone, or red‑haired youth—to judge whether the day promised profit or peril. Renaissance grimoires codified entire bestiaries of chance‑encounters: a hare on the left betokened betrayal; a blackbird alighting south augured sudden windfall. Though later mocked as mere superstition, sailors, gamblers, and hedge‑witches still mutter luck‑charms based on whatever apparition greets them at the threshold.

proficiencies.name: Apple Tree Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Apple Tree Proper Care and Safe Handling
Appliance Utilization
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves successfully using muggle appliances. Included in this is effectively suppressing one's own magical aura to avoid interfering with the appliance's operation.

proficiencies.name: Application of a Charms Spell (with threshold 12) to a prepared object
proficiencies.description: Allows one to affix the effects of a Charms spell (with a threshold value of 12 or lower) to an object that has been properly preparted for artificing. Failure renders the object permanently unenchantable. Critical failure destroys the object outright (assuming there are no other enchantments protecting the item from destruction). The artificer must have at least a dose of the potion on hand to properly artifice it to an object

proficiencies.name: Application of a Charms Spell (with threshold 15) to a prepared object
proficiencies.description: Allows one to affix the effects of a Charms spell (with a threshold value of 15 or lower) to an object that has been properly preparted for artificing. proficiencies.name: Arnica Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Arnica Proper Care and Safe Handling
Arthurian Cosmic Reasoning
proficiencies.description: Through the use of an Arthurian perspective (primarily derived from legendary texts), one can provide a small boost (+2) to any attempts to craft a magical or non-magical weapon including by use of artificing.

proficiencies.name: Artificing Preparation of a Large Object
proficiencies.description: This effect prepares a large object (size 4) for artificing. If the roll is failed, the object is completely destroyed. If the roll is critically failed, the practitioner takes 4H wounds of burn damage. Once the prepared object is created, please add a note in your inventory that the object has been "prepared for artificing"

proficiencies.name: Artificing Preparation of a Medium Sized Object
proficiencies.description: This effect prepares a medium sized object (size 3) for artificing. If the roll is failed, the object is completely destroyed. If the roll is critically failed, the practitioner takes 3H wounds of burn damage. Once the prepared object is created, please add a note in your inventory that the object has been "prepared for artificing"

proficiencies.name: Artificing Preparation of a Small Object
proficiencies.description: This effect prepares a small object (size 2) for artificing. If the roll is failed, the object is completely destroyed. If the roll is critically failed, the practitioner takes 2H wounds of burn damage. Once the prepared object is created, please add a note in your inventory that the object has been "prepared for artificing"

proficiencies.name: Artificing Preparation of a Very Large Object
proficiencies.description: This effect prepares a very large object (size 5) for artificing. If the roll is failed, the object is completely destroyed. If the roll is critically failed, the practitioner takes 5H wounds of burn damage. Once the prepared object is created, please add a note in your inventory that the object has been "prepared for artificing"

proficiencies.name: Artificing Preparation of a Very Small Object
proficiencies.description: This effect prepares a very small object (size 1) for artificing. If the roll is failed, the object is completely destroyed. If the roll is critically failed, the practitioner takes 1H wound of burn damage. Once the prepared object is created, please add a note in your inventory that the object has been "prepared for artificing"

proficiencies.name: Ash Tree Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Ash Tree Proper Care and Safe Handling

proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Aspen Tree Proper Care and Safe Handling
Asphodel Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Asphodel Proper Care and Safe Handling
Astragalomancy
proficiencies.description: By casting knucklebones or dice, the diviner explores the connection with threads of magical energy in the vicinity. This allows them to grant a +3 bonus to arithmancy for any target once per target per day.
proficiencies.history: Greeks first cast astragaloi—polished sheep knucklebones scored with numerals—before Apollo’s shrines, trusting the tumble to reveal the god’s whim. Hellenistic gamblers pocketed the bones for both wagering and prophecy, believing a triple‑four fell only for the divinely favored. In republican Rome, sibyls etched planetary glyphs on astragaloi and claimed to read the spin, not the final number, for subtler fates. Byzantine manuals prescribe purple silk cloths, arguing the hue of emperors placates Fortuna and steadies the throw. By the late Renaissance, ivory dice supplanted bone, yet the old term endured in occult circles as shorthand for any lot‑casting that seeks to bend fortune’s braid.

proficiencies.name: Augury
proficiencies.description: The practitioner gains insights on nearby dangers. The value of this proficiency roll must exceed the set value for the danger itself if one exists. Else, the headmaster sets the necessary value for successful detection of the threat.

proficiencies.name: Austromancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner casts sand, studies banner‑whip, and names the covert cross‑currents. As a result of these resultant insights, their target takes +5 to flying rolls for the next hour.
proficiencies.history: From Latin Auster—the hot, storm‑laden south wind—Etruscan diviners first raised sailcloth “wind‑skins” over sacred groves, reading each billow as a god‑breathed cipher. Republican Roman augurs ranked a duodecad of winds and watched temple banners whip to time campaigns and coronations. Byzantine pilots carried bronze anemoscopes whose vanes, when struck by a sudden gust, foretold reefs or raiders lurking beyond the horizon. Medieval monastic chronologers personified every quarter‑wind as an angelic choir, scribbling weather omens in climataries that guided sowing, siege, and pilgrimage alike. Renaissance cartographers blended compass‑rose and zodiac, plotting the “music of the air” so merchants could sail with fortune’s own bellows at their stern.

proficiencies.name: Avalonian Mist Manipulation
proficiencies.description: The practitioner simply boils water in a specific way so as to create a very low hanging very dense fog over a large area. This substantially lowers perception rolls in the area for anyone.

proficiencies.name: Avoid Precognitive Sight
proficiencies.description: Understanding the Demiguise ability of future sight and how it works on probability. Experienced individuals can take random and unlikely actions to trick the Demiguise.

proficiencies.name: Bacopa Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Bacopa Proper Care and Safe Handling
Balm Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Balm Proper Care and Safe Handling
Bamboo Plant Infection
proficiencies.description: This method infects a bamboo plant.

proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Bamboo Proper Care and Safe Handling
Baneberry Proper Care and Safe Handling
Basic Anthomancy
proficiencies.description: The practitioner can scatter daisy flower petals on the head of a victim of psychological damage (sanity shaking, depressing, or anxiety-related) to understand their troubles, predict their recovery and heal 2L wound of psychological damage once per day per target.

proficiencies.name: Basic Anthropomancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner fashions a foul effigy—wax shell, pork viscera, a strand of the muggle’s hair—then slashes the mock entrails while intoning the name. The named target, no matter the distance, immediately suffers 1 H wound of disease/toxic damage. They will continue to take 1M wound for per day for seven days. Once this is cured, no Anthropomancy attempts are ever successful on this target again.
proficiencies.history: In Akkadian omen‑lists, royal haruspices dissected prisoners to read the “tablet of the gods” writ upon warm viscera, believing each lobe and sulcus mirrored cosmic provinces. Hellenistic warlords occasionally turned to the grisly rite when ovine livers proved inconclusive, though philosophers like Plutarch decried it as tyrannical hubris. Early Byzantine chronicles condemn errant bishops who read a slave’s heart for auguries of plague, prompting imperial edicts that conflated anthropomancy with outright sorcery. Medieval grimoires preserve instructions for crafting wax homunculi stuffed with animal offal as a bloodless substitute, a concession to tightening Church proscriptions. By the seventeenth century the practice lingered only in whispered necromantic circles, its visceral lexicon absorbed into later anatomical “science of correspondences.”

proficiencies.name: Basic Catoptromancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner unveils a lustrous hand‑mirror and reads fleeting images in its depths. This can also be used to heal 1L wound of any anxiety-related, depressive, or sanity-shaking damage
proficiencies.history: Ancient Greek seers lowered a polished bronze mirror into a temple well; the face that shimmered on its surface foretold life or death. Roman matrons peered into hand‑mirrors by moonlight to glimpse would‑be lovers, insisting a true match left a rosy halo round the reflection. Medieval thieves’‑catchers used blackened glass: the first glint of a culprit’s likeness named the pilferer to be seized at dawn. Elizabethan magicians such as Dr Dee prized obsidian “shew‑stones,” claiming fallen angels whispered through the dark glass. By the nineteenth century parlour mystics sold small scrying mirrors, keeping the art alive wherever a flicker of candle met polished silver.

proficiencies.name: Basic Leacanach
proficiencies.description: By dripping oil, water, or any other fluid into other fluids, a the practitioner can heal 1H wound of any type of damage caused by hexes, jinxes, curses, or poisons. Once per day per target.
proficiencies.history: Also called Lecanomancy. From Greek lekane “basin,” lecanomancy reads ripples, oil‑slicks, or floating objects in a shallow dish of water. Babylonian seers used it to unmask hidden guilt; medieval magi prized it for discovering thieves and sorcerous afflictions.

proficiencies.name: Basic Muggle Cooking
proficiencies.description: This proficiency allows one to prepare a basic meal using muggle approaches

proficiencies.name: Basil Proper Care and Safe Handling
Basilisk Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to track, identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for and breed this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Beaker Folk Alchemical Wisdom
proficiencies.description: This grants the practitioner +2 to Alchemy for the next day. Can be granted to others.

proficiencies.name: Beech Tree Proper Care and Safe Handling
Begonia Proper Care and Safe Handling
Bell-Beaker Asterism Despondency
proficiencies.description: An pre-historic practice that involves a form of horoscopic divination that channels the earliest Bell-Beaker understandings of asterisms. The net result for the target, however, is to experience, paradoxically, a deep sense of depression. The target will suffer 1H wound of depressive damage but will not understand that this practitioner was the cause of that injury.

proficiencies.name: Bell-Beaker Celestial Rhythming
proficiencies.description: By tapping on nearby materials, the practitioner connects with the rhythms of the universe to provide a boost to fortitude and willpower (+3) for the remainder of combat (or for about 3-5 minutes).

proficiencies.name: Belomancy
proficiencies.description:
The diviner fires or draws a rune‑etched arrow and reads its landing. For the next hour the target can add +3 to any attempts to use the common shielding spells (protego).
One target per day.
proficiencies.history: Babylonian priests inscribed queries on reed arrows, loosed three skyward, and took the one that landed farthest as the god‑sanctioned course. Greek hoplites knotted omens into fletchings, trusting the first shaft to strike earth to name the safest march‑route. Bedouin soothsayers shook a quiver of marked shafts and drew one blindfolded—the inscription dictated war, peace, or forbearance. Islamic jurists begrudgingly tolerated the practice when “yes,” “no,” and “wait” alone adorned the arrows, so long as no idols were invoked. Medieval crusaders revived the rite, firing black‑feathered bolts over castle walls to foretell which breach would yield before dawn.

proficiencies.name: Betony Proper Care and Safe Handling
Birthing Dragons
proficiencies.description: In the wild, dragons will lay their eggs next to or inside of a fire seed bush. However, this effect can be replicated by cooking the egg in a pot over a fire or by placing the dragon egg on top of fire seed embers or fire seeds themselves (while still smoldering).

proficiencies.name: Bitterroot Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Bitterroot Proper Care and Safe Handling

proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

Boomberry Bomb Creation
proficiencies.description: To create a boomberry bomb, the preparer must have boomberries on hand and have the Preparation and Proper Handling of a Boomberry Bomb expertise. Without this expertise, nothing happens and the bomb will not work.

Borage Proper Care and Safe Handling
Botanomancy
proficiencies.description:
The diviner fires a sprig chosen for the task and studies the sputtering sparks and smoke‑whorls. The target of this reading is inspired to greater efficiency in their harvesting techniques, gathering double the yield of any plant part extraction attempts for the next day.
One target one time per day.
proficiencies.history: Hellenic seers shorn fresh briar and perfumed vervain, carved their queries upon the twigs, then consigned them to the flame, judging destiny by the fuliginous smoke and the way green wood crackled. Roman manuals classed the art as a branch of pyromancy, insisting that east‑drifting ash foretold safe harvests while spiral jets augured civil discord. Medieval herb‑wives refined the rite with specific “speaking plants”: yew for death, rosemary for fidelity, mandrake for sorcery. Renaissance occultists, enamoured of sympathetic correspondences, burned a herb aligned to the querent’s birth planet and read the coiling plumes against black silk screens. Though modern parlours prefer tarot, some hedge‑mages still trust a sudden vermilion flare in vervain’s heart to signal the gods’

proficiencies.name: Bouncing Bulb Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Bouncing Bulb Proper Care and Safe Handling
Bowtruckle Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to track, identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Briton Star Patterning
proficiencies.description: This proficiency allows the practitioner to utilize the star patterns to grant a +1 bonus to agility for the remainder of combat (3-5 minutes non-combat).

proficiencies.name: Brythonic Celestial Harmony
proficiencies.description: By aligning with ancient Brythonic star maps, the practitioner may grant a +2 boost to Willpower for allies, drawing upon the balance and harmony revered by the Brythonic tribes. Lasts for the remainder of combat (or 3-5 minutes non-combat).

proficiencies.name: Brythonic Lunar Mysticism
proficiencies.description: This proficiency allows the practitioner to connect with horoscopic astronomical circumstances to anticipate the intentions of a single enemy, thereby forcing them to miss their next turn in battle.

proficiencies.name: Brythonic Understanding
proficiencies.description: This proficiency allows the practitioner to determine whether a creature intends to attack.

proficiencies.name: Bulbadox Proper Care and Safe Handling
Bulrush Proper Care and Safe Handling
Bundimum Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Callanish Ley-Line Mapping
proficiencies.description: This proficiency improves any shielding casts by +2 for the rest of the day. Can be granted to others.

proficiencies.name: Capnomancy
proficiencies.description:
The diviner burns a pinch of resin or parchment and studies the drift. In so doing, they come into resonance with the area around them. The diviner will notice any person who enters the area.
Wears off at dawn.

proficiencies.name: Caster Tracing
proficiencies.description: This gives the practitioner basic information about who may have cast a given spell that they've detected. This information is typically pretty basic. This will usually include information such as the age of the caster, size, their favored skills or traditions, or possibly a favorite spell. This allows the practitioner to gain insights into who might cast in this particular way.

proficiencies.name: Causimancy
proficiencies.description: Practitioners gather an object from another person, then destroy it in a fire. While observing the specific way that the fire destroys the object, the practitioner understands the relationship between this person's aura and the objects they possess. They grant the target +2 to their next artificing attempt.
proficiencies.history: Ancient Greek hearth‑priests tossed laurel leaves into the blaze; if they crackled sharply and leapt, victory in battle was assured, but if they hissed and smouldered, ruin loomed. Roman household shrines kept a small brazier where bits of cloth were burned each dawn—the direction the first spark flew foretold quarrels or peace in the domus. In Celtic Brittany, midsummer revellers judged the coming harvest by whether tossed woad‑bundles burst blue or fell dark in the bonfire. Medieval smith‑mages read sword destinies from the colour of the first tongue of flame that kissed a newly forged blade. Renaissance alchemists refined the art with chemicals, noting that a green flare meant hidden poison in the patron’s court, while a pure white column promised safe transmutation.

proficiencies.name: Cayenne Proper Care and Safe Handling
Cedarwood Proper Care and Safe Handling
Celebratory Customs
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves effectively celebrating in a muggle way without drawing suspicion. Failure results in negative attention and judgement. The greater the failure, the greater the scrutiny.

proficiencies.name: Celtic Combat Timing
proficiencies.description: The Celts understanding of time and timing can be utilized to provide a slight advantage in combat of +2 to any counterspell attempts for any allies.

proficiencies.name: Celtic Harnessing of Morrigan
proficiencies.description: A connection with the Astral remnants of Morrigan allow for a +3 bonus to Hex or Curse cast by allies for the duration of a battle (or 3-5 minutes non-combat)

proficiencies.name: Celtic Knotwork for Magic Detection
proficiencies.description: Practitioners can create intricate Celtic knots that vibrate in the presence of strong enchantments, increasing their ability to detect hidden magical items, locations, and wards.

proficiencies.name: Celtic Knotwork Illusions
proficiencies.description: The practitioner hangs these intricate patterns of knotwork nearby to create a perimeter. Those approaching the perimeter will experience illusions of creatures, spirits, or other distractions that draw their attention away from the perimeter itself.

proficiencies.name: Centaury Plant Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Centaury Proper Care and Safe Handling
Cephalomancy
proficiencies.description: Diviners break the skull of an animal at the feet of one they wish to read granting +2 to naturalism for the next day.
proficiencies.history: Priests in ancient Babylonia set a donkey’s severed head on live coals and listened for the first crack to name a hidden culprit. Greek seers boiled a kid’s skull until fissures traced letters that spelled out the thief or the sick child’s fate. Medieval judges sometimes balanced a suspect’s shorn skull‑cap on red‑hot iron; the way the bone smoked was said to prove innocence or guilt. Breton hedge‑healers tapped hollow cow skulls like drums—the pitch of the echo diagnosed madness or possession. By the seventeenth century fortune‑tellers were rapping coconuts at fairs, claiming the hollow thunk still carried the old omens of cephalomancy.

proficiencies.name: Ceraunoscopy
proficiencies.description: The diviner's foresight fully mitigates any eruption/explosion damage for a target for the rest of the day. The target cannot take damage of this type at all.
proficiencies.history: Etruscan fulguratores fixed their gaze on storm‑torn skies, claiming each jagged bolt etched the gods’ verdict across the clouds. Greek temple records ranked omens by quadrant: a flash over the northern horn portended famine, while one in the west promised naval triumph. Roman augurs sent lictors to every smoking strike‑site, reading vitrified soil like crystal to divine the Senate’s fortunes. Medieval weather‑witches timed the gap between flash and clap, swearing the thunder’s cadence matched the march‑beat of looming armies. Renaissance pilots watching forked fire over capes charted its branching arcs as invisible coastlines, steering ships clear of hidden shoals.

proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves effectively complimenting a muggle. Failure results in negative attention and judgement. The greater the failure, the greater the scrutiny. It may also involve offending the muggle.

proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

Conjunctivitis Pus Extraction
proficiencies.description: The puss from the eye of a human with conjunctivitis can be extracted.

proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

Cornish Potioning Practices
proficiencies.description: The proficiency grants the practitioner a +1 bonus to any potioncrafting attempts. Can be granted to others.

proficiencies.name: Cornish Solar Channeling
proficiencies.description: Through the use of solar resonance, the practitioner can provide a +3 boost for any spell casts involving fire to any ally for the remainder of a battle.

proficiencies.name: Cowbane Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Cowbane Proper Care and Safe Handling
Creature Bonding Detection
proficiencies.description: Let's the practitioner know whether this creature is bonded to anyone.

proficiencies.name: Creature Cave Drawing
proficiencies.description: By drawing creatures on a cave wall using Red Ochre and in this Early European Stylistic Tradition early, you can detect the exact location of any such creature in the area. However, it may move from the time you finish your drawing to the time you make it to the location in question.

proficiencies.name: Creeper Proper Care and Safe Handling
Crystallomancy
proficiencies.description: Rotating a faceted crystal beneath steady light and interpreting the refracted lattice of beams and shadows for hidden vectors of fate. Using a crystal ball allows oneself to peer through the eyes of a far away target sometimes gleaning information about their location or motivations. The target rolls a fortitude roll to defend against this incursion into their perspective.
proficiencies.history: Bronze-Age miners in Nubia submerged freshly quarried quartz in Nile water; any shard that shimmered thrice foretold a pharaoh’s safe passage through the cataracts. Gupta-period r̥ṣis carved hexagonal lenses from rock-crystal, claiming the sun-spun rainbows inside named exactly which village would bear the next enlightened child. When crusaders reached Outremer, Levantine lapidaries traded “star stones” whose asterisms, read at moon-rise, warned caravans where Bedouin raiders waited in the wadi bends. In 1780s Prague, alchemists suspended beryl spheres over candle grids; the dancing caustics mapped debt-bubble collapses weeks before the court bankers panicked. Modern laser technicians still keep a sliver of yttrium garnet on the bench—an unexpected green flash in its core famously tipped CERN physicists to a cooling-tower leak hours before sensors tripped.

proficiencies.name: Cure for Jaundice using Dandelions
proficiencies.description: This simple remedy can be used to immediately and completely cure jaundice.

proficiencies.name: Currency Usage
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves understanding and utilizing muggle currency. Failure results in spending too much or otherwise losing money.

proficiencies.name: Curse Tracing
proficiencies.description: This allows the practitioner to notice any lingering curses in an area. Typically the practitioner will get a vague sense for the intensity, breadth (how wide an area does this cover), and duration of the curse. For instance, the practitioner may be able to determine that a curse was recently placed and is "contained" to this small area. They may alternatively learn that this curse is quite old and rather vast.

proficiencies.name: Cyclomancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner sends a rune‑carved top spinning and watches the arc where it comes to rest. In so doing, their target takes +1 to their next spell cast.
proficiencies.history: Ancient Greek soothsayers spun bronze shields on their rims and read fate in the way the disks wobbled and fell. Roman household magicians used tiny bone tops etched with gods’ names, letting the pointing god dictate which petition they should make. In the Middle Ages, hedge‑witches whirled spindle whorls over chalk sigils to pick healing herbs or curse‑targets. Renaissance occultists preferred compass‑like wheels engraved with zodiac signs, claiming the stopping sign marked the querent’s ruling planet for the day. Spiritualists of the nineteenth century adapted the idea into table‑turning and planchette boards, keeping the spinning‑object oracle alive into modern parlours.

proficiencies.name: Dactylomancy
proficiencies.description: The diviner suspends a consecrated ring on a strand of hair and lets it swing over a circle of runes. If the target or the seer successfully blocks or dodges any incoming attack during a battle, they both will be immune from that attack for the rest of the battle. The diviner and target fully anticipate this specific kind of attack for the rest of the battle and will not be affected by it.
proficiencies.history: Greek matrons looped a gold ring on flax thread above a lettered tray; the letters the ring tapped spelled the gods’ reply. Roman legionaries spun iron signet rings over maps to choose a battle’s breach point, trusting whichever valley the ring’s gem pointed toward. Medieval scholars dangled bishop’s rings over astrolabes, reading the arc of the swing to divine eclipses and hidden treachery. Fortune‑tellers of the Victorian séance era used wedding bands as pendulums, claiming the number of sways foretold births or deaths in a household. Even modern dowser‑witches keep a silver ring in their kit, saying metal that has “tasted skin” sings truer to destiny than any crystal.

proficiencies.name: Daisy Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Daisy Proper Care and Safe Handling
Dandelion Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Dandelion Proper Care and Safe Handling
Daphnomancy
proficiencies.description: The practitioner burns the leaves or fallen plant parts off of a sick or dying plant more deeply understanding its destiny. This allows them to grant +5 to any herbology checks or spell cast targeting this plant.
proficiencies.history: Priests of Apollo kept groves of bay trees; when their leaves rustled without wind, the oracle declared the god was near. Greek seers also flung fresh laurel leaves onto sacred fires—if the leaves crackled and jumped, a petition would succeed, but if they smouldered quietly, misfortune lurked. Roman generals crowned themselves in bay after victory, believing the tree’s spirit had guided their strategy. Medieval herb‑witches still burned laurel on Midwinter nights, saying the popping leaves warned of sickness or witch‑craft approaching the village. Even today, coastal folk in the Mediterranean toss a bay leaf on the hearth: a sharp snap means welcome news, a dull hiss means keep the door barred.
