proficiencies.name: Moondew Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Moonseed Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Morning Glory Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Mortomancy
proficiencies.description: Palming the chill flesh of a newly stilled body and reading the first twitches of post-mortem drift allow the user to ask the fallen a single question. If answerable, the fallen shall answer.
proficiencies.history: Old-Kingdom embalmers in Abydos pressed reeds into cadavers’ throats; the pattern of expelled air pockets declared which heir the god-king’s ka favored. Roman battlefield surgeons mapped muscle-spasm “death smiles,” recording that a left-tilted grimace forewarned plague in the nearest legion camp. In 13th-century Samarkand, plague-pit guardians timed city gates by how long fresh corpses bled clear—merchants swore it saved whole caravans. Victorian anatomists at Guy’s Hospital kept secret ledgers: if a corpse’s fingers curled during the first incision, the next cholera wave would follow the Thames that week. Modern forensic teams in remote war zones still log body-cooling anomalies; NATO relief planners quietly consult those graphs before air-dropping supplies.

proficiencies.name: Motherwort Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Muggle Entertainment
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves effectively entertaining muggles without being detected as a magician (often leading to fame)

proficiencies.name: Muggle Fashion
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves understanding muggle fashion well enough to blend in amongst muggles in public. Failure results in negative attention and judgement. The greater the failure, the greater the scrutiny.

proficiencies.name: Muggle First Aid
proficiencies.description:
This proficiency allows the practitioner to utilize muggle approaches to healing wounds of cut/scratch, abrasion/scrape, slash/gash, puncture/pierching (the piercing object must be removed first), gouge, burn, eruptive/explosive types by 1L wound per injury.
Critical fails cause an infection (disease type damage) which intensifies if not healed.
Day - Wound Amount
Day 1 - 2L
Day 2 - 1M
Day 3 - 1H
Day 4 - 2H
Day 5 - 3H

proficiencies.name: Muggle Post
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves effectively utilizing the muggle postage system

proficiencies.name: Muggle Sports Spectation
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves blending in while observing muggle sports. Failure results in negative attention and judgement. The greater the failure, the greater the scrutiny.

proficiencies.name: Muggle Workplace Decorum
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves fitting in at a muggle workplace.

proficiencies.name: Myomancy
proficiencies.description: Myomancy involves observing the movements of small rodents. Doing so allows the practitioner to grant any target +3 to their next creatures roll.
proficiencies.history: Thracian grain-keepers scattered fresh barley at dusk, judging a harvest’s fate by which quadrant temple mice emptied first. Republican Roman augurs raised white mice in cedar cages, freeing them before battle; a scamper toward the enemy’s banner foretold victory bought dearly. Plague-era Florentine doctors made nightly sketches of sewer-rat routes, claiming the morning’s curve of corpses followed those very lines. In Edo-period Osaka, “nezumi onmyōji” mapped bite-marks on ledgers to warn merchants of looming warehouse fires. Today’s metro clairvoyants time the scuttle of platform rats against train whistles, selling commuters whispers of delays, derailments, or market dips hours ahead.

proficiencies.name: Neem Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Neighborly Relations
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves all things related to the external presentation of one's home and interactions with the neighbors. Failure results in the home being seen as a blight or oddity on the street bringing scrutiny, disdain, and declining property values.

proficiencies.name: Neolithic Fertility Rites
proficiencies.description: These rites increase the likelihood of conceiving.

proficiencies.name: Nettle Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Niffler Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to track, identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Niffler's Fancy Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Nightshade Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Norman Stellar Fortification
proficiencies.description: This practice allows the practitioner themselves to absorb 1H wound of damage of any type without suffering any injuries. If the damage that would have been sustained exceeds 1H wound, the practitioner does take this additional damage. In the event that the damage that would have been sustained is less than 1H wound, the damage may be reduced from this stellar fortification successively until a total of 1H wound of total damage has been reached.

proficiencies.name: Numerological Ciphering
proficiencies.description: When an Arithmancer believes that a seal is numerological in nature, they may attempt this proficiency to crack the numerological code and attempt to break the seal.

proficiencies.name: Numerology for Divination
proficiencies.description: Arranging numbered lots and scribbling rapid-fire sums until a sigil-like total emerges, then reading that value for destiny’s hidden equation allows the diviner to grant +1 to arithmancy checks to their target.
proficiencies.history: Pythagorean adepts in Croton weighed consonants as integers, claiming any name whose factors summed to ten would topple tyrants by year’s end. Late-Republic augurs carved rows of tally cuts on olive wood; a column that completed the Fibonacci step foretold a Senate vote swinging unexpectedly east. Medieval Kabbalists mapped Hebrew verses to prime spirals, timing exiles and festivals by where the spiral first bent. German Renaissance bankers slipped cipher-squares beneath inkblots on bonds, insisting a contract whose digits reduced to six would never default. During the Apollo program, NASA “trajectory witches” checked burn times against digital roots; a mis-rooted nine famously tagged the valve that stuck on Apollo 13.

proficiencies.name: Ogham Stave Writing
proficiencies.description: The practitioner writes these staves in dirt, mud, or sand. This allows the diviner to see future individuals or creatures that may intend to appear in this area in the future. If no dirt, mud, or sand is available, this proficiency cannot be utilized.

proficiencies.name: Oinomancy
proficiencies.description:
The diviner observes the swirls and legs of wine in a goblet. They then can have one of several effects on their target (who must then drink the wine for the effects to take hold):
1. The target takes 1L wound per minute (or one battle turn round) of curse damage
2. 1H wound of any type is healed
3. +6 to the target's next charms cast
4. +6 to the target's next charisma check
proficiencies.history: Homeric seers dripped wine onto sacrificial ash, judging a voyage’s luck by how the crimson threads spread across gray dust. Roman patricians swirled amphora dregs and traced the residue on silver platters, claiming Mars himself steered spill-patterns toward victory or defeat. Monastic vintners of Burgundy tasted cask lees at matins, their whispered notes on sourness or bloom shaping which abbot dared build new cloisters that year. Venetian spice merchants in the Age of Sail cracked resin into goblets: if the bead turned the wine cloudy, they rerouted fleets before storms the Doge’s charts missed. By the Roaring Twenties, speakeasy “sommeliers” read tear-trails on coupe glasses, selling flappers midnight hints of market highs—or Prohibition raids—before ticker tape could whisper.

proficiencies.name: Olmec Era Conjuration
proficiencies.description: This Mayan ritual involves the direct ingestion of Agarin. The result is the conjuration of three sprite-like creatures that each cause 1L wound of cutting damage per attack. Each has a heavy wound capacity of 1. These are conjured within one turn and can begin attacking immediately. They may be reconjured once all initial sprites have died.

proficiencies.name: Olmec Era Possession
proficiencies.description:
This proficiency requires that one have morning glories (or their seeds) in their possession. After consuming the morning glory seeds and waiting 30 minutes, the user conjures an image in their mind of the one they wish to possess. Even over vast distances, they will temporarily possess this being for approximately 5 minutes. The target's sense of self-preservation will still be intact and the bond will break if the target senses they are in grave danger.
Target rolls Mental Fortitude to resist this control.

proficiencies.name: Orchid Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Oregano Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Orkney Rune Channeling
proficiencies.description: By channeling the ancient runes found in Maeshowe and other Neolithic Orkney sites, a practitioner gains +2 to any mental casts.

proficiencies.name: Ornithomancy
proficiencies.description: Watching the looping flight, wing-flicks, and warning cries of wild birds to decode the sky’s verdict on earthbound affairs. This grants the target +3 to perception for the next hour.
proficiencies.history: Etruscan augurs stood on oak platforms at dawn, tracing every raven arc as a rough sketch of next week’s battle lines. Roman legionaries swore by the auspex who halted an entire column after vultures crossed left-to-right—hours later the road was found mined with makeshift spikes. Medieval falconers in Aragón timed royal marriages by how kestrels hovered over the palace chimney; a sudden stoop meant the betrothal would break like brittle bone. During the spice boom, Javanese traders read frigatebird kettles over the strait, rerouting clove junks away from storms Portuguese charts never saw. In the age of telegraph wires, Australian telegraphists judged cockatoo alarm flights; when the flock veered seaward, they cut the line current and saved miles of cable from a lightning surge that hit minutes later.

proficiencies.name: Ovomancy
proficiencies.description: Cracking a fresh egg into cool water and reading the drifting yolk-globes and albumen filaments for omens of birth, shelter, and brittle peril. Doing so grants +1 to naturalism for a target for the next hour.
proficiencies.history: Roman midwives floated eggs in wine, judging a mother’s fate by whether the yolk broke before the first bubble rose. Basque sorginak (“egg-witches”) left sunrise eggs on slate; a curling white thread that pointed uphill foretold the next thunder-witch’s path. Russian Old-Believers candle-read Easter eggs, claiming a dark blood-spot in the air cell warned of tsarist arrests within the week. In coastal Yorùbá towns, fishermen stirred hen eggs into tide-pools; if the yolk coiled clockwise, the surf would spare their canoes but drown rivals. By the Dust Bowl era, Kansas healers dropped farm eggs into Mason jars—clouded whites meant the coming storm would strip topsoil clean, and they moved cattle two counties east.

proficiencies.name: Palmistry
proficiencies.description: Through the use of palmistry, the practitioner may (once per play session) complete a palm reading for another character granting detailed knowledge of their true path. At any point in that play session, the receiver of the reading may reroll one roll of their choosing taking whichever of the two results they prefer.
proficiencies.history: From Vedic sages through Hellenistic mystics to Romani caravans, seers have parsed the palmar creases—life‑line, fate‑line, and mounts of Venus and Luna—to limn temperament, longevity, and the ebb of fortune.

proficiencies.name: Parsley Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Penelope's Welwitschia Extraction Procedures
proficiencies.description:
These oils are exceptionally difficult to extract. The user must boil copious amounts of crystallized potioning water and dip the leaf (while still attached to the plant) in the water. If the water is too hot, it will boil the leaf and all the oils will be lost. If it is too cool, the oils will not secrete and cannot be extracted.
To add to the difficulty, Welwitschia plants have a natural protective response such that if they detect that someone is attempting to harm them, they will stop secreting their oils and begin secreting a mild poisonous gas instead. Thus, critical failures (rolling a 1 on the die) while attempting to extract these oils will, instead result in the extractor being gassed (2L wounds per hour until healed).
The Welwitschia plant will not begin secreting the oils again for another 30 days once its defenses are activated.
This procedure extracts a significant amount of Welwitschia leaf oils through the use of Caster Oil to force a greater amount of the oils out of the plant.

proficiencies.name: Peony Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Peppermint Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Pessomancy
proficiencies.description: Casting multiple pebbles into water while reading their drift and collisions allows the practitioner to heal 1H wound of vital damage.
proficiencies.history: Classical Greek polemarchs rattled black-and-white pebbles in bronze urns, trusting the spill-pattern to pick tomorrow’s march or retreat. Delphic priests later etched cryptic sigils on river stones and tossed them into sacred pools, mapping the ripples to warn of earthquakes days ahead. Byzantine judges kept velvet bags of numbered pebbles; any lot that landed point-up twice foretold riots in the Hippodrome, prompting extra guards. Berber caravan leaders in the Sahara still cast polished agate chips onto goat-hide, their scatter pointing to hidden wells—knowledge French engineers once tried (and failed) to buy. In Prohibition-era Chicago, speakeasy “stone readers” skimmed bar-top gravel into shot glasses; the first pebble to clink against glass walls named which rival joint the police would raid before dawn.

proficiencies.name: Phoenix Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to track, identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Phrenology
proficiencies.description: By reading the ridges and bumps on one's skull, one can gain insights into the target's inner self and strengths. This grants them +5 on their next equanimity, creativity, fortitude, or willpower roll.
proficiencies.history: Late-Georgian doctors in Vienna stroked students’ scalps, declaring bumps behind the ear the surest markers of musical genius—fuel for salons and scandals alike. Victorians carried brass calipers to factories, measuring crown ridges to assign foremen or sack “dishonest” hands before payday. Abolitionists flipped the art on its head, publishing pamphlets whose sketched crania “proved” enslaved Black scholars their moral equals—sending slave-owners into a legislative panic. In Gilded-Age America, showboat mesmerists charged a dime to feel lovers’ heads and predict marital bliss or bankruptcy, turning river towns into carnival clinics. By the 1920s, Parisian detectives catalogued murderers’ skull-charts, cross-referencing lumps with arrest records to forecast the next spree—until statisticians exposed the ruse and the charts slipped quietly into occult backrooms.

proficiencies.name: Phyllorhodomancy
proficiencies.description: By smashing rose petals in a target's hand, you allow them to see more clearly anyone who may have a romantic interest in them. They still must complete a social skills check (headmaster's discretion for the threshold value), but gain +6 to that one specific check roll.
proficiencies.history: Hellenistic hedge-seers plucked fresh rose leaves, striking them against stone; a clear, ringing crack foretold steadfast love, while a dull thud warned of betrayal. Medieval Provençal troubadours carried pressed petals in songbooks, consulting their crispness before daring a serenade beneath castle balconies. In Mughal Lahore, court perfumers floated rose-leaf chains on still pools, reading the drift to schedule treaties—or poisonings—between rival nawabs. Victorian parlor mystics dried petals on silver trays and judged suitors by how quickly each fragment curled in the heat of a candle flame. Wartime resistance cells in Occupied Paris pinned secret orders to rose stems; operatives swore a glance at the bloom’s tilt told whether Gestapo eyes lurked nearby.

proficiencies.name: Pictish Astral Beast Lore
proficiencies.description: Leveraging knowledge of Pictish beast symbols and their relation to star patterns, the practitioner grants all allies +3 to any attacks on any animal or beast (not beings or spirits) for the remainder of combat. Can only be utilized once the creature has attacked.

proficiencies.name: Pictish Constellation Myths
proficiencies.description: This allows the practitioner to grant allies a +4 advantage to any casts of the mental subtype for the remainder of combat (or 3-5 minutes non-combat).

proficiencies.name: Pictish Shield Shattering
proficiencies.description: Practitioners carve Pitish Symbols in a stone and throw it at a magical shield or barrier to attempt to damage or destroy it.

proficiencies.name: Pictish Stone Magic
proficiencies.description:
If any stone is nearby, this practitioner may hold the stone in their hand to determine its cosmic origins (which then can be used to provide a casting boost for the rest of the battle. The player then rolls a 1d4 as the stone tunes into one of the four casting types (Charms, Transfiguration, Dark Arts, or Defense). Whichever number is shown corresponds to which of the four casting types will receive the benefit. Secondly, the practitioner rolls another 1d4 to determine the strength of resonance. 1-4.
Example if the first d4 roll shows a 2, then the stone will provide a Transfiguration boost. If the second d4 shows a 3, the strength of that boost would be +3. Thus, holding the stone would provide a boost of +3 to Transfiguration.
The stone can then be handed to any being (with hands that can hold a rock) and they gain this advantage. The stones resonance will wear off within minutes of the battle ending (or within about 3-5 minutes of non-combat time).

proficiencies.name: Pictish Symbolism
proficiencies.description: By drawing particular Pictish symbols on a surface or book, one can then cast a curse on that symbol (within 3 minutes) and that curse will resonate from this origin point. The higher the cast, the farther and longer that curse will resonate.

proficiencies.name: Political Involvement
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves understanding and appropriately interacting with the muggle political system, helping one advance their political aims.

proficiencies.name: Polypody Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Pop Culture Knowledge
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves the practitioner having detailed knowledge of pop-culture references

proficiencies.name: Poppy Plant Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Potion Detection
proficiencies.description: Allows the practitioner to determine whether a given substance has potions contained within it or is a potion.

proficiencies.name: Primitive Aichmomancy
proficiencies.description: In battle, the practitioner presses small pins gently into a nearby surface watching how the lean and rest. This gives them insights into possible futures which they can share with their allies. This gives any ally a +1 to any combat-related roll for one turn. It wears off when this practitioner's turn reemerges.

proficiencies.name: Primitive Bouddican Battle Tactics
proficiencies.description: This proficiency grants the practitioner +1 to all casting attempts for the duration of battle

proficiencies.name: Primitive Numerological Ward
proficiencies.description: Through the use of specific numerical references contained in the text, this will allow the practitioner to magically seal a letter.

proficiencies.name: Pritcher's Porritch Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Proleptic Gregorian Calendar
proficiencies.description: Through an examination of the events preceding approximately 8 AD, the historian can have a deeper sense of the timeline of events during this period. As such, utilizing this proficiency provides both a +3 bonus to astronomy checks and a +5 bonus to history checks for the next day.

proficiencies.name: Proleptic Julian Calendar
proficiencies.description: Through an examination of the events preceding approximately 8 AD, the historian can have a deeper sense of the timeline of events during this period. As such, utilizing this proficiency provides both a +1 bonus to astronomy checks and a +3 bonus to history checks for the next day.

proficiencies.name: Psilomancy
proficiencies.description: Examining the patterns in shed skin, hair, and dandruff allows the diviner to aid the target in discovering their inner strength. They take +5 to their next strength roll.
proficiencies.history: Babylonian barber-priests swept palace thresholds at dusk, spreading the king’s shed hairs on clay and reading which locks curled toward the rising moon to gauge the monarch’s waning strength. Hippocratic physicians at Kos dried patients’ skin scrapings on linen, claiming the pattern of flakes forecast fevers days before a pulse turned hot. Plague-era apothecaries in Avignon sifted church pew dust, tracing the tiniest blood-tinged specks to chart where the Black Death would bloom next. Edo-period street mystics strung beggars’ fallen hairs on rice paper; if the strands bent as kanji for “rust,” they warned smithies of impending iron shortages. In modern forensic labs, clandestine “strand readers” slip dandruff under polarized light, selling corporations twelve-hour leads on viral outbreaks that could tank their quarterly forecasts.

proficiencies.name: Ptolemaic Calculation of the Synodic Month
proficiencies.description: Through a complex calculation of the exact position of the moon within its monthly cycle, the astronomer can detect ways to minimize the damage friendly humans or creatures take in battle. As such, any friendly creature (including humans) take -1L wound less damage anytime they succumb to damage in a battle for the remainder of this battle.

proficiencies.name: Puffapod Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Pungous Onion Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Pyromancy
proficiencies.description: This practice involves the practitioner holding in their mind a person they know well while staring into the flames of a self-lit fire. The observation allows for long distance communication with that other. However, critical fails cause 1H wound of burn damage to the target.
proficiencies.history: Bronze-Age Hittite diviners scattered sesame oil onto hearth embers, claiming the way each bead flared named tomorrow’s victor or sacrifice. Greek temple attendants at Delphi judged wars by the color tongues dancing atop goat fat, recording crimson forks as Apollo’s nod to bloodshed. Medieval Andalusian alchemists blew glass tubes through charcoal flames, sketching how sparks spiraled to pick safer trade winds across the Strait. Mongol shamans carried travel-altars of yak dung; if the campfire cracked twice before dawn, they rerouted horse herds away from unseen raids. In Gilded-Age Chicago, “fire readers” hired by insurance barons watched warehouse infernos, boasting a glance at collapsing beams could foretell the board’s next hostile takeover.

proficiencies.name: Querimancy
proficiencies.description: Hurling inscribed tokens representing chosen persons against a hard surface, then reading the clatter, rebounds, and resting angles to chart converging reveal elements of the map around them that were previously hidden. This may even include the position of particular creatures or humans.
proficiencies.history: Iron-Age Etruscan judges flicked numbered knucklebones at a slate court-stone; whichever bounced furthest named the litigant whose distant kin would ignite the next feud. Medieval Welsh bards carved hazel chips with clan sigils and hurled them against dolmen pillars, listening for which rune rang out to warn of coming grief. During the Dissolution, recusant nuns near York cast tiny pewter saints at abbey walls; a figure that lodged upright foretold whose secret mass the sheriff would raid. In the Boer War, Cape Town bookies skipped ivory tiles off granite quays, claiming the ricochet path spelled each regiment’s odds of surviving its next sortie. London codebreakers of 1940 kept Bakelite name-tokens in their pockets; a lunchtime flick down a bunker corridor decided which encrypted message hid the imminent raid—and saved whole blocks when the sirens lagged behind the echoes.

proficiencies.name: Questions to the Gods
proficiencies.description: Inscribe Oracle Bones with questions to the gods, heat them, then read the answers in the patterns of cracked bone. As a divination technique, this proficiency can yield visions of direction and guidance from the gods which vary in specificity and accuracy depending on the quality of the roll. This will typically yield information about specific people and their motivations.

proficiencies.name: Queue Etiquette
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves understanding how to stand on line without being detected. Failure results in negative attention and judgement. The greater the failure, the greater the scrutiny.

proficiencies.name: Radiomancy
proficiencies.description: Sweeping a hand-crank tuner across empty bands, parsing crackle harmonics and stray carrier tones to pull auguries from the electromagnetic hush the seer gains the ability to open a long-distance channel of communication to any ally at any distance for a short window of time.
proficiencies.history: Telegraph operators at the close of the 19th century swore that a sudden snow of Morse “sparks” across the sounder foreshadowed coastal squalls hours before barometers twitched. Front-line signallers in 1918 kept pocket crystal sets; a whisper of “whistler” static in the headphones meant incoming shellfire long before the spotters sighted muzzle-flashes. Cold-War hams in Soviet bloc attics tuned number-stations and claimed that an errant burst of “UVB-76” buzz predicted Politburo purges within the week. Caribbean pirate-radio jockeys of the 1980s mixed field recordings of ionospheric hiss; when the pitch dipped a semitone during a set, they cleared the van before the police triangulated their rig. Modern solar-weather analysts still note that a chorus of short-wave crackles nine minutes after a flare can map which satellites will glitch—stock traders quietly pay for that minute-nine spreadsheet.

proficiencies.name: Red Ti Plant Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Refined Aichmomancy
proficiencies.description: In battle, the practitioner presses small pins gently into a nearby surface watching how the lean and rest. This gives them insights into possible futures which they can share with their allies. This gives any ally a +4 to any combat-related roll for one turn. It wears off when this practitioner's turn reemerges.

proficiencies.name: Refined Bouddican Battle Tactics
proficiencies.description: This proficiency grants the practitioner +4 to all casting attempts for the duration of battle

proficiencies.name: Refined Numerological Ward
proficiencies.description: Through the use of specific numerical references contained inside, this will allow the practitioner to magically seal a room.

proficiencies.name: Restaurant Ordering
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves understanding how to order at a restaurant. Failure results in negative attention and judgement. The greater the failure, the greater the scrutiny.

proficiencies.name: Rose Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Rosemary Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Rudimentary Aichmomancy
proficiencies.description: In battle, the practitioner presses small pins gently into a nearby surface watching how the lean and rest. This gives them insights into possible futures which they can share with their allies. This gives any ally a +2 to any combat-related roll for one turn. It wears off when this practitioner's turn reemerges.

proficiencies.name: Rudimentary Bouddican Battle Tactics
proficiencies.description: This proficiency grants the practitioner +2 to all casting attempts for the duration of battle

proficiencies.name: Rudimentary Numerological Ward
proficiencies.description: Through the use of specific numerical references contained in the text, this will allow the practitioner to magically seal a journal or book.

proficiencies.name: Runespoor Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to track, identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Sage Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Saint John's-Wort Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Saxon Navigation
proficiencies.description: When lost, this skill will help one orient themselves to nearby landmarks or potential nearby fruitful directions.

proficiencies.name: Saxon Plant Care
proficiencies.description: By recalling the ancient Druidic rituals of plant care, the practitioner can revive a plant that is dying or ailing.

proficiencies.name: Saxon Runic Starcraft
proficiencies.description:
By interpreting Saxon runes under specific star configurations, the practitioner can provide a +2 bonus to any spells of the Shielding
subtype to any ally for the remainder of combat (3-5 minutes non-combat). Can only be utilized once battle has begun.

proficiencies.name: Saxon Sigilcrafting for Political Disruption
proficiencies.description: These Saxon-based sigils are drawn or painted in specific strategic places around the perimeter of a building, town, or area. In so doing, they slowly and subtly begin to weaken the power of those who are presently in control of the dominion within these perimeters.

proficiencies.name: Saxon Star Lore
proficiencies.description: Can only be used during nighttime. When utilized, any allies gain a +2 advantage to any spell cast for the remainder of combat (or 3-5 minutes non-combat).

proficiencies.name: Sciomancy
proficiencies.description: Casting a single light and reading the stretch, split, and tremor of resulting shadows for secrets cloaked in darkness. This allows one target to gain +3 to any attempts to use the common shielding spell (protego) against a dark art cast.
proficiencies.history: In dusk-flooded Sumer, temple watchers stretched linen cords beside obelisks, claiming the extra inch of shadow presaged either famine or a king’s long reign. Hellenistic philosophers at Eleusis charted “noon-shades” that defied the sun’s zenith, reading the anomaly as Demeter’s whispered price for next spring’s wheat. Umayyad astronomers in Damascus inked moonlit silhouettes across marble courtyards, their star charts laced with footnotes on how a flick of silver darkness foretold plague winds. Edo-period onmyōji trained paper-lantern beams through lattice screens, mapping shifting silhouettes of courtiers to unmask intrigue hours before rumor stirred. In the age of arc lamps, Parisian stage magicians timed spotlight eclipses against backstage walls, swearing the first errant flicker previewed critics’ applause—or derision—before the curtain even rose.

proficiencies.name: Scottish Clan Healing
proficiencies.description: This proficiency increases the effectiveness of any healing attempts (by 1L wound per attempt; whether by spell or proficiency) for the next day.

proficiencies.name: Scurvy Grass Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Shopping Mastery
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves successfully navigating a shopping outing (excluding money exchange). Failure results in negative attention and judgement. The greater the failure, the greater the scrutiny.

proficiencies.name: Shrivelfig Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Small Talk Mastery
proficiencies.description: This proficiency involves understanding how to engage in very basic muggle small talk. Failure results in negative attention and judgement. The greater the failure, the greater the scrutiny.

proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Snowdrop Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Sophisticated Aichmomancy
proficiencies.description: In battle, the practitioner presses small pins gently into a nearby surface watching how the lean and rest. This gives them insights into possible futures which they can share with their allies. This gives any ally a +7 to any combat-related roll for one turn. It wears off when this practitioner's turn reemerges.

proficiencies.name: Sophisticated Boudiccan Battle Tactics
proficiencies.description: This proficiency grants the practitioner +7 to all casting attempts for the duration of battle

proficiencies.name: Sophisticated Numerological Ward
proficiencies.description: Through the use of numerological references contained within, this allows the practitioner to magically seal an area (headmaster's discretion regarding size, however this typically involves multiple addresses and may even include something as large as a town or county).

proficiencies.name: Soy Plant Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Spanish Calculation of the Solar Year
proficiencies.description: This calculation allows the astronomer to provide a +1 bonus to power and a +2 bonus to willpower for the remainder of the day to all friendly creatures (including humans).

proficiencies.name: Spatilomancy
proficiencies.description: Inspecting fresh scat—its shape, spread, and scent—to read the hidden movements of bodies, beasts, and blight. The practitioner grants +2 to any rolls attempting to protect against a creature.
proficiencies.history: Akkadian herdsmen read omen-clumps in goat yards, swearing a crooked spiral foretold jackal raids before nightfall. Roman haruspices extended the art, charting pigeons’ droppings on temple tiles to pick auspicious voting days for rival consuls. Black-Death chirurgeons in Avignon compared the flecks in patients’ stools to predict whose humors would sour by dawn—grim but startlingly accurate. Among San rock trackers of the Kalahari, the morning scatter of gemsbok pellets still guides hunters toward water or away from lions long before prints appear. Modern epidemiologists have quietly revived the craft, mapping sewer-borne traces to spot citywide outbreaks a week before lab tests turn positive.

proficiencies.name: Spattergroit Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts. They'll be able to sanitize areas infected with spattergroit and can identify an outbreak of spattergroit safely without becoming infected themselves.

proficiencies.name: Spell Detection
proficiencies.description: This allows the practitioner to determine if any spells were recently cast in an area.

proficiencies.name: Spell Tracing
proficiencies.description: This allows the practitioner to receive hints about which spells have been recently cast in an area. General information may include the skill or subtype. Rarely will the practitioner receive multiple pieces of information. It may also contain information like the refinement of the cast (as in, how high of a value was the cast).

proficiencies.name: Squill Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Star Grass Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Sugar Shrub Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Sulawesi Cave Painting Ritual
proficiencies.description: By drawing a specific creature in this style (on a tablet or other hard service), you gain temporary (one-time) control of that creature. It will follow the drawer’s directions very closely for a period of about one hour. However, the creature will then be impervious to this ritual for the rest of its life.

proficiencies.name: Sycomancy
proficiencies.description: Inscribing names or symbols on fresh fig leaves and watching how the sap-brown bruises spread and curl to unveil fortune or peril. Any efforts to conceal magic or any enchantments (in the immediate area) with a set value below that of the Sycomancy roll are immediately revealed.
proficiencies.history: Priests of Old Memphis inked petitions onto supple sycomore-fig leaves, declaring that whichever name first puckered beneath the desert sun would draw the gods’ immediate gaze. Hellenistic orchard-keepers on Cyprus pierced green figs with bronze pins; the pattern of milky latex flecks foretold blight, windfall, or brigand raids along the coast. Early Byzantine ascetics floated carved fig chips on cisterns, timing their slow spin to choose which monastery bell should toll at dawn. In medieval Al-Andalus, huerta farmers strung drying leaves along canal walls—the first to crackle warned of frost two nights hence, saving whole groves. Sicilian dockworkers’ wives of the 1880s pinned fresh foliage to shutter slats; if a leaf turned black by moonset, they rerouted contraband before the carabinieri’s lanterns reached the quay.

proficiencies.name: Tarot
proficiencies.description: The art of Tarot is subversive. While ostensibly providing a "reading" to a user, tarot is usually used to gain insights about them. Whilst fanning illustrated trumps and suits into a spread, then parsing upright, reversed, and crossing cards for woven destinies allows the user to gain insights about the individual themselves, their backstory, strengths, weaknesses, motivations, and desires. The target may use willpower, fortitude, or equanimity to resist this incursion into their perspective. However, they take a -7 penalty to any of these characteristic rolls.
proficiencies.history: Renaissance card-makers in 15th-century Milan painted allegorical “trionfi” decks for ducal pageants; court astrologers soon dealt the images to whisper which condottieri would betray their contract next. Enlightenment occultists in Paris—most famously Etteilla—rewrote the suits into an esoteric grammar, reading The Tower’s lightning as a warning of revolution three years before the Bastille fell. Victorian spiritualists carried trimmed Marseille packs to colonial outposts, timing risky railway ventures by whether The Wheel of Fortune landed upright or reversed in the daily spread. In Harlem’s Roaring-Twenties salons, Madam Juno stacked gilded Rider–Waite cards to predict Prohibition raids—her clients claimed the flopped Devil meant “put the gin in the basement, honey.” Modern code-breakers at Bletchley Park flirted with “SIG-INT spreads,” laying encrypted intercepts beside the cards; a string of swords allegedly marked every night the Luftwaffe crossed the Channel.

proficiencies.name: Tasseography
proficiencies.description:
Tasseography involves reading tea leaves. When the practitioner attempts Tasseography, They will see some combination of symbols which the headmaster selects to indicate something about the drinker of the tea. To use tasseography, you must convince another to drink the tea and give you the cup.
Doing this will allow you to read more about their intentions, motivations, needs, sorrows, or preferences.

proficiencies.name: Thaumatagoria Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Thestral Proficiency
proficiencies.description:
Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle and interact with, and care for this creature and know how to extract all relevant parts.
The practitioner cannot see thestrals unless they have seen death, but they will know their location and be able to track them regardless.
Additionally, anyone with this proficiency can ride a thestral and fly on their back.

proficiencies.name: Transit Navigation
proficiencies.description: This proficiency is used any time the individual needs to utilize muggle transit systems. Failure results in ineffectively utilizing the service.

proficiencies.name: Turmeric Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Tzolk'in Calendar
proficiencies.description: Utilization of the Tzolk'in Calendar allows the astronomer to provide +2 bonuses to potions and herbology for the rest of the day.

proficiencies.name: Uromancy
proficiencies.description:
Swirling fresh urine in a clear vessel and parsing its color, clarity, and sediment rings for omens of health and hidden contagion allow the seer to mitigate 1H wound of damage for the next day. Mitigation means that any damage taken above this amount is reduced by 1H wound.
For example: if a damage spell is cast that typically causes 2H wounds, the target would mitigate 1H wound and take 1H wound worth of damage.
proficiencies.history: Babylonian āšipu inspected royal chamber pots at dawn, claiming flecks that floated foretold drought while swift‑sinking grains prophesied war. Hippocratic physicians charted hue and foam in glass beakers, timing fevers by how deep a ring of sediment settled by sunset. Medieval monastic infirmaries hung “wheel of waters” charts beside privy doors; pilgrims queued to have their flask compared and sins prescribed. In Renaissance Prague, alchemist‑doctors swirled urine over silver salts—the first flash of green warned of plague spores days before city bells tolled. By the Jazz Age, bootleg‑clinic diagnosticians in Chicago read cocktail‑yellow samples under neon light, selling whispered alerts of influenza spikes to speakeasies eager to dodge closures.

proficiencies.name: Utilization of the Haab' Calendar
proficiencies.description: Utilization of this calendar allows the astronomer to provide a +1 bonus to Arithmancy and Ancient Runes for the rest of the day.

proficiencies.name: Valerian Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Vasomancy
proficiencies.description: Striking or spinning a vessel and interpreting its tone, wobble, and hairline fractures allow the seer to bestow +2 to alchemy rolls on any target.
proficiencies.history: Old-Kingdom potters at Memphis tapped cooling amphorae and claimed the pitch shift named which Nile bank would flood first. Classical Corinthian merchants weighed wine-jugs on tripods; a jar that groaned before settling foretold storms in the Saronic Gulf, saving many a cargo. Tang-dynasty tea-masters in Chang ’an spun fine porcelain and read the concentric rings left by drying glaze to time imperial edicts—court scribes swore the cups “spoke” a day ahead of the courier. Renaissance apothecaries clinked nested alembics, charting the resonance to predict which tincture would spoil or sparkle, quietly cornering guild markets. By the early electric age, Bohemian glass-blowers etched tuning lines on goblets; a single sung note that cracked the rim meant a war bond should be sold by morning.

proficiencies.name: Vehical Usage
proficiencies.description: This proficiency allows the practitioner to drive a muggle vehicle without the use of magic

proficiencies.name: Venomous Tentacula
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts. The practitioner can safely evade bites from this plant.

proficiencies.name: Vervain Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Wartcap Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Welsh Bardic Charms
proficiencies.description: By playing the Crwth (which must be on hand) in a particular series of notes, one tunes into certain star-songs from Welsh lore. In so doing, this provides a (+4) boost to any charms casts by anyone in the party (or their immediate allies).

proficiencies.name: Welsh Bardic Charms
proficiencies.description: Practitioners recite bardic poetry to gain favor from others.This increases others perceptions of the practitioners intellect and charisma.

proficiencies.name: Welsh Bardic Constellations
proficiencies.description: Drawing inspiration from Welsh bardic tales linked to constellations, this proficiency allows the practitioner to enhance the Charisma and Creativity of allies by +2 found about an hour once per day.

proficiencies.name: Welwitschia Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant. Welwitschia is a particularly delicate plant complicated plant, so this proficiency does not allow you to extract leaf oils from the welwitschia plant.

proficiencies.name: Wessex Warding Stones
proficiencies.description: By carving runes into stones and placing these around an encampment, a location can be concealed from view by anyone outside of the perimeter of the stones. Sound and smell may carry out from the inside of the encampment, however, these will be substantially muted. Only critically successful perception rolls will detect these lingering traces. Finally, muggles will be automatically repelled by the ward and magical folks will tend to unconsciously stop short of the threshold unless they pass the aforementioned perceptual roll.

proficiencies.name: Wiggentree Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Witch's Ganglion Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Woddomancy
proficiencies.description: Entering a deliberate frenzy and gleaning auguries from the first lucid fragments that tear through the howl allows the practitioner to glean weaknesses in a target's psyche rendering them passive for one battle turn round or approximately 5 seconds.
proficiencies.history: — Bronze-Age Thracian maenads bit laurel and spun until speech dissolved into glossolalia; chieftains recorded the first coherent phrase and marched as if Ares had barked the order. In early Saxon courts the “woðe-women” were kept half-starved on henbane: their midnight shrieks, scratched on horn, mapped raids that struck days later. Constantinople’s beggar-saints turned rage into rote—scribes plucked a syllable from each outburst and, stitched together, foresaw which patriarch would fall from favour. During the Black Death, Tuscan hospitals hired “fool listeners” who copied plague-patients’ ravings; a cluster of identical words meant the next parish would need fresh graves by sunrise. By the Jazz Age, Harlem storefront prophets blasted trumpets until breath became broken howls; speakeasy owners swore a recording of those notes let them shutter two hours before the Untouchables kicked in the doors.

proficiencies.name: Wolfsbane Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Xylomancy
proficiencies.description: Dropping snapped twigs and reading the way they settle gives the seer a guide toward powerful creatures or plants.
proficiencies.history: Iron-Age Celts tossed freshly snapped hazel rods onto dew-soaked turf, swearing the angle of the longest stick named the clan a raider would strike before the next moon. In medieval Finland, Sámi noaiddit planted birch splinters in snowdrifts; the first to twist upright pointed toward reindeer herds—or hungry wargs—behind the ridge. Edo-period carpenters in Nara kept cedar shavings in lacquer boxes, consulting their spiral before raising temple beams; a curl that cracked warned of hidden rot aloft. Appalachian dowser-witches scattered dogwood twigs at crossroads, reading the pattern to guide moonshine runners past revenuers. By the Second World War, British code-rangers snapped match-sticks in mess tents: the way fragments aligned under lamplight told whose convoy would meet U-boats first.

proficiencies.name: Yarrow Proficiency
proficiencies.description: Practitioners utilizing this proficiency understand how to identify, safely handle, and care for this plant and know how to extract all relevant parts.

proficiencies.name: Ydromancy
proficiencies.description: Dripping measured water into hollow vessels or fissures, then reading the pitch, pulse, and echo of each drip to map unseen currents above and below. This grants a target +5 to history checks for the next hour.
proficiencies.history: — Hellenistic engineers at Rhodes lowered bronze cups into sacred wells and divined imperial fortunes from the pitch of escaping bubbles. Coptic hermits later ladled Nile seepage onto sun-hot flagstones, judging a village’s moral drift by the direction first vapor tendrils curled. In medieval Novgorod, ice-fishers drilled test holes at dusk; the swirl speed of rising slush allegedly plotted both blizzards and boyar treachery. Ottoman aqueduct-keepers floated orange blossoms down cistern tunnels—where a petal snagged foretold which marble lion-head would crack before spring floods. During the California Gold Rush, Chinese “water sha” prospectors listened at bamboo tubes: a hollow throb in the bedrock named the gulch whose hidden vein would give out next, days before the picks rang dry.

proficiencies.name: Zoomancy
proficiencies.description: Observing sudden shifts in nearby animal calls, posture, or flight grant to the target +3 to any attempts to tame or bond with a creature for the next hour.
proficiencies.history: Mesopotamian pastoralists watched the restless pacing of ewes on windless nights; a single, sudden stillness foretold sand-storms that buried caravans before dawn. Greek hoplites trusted camp dogs whose hackles signaled unseen ambushes better than any scout, and philosophers at the Lyceum formalized charts of “canine augury.” In Song-dynasty China, court astrologers bred crickets behind silk screens, timing edicts by whether the chorus swelled or fell at the banging of the night-watch drum. Renaissance naturalists along the Danube recorded that swallows skimming river foam one hand-width lower than usual meant plague barges were days away—earning them fat stipends from nervous princes. By the era of telegraph wires, frontier rangers in Australia read cockatoo alarm flights to triangulate bush-fires fast enough for towns to douse sparks before telegrams even reached them.

spells.name: Aberto
spells.description: Forces open a door that is barred or locked.

spells.name: Advanced animal-beast switching spell
spells.description: Switches an animal and a beast of any size. You must be able to see both at the same time. No part of the result may be used in potions. Cannot be cast on beings, but can be cast on a small part of a being. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced animal-plant switching spell
spells.description: Switches an animal and a plant of any size. You must be able to see both at the same time. No part of the result may be used in potions. Cannot be cast on beings, but can be cast on a small part of a being. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Animation Spell
spells.description: Advanced Animation Spell. This spell will animate an object giving it three simple commands and two more complex/abstract commands. The headmaster determines what counts as simple, complex or abstract and the headmaster has final say on the behavior of the transfigured object. The transfiguration will wear off after 1 day.

spells.name: Advanced beast-plant switching spell
spells.description: Switches a beast and a plant of any size. You must be able to see both at the same time. No part of the result may be used in potions. Cannot be cast on beings, but can be cast on a small part of a being. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Broom Braking Charm
spells.description: Improves a broom's braking capabilities such that the user can maneuver more effectively. +5 to flying checks when specifically maneuvering around corners or stopping in mid-flight.

spells.name: Advanced Dark Trace Placement
spells.description: Allows one to place a trace on a particular person to detect if they use a specific evil word or phrase (usually a name). The trace cannot be placed on a spell incantation.

spells.name: Advanced Human to Animal Transfiguration
spells.description: Advanced Human to Animal Transfiguration

spells.name: Advanced Human to Object Transfiguration
spells.description: Advanced Human to Object Transfiguration

spells.name: Advanced Human to Plant Transfiguration
spells.description: Advanced Human to Plant Transfiguration

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Animal to Beast Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any animal into any beast regardless of size. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Animal to Object Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any animal into any object regardless of size. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Animal to Plant Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any animal into any plant regardless of size. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Beast to Animal Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any beast into any animal regardless of size. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Beast to Object Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any beast into any object regardless of size.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Beast to Plant Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any beast into any plant regardless of size.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Object to Animal Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any object into any animal regardless of size. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Object to Beast Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any object into any beast regardless of size. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Object to Plant Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any object into any plant regardless of size. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Plant to Animal Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any plant into any animal regardless of size. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Plant to Beast Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any plant into any beast regardless of size. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Interclass Plant to Object Transfiguration
spells.description: Generic transfiguration allowing you to transfigure any plant into any object regardless of size. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Intraclass Switching Spell
spells.description: Switches two objects that are of the same class regardless of their size. You must be able to see both objects at the same time. The resulting objects cannot be used in potions. Cannot be cast on beings, but can be cast on a small part of a being. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Intraclass Transfiguration
spells.description: You may transfigure the target into any other thing of the same class regardless of its size. Does not require switching. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Magical Curse
spells.description: A blunt force crushing spell that causes 1 heavy wound of blunt force damage.

spells.name: Advanced Magical Hex
spells.description: A blunt force crushing spell that causes 1 heavy wound of blunt force damage.

spells.name: Advanced Magical Jinx
spells.description: A blunt force crushing spell that causes 1 heavy wound of blunt force damage.

spells.name: Advanced object-animal switching spell
spells.description: Switches an object and an animal of any size. You must be able to see both at the same time. No part of the result may be used in potions. Cannot be cast on beings, but can be cast on a small part of a being. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced object-beast switching spell
spells.description: Switches an object and a beast of any size. You must be able to see both at the same time. No part of the result may be used in potions. Cannot be cast on beings, but can be cast on a small part of a being. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced object-plant switching spell
spells.description: Switches an object and a plant of any size. You must be able to see both at the same time. No part of the result may be used in potions. Cannot be cast on beings, but can be cast on a small part of a being. This transfiguration will last about 1 hour.

spells.name: Advanced Plant Accelerant
spells.description: Allows one to speed the growth of a plant by 100% on any plant below the level of the cast

spells.name: Advanced Plant Decelerant
spells.description: Allows one to slow the growth of a plant by 100% on any plant below the level of the cast

spells.name: Advanced Self Watering Spell
spells.description: Self waters plants up to every 3 hours. The water source must be nearby.

spells.name: Advanced Self-Stirring Spell
spells.description: Allows a potion to be stirred automatically at least once every 3 hours

spells.name: Advanced Trace Placement
spells.description: Allows one to place a trace on a particular person to detect if they use a specific word or phrase (usually a name). The trace cannot be placed on a spell incantation.

spells.name: Advanced Vacuum Charm
spells.description: Temporarily vacuum up items. Anything smaller than a mug stays inside the wand permanently.

spells.name: Age Line
spells.description: This spell stops any person under an age determined by the caster, from crossing the line. The strength of the ward depends on the caster's roll result.

spells.name: Alarm
spells.description: Creates a silent alarm around a small area so that the original caster is made aware if someone enters the warded area.

spells.name: Alerte Ascendare
spells.description: Shoots the target high into the air. Landing costs one light wound. If the caster critically succeeds and the spell lands on the target (not blocked, countered, dodged) the target takes 1 medium wound of blunt force damage.

spells.name: Animagus
spells.description: THIS SPELL REQUIRES SOME EDITING BECAUSE IT WAS PREVIOUSLY INCONSISTENT WITH LORE.

spells.name: Animal Information Charm
spells.description: Provides the caster with some information about a specific beast, often cryptic.

spells.name: Animal Revivification
spells.description: This spell revives a dead animal (doesn't work on beasts). The animal will be cursed and aggressive for the rest of its reanimated life.

spells.name: Animating Charm
spells.description: Causes an inanimate object to spring to life. The item will act on its own accord and the caster has no control over it. The effects usually last for around 30 seconds at most.
spells.rationale: This spell is a training spell, but also can be used as a distraction.

spells.name: Animation Transmogrification: Water
spells.description:
This spell only works on animations. When cast on an animation, the animation temporarily draws into its body all nearby water (at a fast rate with no capacity limitation). When the animation wears off, all the water will rush out of the animation's body in all directions with significant force.
The size of the animation will depend on the amount of water consumed.

spells.name: Anti-Apparition Ward
spells.description: Prevents Apparition/Disapparition in a small area. The caster can extend this over a larger area but they take a commensurate penalty. If the caster wants to extend this over an area about the size of a room, they take a -5 penalty, a house: -10 penalty. a city block, they take a -15 penalty. a castle or estate: -20 penalty, entire city or town: -25 penalty.
spells.rationale: Dumbledore cast this to prevent apparation from Hogwarts.

spells.name: Anti-Cheating Spell
spells.description: Prevents cheating on an exam

spells.name: Anti-Intruder Jinx
spells.description: Prevents Intruders from entering an area by placing a solid, impenetrable barrier on all entrances.

spells.name: Anti-tickling Spell
spells.description: This spell heals incapacitation the results from a feeling of being tickled (such as by the Titillando or Rictumsempra spells). However, if one has suffered indefinite incapacitation from the Rictumsempra spell, the anti-tickling spell must successfully be cast at a total roll value of 25 or greater four successive times (without fails) with each cast separated by six hours. For this reason, this is usually cast in a hospital setting. If one fails to cure the indefinite Rictumsempra curse, they must start over with their four-casts. One may try infinitely.

spells.name: Antler Jinx
spells.description: Target grows male antlers on their head. Wears off after about one day.

spells.name: Anxiety Reducing Charm
spells.description: Reduces one's anxiety, heals psychological damage (typically caused by spirits) by two light wounds and removes embarrassment. If one is currently unaffected by any psychological damage, the charm will boost one's spirit for the rest of the day by 1 (giving them +1 to their next spell cast).

spells.name: Apparition/Disapparition
spells.description: Allows the caster to transport themselves to/from a specific area they have been before. You can side-along apparate/disapparate by holding onto the apparating witch/wizard. The difficulty increases by 5 for each side-along apparator. One cannot cast apparition on someone else without also apparating themselves. Apparition typically works within an area of about 200 hundred kilometers. That distance can be doubled with rolls of 15+. It can be tripled with rolls of 20+. If the caster is unsuccessful at casting the spell, they are splinched, causes 3 heavy wounds. In the cast of side along apparation, splinching happens to the passanger if the roll+5 isn't achieved. Splinching only affects the caster if the original spell difficulty is not achieved. There is no penalty for casting this silently.
spells.rationale: This spell does not appear in books and is taught by trained instructors.

spells.name: Aqua Eructo
spells.description: This spell is used to create, and control, a jet of clear water from the tip of the wand; it is probably related to Aguamenti. The water must come from a nearby source or the spell will not work.

spells.name: Arrow-shooting Spell
spells.description: Shoots conjured arrows from the caster's wand. Everyone in front of the caster can be hit by these arrows (Protego is permitted). Any who are hit take 1 medium wound.

spells.name: Ascendio
spells.description: Lifts the caster high into the air. If under water, they will rapidly rise to the surface. If on the ground, they will be lifted approximately 10 to 15 feet. If they are actively flying, this simply helps them generate slightly higher altitude. This is one of the charms placed on most standard brooms.

spells.name: Avifors
spells.description: Transforms a very small target into a bird. Wears off after 15 seconds. Many have been injured because their transfiguration wears off mid-flight. Falling damage is possible. You've been warned!

spells.name: Babbling Jinx
spells.description: The target begins to speak in an almost incessant incoherant babble. All spells cast by an afflicted target must be cast as non-verbal

spells.name: Badgering
spells.description: Turns an object into a badger (cannot be used on humans or creatures). The badger has limited abilities and will not fight. The transfiguration will usually wear off after about 30 seconds.

spells.name: Bandage Conjuring Charm
spells.description: Conjures a magical bandage. Applying this bandage will heal 2L wounds per day per bandage (of cut, abrasion, or burn damage). Can be cast in addition to the bandaging charm.

spells.name: Bandaging Charm
spells.description: Conjures a magical bandage that fuses with and is absorbed into the skin. Bandages light wounds of the following types: cuts, abrasions, burns (healing for 1L wound). Can be used in addition to the bandage conjuring charm. Can be cast on the same wound repeatedly (once per hour).

spells.name: Basic Animation Spell
spells.description: Basic Animation Spell. This spell will animate an object giving it three simple commands (at the discretion of the Headmaster). The transfiguration will wear off after 10 minutes.

spells.name: Basic Broom Braking Charm
spells.description: Improves a broom's braking capabilities such that the user can maneuver more effectively. +2 to flying checks when specifically maneuvering around corners or stopping in mid-flight.

spells.name: Basic Dark Trace Placement
spells.description: Allows one to place a trace in a general location (no larger than a few dozen acres) on the use of an evil word (usually a name). The trace cannot be placed on a spell incantation.

spells.name: Basic Human to Animal Transfiguration
spells.description: Basic Human to Animal Transfiguration

spells.name: Basic Human to Object Transfiguration
spells.description: Basic Human to Object Transfiguration

spells.name: Basic Human to Plant Transfiguration
spells.description: Basic Human to Plant Transfiguration

spells.name: Basic Magical Curse
spells.description: Causes 1L wound of Blunt Force Damage

spells.name: Basic Magical Hex
spells.description: Causes 1L wound of Blunt Force Damage

spells.name: Basic Magical Jinx
spells.description: Causes 1L wound of Blunt Force Damage

spells.name: Basic Plant Accelerant
spells.description: Allows one to speed the growth of a plant by 50% on any plant below the level of the cast

spells.name: Basic Plant Decelerant
spells.description: Allows one to slow the growth of a plant by 50% on any plant below the level of the cast

spells.name: Basic Self Watering Spell
spells.description: Self waters plants up to every week. The water source must be nearby.

spells.name: Basic Self-Stirring Spell
spells.description: Allows a potion to be stirred automatically at least weekly

spells.name: Basic Trace Placement
spells.description: Allows one to place a basic trace on a fixed location to determine if a spell of any kind is cast in a particular location.

spells.name: Bat Bogey Hex
spells.description: Causes the mucous in a person's nose to grow to resemble the shape of a bat and attack their face. The attack deals one light wound but can only be cast once on a target (until they are healed). The transfiguration will wear off on its own after about 12 hours.

spells.name: Baubillious
spells.description: Causes white light to shoot from your wand, temporarily blinding anyone looking at your wand when you cast it.

spells.name: Bedazzling Hex
spells.description: Causes the target object to gleam so violently that the targeted object cannot be looked at directly.

spells.name: Bird-Conjuring
spells.description:
Conjures a small flock of birds. This flock can follow the following commands:
1. Swarm (a single target)
2. Signal (as in, fly in a specified pattern in the sky)
3. Attack (causing 2 light wounds of piercing damage)
After the flock completes their command, they disintegrate. You may have them complete their command on delay (of up to 2 minutes). The flock cannot last longer than 2 minutes.

spells.name: Blasting Curse
spells.description: Causes a large uncontrolled explosion to erupt from the caster's wand, the target will be knocked back and take one heavy wound of blunt force damage. Rolls of 1 on the die result in a backfire that injures the caster (causing 1 heavy wound).

spells.name: Bleeding Hex
spells.description: Causes many small cuts to open up on the target's skin, dealing one light wound per round of combat.

spells.name: Blindfold Jinx
spells.description: Causes a blindfold to appear over the target's eyes. It will remain magically bound to the target's eyes for 15 seconds or one battle turn round unless removed by magic. The target takes a -10 to any attempts to cast at a specific target during this time. Area spells or spells on onesself are not affected.
spells.rationale: The penalty is entirely related to being able to see one's target. The rationale for the penalty is simply that the one with a blindfold on cannot see what they are aiming at.

spells.name: Blood Boiling Charm
spells.description: Causes 1 heavy wound of vital damage by increasing the target's blood temperature to an uncomfortable level momentarily. The target will quickly stabilize and experiences no lasting effects. This will revive anyone from Glacius.

spells.name: Bluebell Flame Charm
spells.description: Creates a blue flame which burns without fuel or oxygen. Lasts as long as the caster wishes. Flames cannot cause damage or cook.

spells.name: Bombarda
spells.description: Causes a large sized object to explode (can also explode a hole in a wall) by increasing its internal pressure. Cannot be cast on beings, beasts, or spirits. If a being or beast is damaged by the debris from the explosion, the size of the debris and its proximity should determine the damage that is done. Higher rolls (above 20) produce larger pieces of debris (up to 1 heavy wound of blunt force or gouging damage). Lower rolls (closer to 13) produce smaller wounds from splinters and smaller chunks (cut or piercing damage: 1 medium wound).

spells.name: Brachiobindo
spells.description: Locks the targets arms to their side. Critical successes usually mean that the target usually drops whatever they are holding. Their arms will remain locked at their side for two battle turn rounds (about 10 seconds). This hampers their ability to cast spells causing a -3 penalty to any spell casts.
spells.history: This was an invention of Herpo the Foul as he experimented with body bind curses.

spells.name: Bravery Charm
spells.description: +2 to Fortitude for one minute

spells.name: Broom Mending
spells.description: Fixes broken brooms

spells.name: Bubble Conjuring
spells.description: Creates a stream of floating bubbles which are hard to pop.

spells.name: Bubble-Head Charm
spells.description: Creates a bubble of air which affixes itself to the caster's head as long as they remain underwater. Allows underwater breathing. This transfiguration will last for one hour.

spells.name: Cantis Jinx
spells.description: Causes the victim to burst into song uncontrollably. Spells must be cast as nonverbal.

spells.name: Carpe Retractum
spells.description: Produces a rope of light that pulls the object toward the caster. If the target is fixed in place, the caster will be pulled instead.

spells.name: Cascading Jinx
spells.description: This spell conjures spell energy bounces off of the target dealing one light wound to any in close proximity to the target. The target itself is unaffected if the cast is successful. If the cast is unsuccessful, the target takes one light wound. Duri and Maxima variants of this spell also knock opponents back (repelling). The "Ante" modification does not work on this spell.
spells.rationale: This spell is a quirky one. It's automatically going to do damage to someone because a failed cast still damages the target. But, it has the potential to be a minor area attack since, if successful, it can damage multiple others.

spells.name: Caterwauling Charm
spells.description: Causes a loud noise to echo through an area whenever someone enters the area from the outside.

spells.name: Cauldron to Sieve
spells.description: Empties the contents of a cauldron by temporarily turning it into a sieve. The cauldron will usually revert back to a cauldron, proper, in a matter of seconds.

spells.name: Cave Inimicum
spells.description: Produces an invisible boundary that conceals the caster. To conceal bigger objects, higher checks are required.

spells.name: Cheering Charm
spells.description: Causes the target to become more happy. Persuasion attempts on that target are +1 . If unsuccessful, persuasion attempts on that target are -1. Lasts for about one minute.

spells.name: Circumrota
spells.description: Rotates an object. The caster must maintain focus.

spells.name: Cistem Aperio
spells.description: Opens chests and boxes that are sealed without a lock.

spells.name: Coag
spells.description: Stops bleeding thereby preventing a patient from accruing any more damage over time. Works on wounds of the slashing and gouging types.

spells.name: Colloportus
spells.description: Locks a lock.

spells.name: Colour Change Charm
spells.description: Changes the target's color.

spells.name: Common Shielding Spell
spells.description:
Protego is the general counter spell and the most basic defensive shielding spell. The target of a spell can attempt to cast this as a reaction to any incoming spell.
