spells.name: Upgraded Trace Placement
spells.description: Allows one to place a trace on a particular person to determine any time they cast a specific spell. The target will not be made aware of the trace automatically. The caster must decide which spell they are tracing when they cast the spell. They can only detect spells cast below the skill of their own cast.

spells.name: Urchinifors
spells.description: Transforms the target into a sea urchin (works on any size target). This transfiguration lasts for about 45 seconds. The target can hold their breath underwater for the entire duration of the transfiguration. However, if the transfiguration wears off while underwater, the target must get to the surface quickly or risk taking fatal vital damage.

spells.name: Vacuum Charm
spells.description: Cleans objects by using the wand to suck up dust like a vacuum cleaner. (Nothing larger than dust can be cleaned up using this spell.

spells.name: Ventus
spells.description: Shoots a jet of strong spiralling wind from the tip of the wand obfuscating the caster. All in front of the caster take 2 light wounds of abrasive damage from spraying debris and dust. If the caster gets a critical success, the damage increases to 1 medium wound.

spells.name: Vera Verto
spells.description: Transforms small animals into water goblets. The transfiguration lasts about 1 day.
spells.history: This was specifically created by Minerva McGonagall.

spells.name: Vermiculus
spells.description: Transforms very small objects into worms. This transfiguration for about 30 seconds.

spells.name: Vipera Evanesca
spells.description: Vanishes a snake. Deals two heavy wounds to snakes.

spells.name: Voice Casting
spells.description: Causes the caster's voice to boom loudly such that the voice can be heard over an especially large crowd (for example an entire stadium)

spells.name: Voice Quieting
spells.description: Causes the caster's voice to become very quiet. Incantations can still be as effective as before, but it may be harder for others to hear the incantations. This spell can also reverse the effects of the voice casting charm.

spells.name: Vulnera Sanentur
spells.description: Healing spell that slows blood flow, clears residue and knits wounds. Recover one light wound and stop all damage over time. Works on eruptive/explosive damage. Can only be cast once per injury.

spells.name: Waddiwasi
spells.description: Shoots small, soft masses of whatever the caster desires at the target. Cannot shoot larger or hard objects. Will not cause damage.

spells.name: Wand-Clearing
spells.description: Erases any trace of previous spell casts such that one cannot utilize the spell replay charm.

spells.name: Wand-Extinguishing
spells.description: Reverses the effects of the wand-lighting charm.

spells.name: Wand-Lighting
spells.description: Causes a bright light to emit from the tip of the caster's wand, thereby providing a torch in dark spaces

spells.name: Warming
spells.description: Raises the temperture of the target until they are comfortably warm.

spells.name: Warming Spell
spells.description: In general, this spell causes the internal temperature of an object to increase in temperature by a few degrees. Many cooks love this spell because it helps to ensure the perfect internal temperature for many foods. However, some have discovered that this spell also works as a ward against the Glacius spell and can revive anyone who has been frozen by the glacius spell. Lastly, many have found this spell to be essential for surviving exceptionally harsh cold weather environments.

spells.name: Washing up spell
spells.description: Cleans an object of any non-magical gunk or dirt. Usually instantaneous. Can be used on delicate surfaces.

spells.name: Water Detection Spell
spells.description: Detects whether a liquid is water or not. Also detects whether the water is potable or not.

spells.name: Water-Making Spell
spells.description: Creates water from the tip of the caster's wand. The actual source of the water must be nearby or the spell will not work. This pours out of the wand like a faucet.

spells.name: Weak Evocation
spells.description:
The caster transforms an image into a temporarily animated object, animal, creature or plant (called an animation henceforth). This version of the spell only works on small objects, animals, creatures, plants. The effect lasts for approximately 2 minutes. The animation is non-magical.
If the animation is a plant, it will not work in potions and will have no active effects if ingested or touched. If the animation is an animal or creature, it will only act like a very basic version of the real animal/creature (and can only obey very basic commands).

spells.name: Wizardkind Detection
spells.description: Allows one to determine whether a particular person is a wizard, muggle, squib.

spells.name: Zagovory: Arithmatical Magical Detection
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that detects any magically imbued, magically historical, or magically concealed objects in an area.

spells.name: Zagovory: Banality Transfiguration
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that convincingly transfigures one into a background object. The concealment will last approximately 30 minutes, can be applied to multiple targets at once and be cast instantaneously. Any who witness the transfiguration will be wise to the deception. Those who successfully attempt a perception check with a higher score than the cast of this spell will similarly be aware of the transfiguration.

spells.name: Zagovory: Binding Love
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that binds two individual's love together. When both lovers cast this spell, their love is permanently bound. While they may separate at some point, their love for one another will remain for the rest of their lives. Both casters must cast this spell willingly.

spells.name: Zagovory: Casted Shadows
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that causes any target in the area to see shadows. The caster only needs to roll once to cast the spell. Those who see the shadows will become disoriented and begin to a medium wound of sanity shaking damage for each turn they are affected by the shadows. Those affected by this cast must roll higher than the caster using either intelligence or perception to avoid being affected by the shadows. However, once the affected individual successfully scores higher than the caster of this spell, they are permanently immune to its effects.

spells.name: Zagovory: Concealed Light
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that disguises the location of sources of light.

spells.name: Zagovory: Concealed Squalor
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that conceals the drab nature of one's surroundings, making them seem clearner, more ornate, brighter, and of higher taste. The caster usually casts this prior to one's arrival in an area to conceal the true appearance of a place.

spells.name: Zagovory: Cursed Futures
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that curses one's fortune. Whenever the caster attempts to find love, friendship, or good fortune, they will fail to do so. Most do not know they have had this spell cast upon them. The original caster can instantly remove this curse without effort. However, it can also be broken by even a small act of true selflessness.

spells.name: Zagovory: Detect Danger
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that can be cast in real-time to detect any plant, creature, being, or environmental dangers in the immediate area. The caster can continually cast this as often as they please and will gain awareness of any dangers (as long as those dangers are not concealed by more powerful means).

spells.name: Zagovory: Draw Spirits
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that draws and binds spirits to a location. The spirits can only be cast away temporarily and will eventually return (unless a more powerful counteracting spell is cast on them or the area).

spells.name: Zagovory: Emptiness Transfiguration
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that conceals everything in an area making it appear to be an empty or banal area or room.

spells.name: Zagovory: Find Caster
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that detects the last caster of a spell in an area. The one who recites this incantation will have a momentary glimpse in their mind of the person's present whereabouts. They may only look once.

spells.name: Zagovory: Ghostly Transfiguration
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that transfigures the target into a rather terrifying ghost. The target is then able to use a terror-based attack that causes 1 medium wound per attempt (one target only). The incantation wears off after one hour. The target cannot wand cast while they are a ghost.

spells.name: Zagovory: Infected Object
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that imbues an object with the ability to infect the next person who touches it with an illness. The illness will cause one light wound of damage per day until healed. The incantation takes three hours to complete.

spells.name: Zagovory: Internal Bleeding
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that causes internal bleeding to any targets of the caster's choosing (though they must be visible at the time).

spells.name: Zagovory: Internal Constitution
spells.description: A Zagovory healing incantation. The caster can recite this incantation to gain control over a specific injury inside a specific person. This will allow them to heal one medium wound of blunt force, disease, eruptive, or vital damage per 30 minutes. Once the caster begins, they cannot be interrupted or they will permanently lose contact with the wound and cannot heal it any longer. They must continue to roll successfully to maintain control over the injury. If ever the caster fails a roll, they can never regain control over the injury and cannot heal it any further using thise method.

spells.name: Zagovory: Mend Cuts
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that can heal all wounds of the following types: cut, abrasion, burn.

spells.name: Zagovory: Positive Impressions
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that is cast on a specific area (or room). The incantation takes two hours to cast and the caster cannot be interrupted or they will fail to regain control of the environment for a full day. Once successful, anyone who enters the area will have a positive opinion of the caster unless they intentionally cast a perception check that scores higher than the caster's Mozenhohst score. Those affected by this spell will be friendly and forthcoming with the caster. If they do perceive the deception, they will be angry, but not automatically aggressive.

spells.name: Zagovory: Reveal Deception
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that will reveal anyone who is actively concealing their identity or true intentions. The incantation can also reveal any other forms of subterfuge. The caster will not know what the form or intention of the deception is (or any of the contents of the deceptived information). However, they will be instantly aware that deception has occurred.

spells.name: Zagovory: Shadow Shifting Transfiguration
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that allows multiple individuals to swap their appearances with one another temporarily (lasts about one hour and all must stay in range of one another). The caster can choose any targets they like (so long as they are visible at the time of casting). Any who have swapped appearances will maintain a shadowy, semitranslucent appearance so that it is apparant that they are not who they seem to be. However, their true identity will not be immediately apparent. The incantation can only be used once per day.

spells.name: Zagovory: Siphoning Warmth
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that gradually draws heat away from a space. The heat in the immediate area will drop by about 1 degree (celcius) per half hour. The caster must continue to successfully roll to keep reducing the temperature. If the caster ever fails their roll, they will be unable to gain control of the environment for the rest of the day.

spells.name: Zagovory: Supress Casters
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that suppresses wand casting in an area. All casters in the area take a -5 penalty to all casts while this incantation is in effect. The one who is casting this incantation must continually roll to keep the spell in effect. However, if they lose focus, they can begin the suppression again and it will remain in effect as long as they maintain focus.

spells.name: Zagovory: Swell
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that makes the target appear larger. This has the effect of reducing the likelihood (-5 to any cast attempts) that spells casted at the target will hit.

spells.name: Zagovory: Trace
spells.description: A Zagovory incantation that imbues an object with a trace. The next person to touch the object will have a trace placed on them. Once the person touches the object, the caster will know their exact whereabouts at all times. The Trace will wear off after two days and the victim will be unaware of the trace (provided they do not intentionally attempt to detect magic being placed on the object)

schools.name: Aiccokrea
schools.description:
Overview
Aiccokrea is Central Africa’s most respected wizarding academy. Cut into the sheer cliffs of the Kabobo Massif and linked by vine‑hung bridges to terraces above Lake Tanganyika, the school teaches disciplined wand‑casting anchored in regional ritual practice. Early study is broad—students master protective nkisi enchantments, lunar divination, and ritual herb‑harvest—before narrowing to advanced scholarship in Years 4‑7.
Philosophy
• Nkisi Craft & Defensive Duty – Wizards are guardians; they learn to bind iron, wood, and herb charges into nkisi bundles that protect communities and punish wrongdoing.
•
Sacred‑Mountain & Lake Ethics – The massif’s forests and Tanganyika’s depths are taboo zones; harvesting or creature work follows strict ritual etiquette to keep natural and spirit worlds in balance.
Architecture
Aiccokrea is perched high in the Kabobo Massif’s montane forest, its entire campus built into the canopy of towering red mahogany and African yellow‑wood trees. Three sturdy, railed walkways form concentric rings around the trunks, linked by simple lifts and ladders. Iron‑rune collars secure platforms without harming the living wood, keeping classrooms, dorms, greenhouses, and a small forge deck safely suspended above the forest floor.
Areas Served
Aiccokrea admits students from the DRC, Rwanda, Burundi, Tanzania, Zambia, and Malawi, with a small cohort from wider Central Africa drawn by its reputation for iron‑hard defensive magic and rigorous history scholarship.

schools.name: Beauxbatons
schools.description:
1 ▪ School Introduction
Beauxbatons straddles a limestone saddle high above the spa town of Barèges, its pale galleries half‑grown from warm travertine, half‑carved into the mountain itself. Mist rising from sulphur springs keeps unwary hikers away, while optical veils redirect GPS satellites into harmless loops. The academy’s creed is simple: grâce par le rite – grace through ritual. Students are expected to move as easily between ballroom etiquette and goat‑haunted meadows as between lecture hall and night sky.
2 ▪ Historical Note
Founded in 1465 by Gascon alchemists under the patronage of Queen Eleanor of Navarre, Beauxbatons began as a finishing college for daughters of Pyrenean nobility. Within a century it absorbed Basque sorginak, Catalan herb‑women, and Occitan fire‑keepers, growing into a bilingual stronghold of mountain lore. The school narrowly escaped destruction during the 1609 Labourd witch purges by sheltering inquisitors in its baths – a calculated act of hospitality that secured lasting autonomy.
3 ▪ Academic Structure
Core instruction for every student, all seven years:
Charms
Defense
Transfiguration
History
Social
Electives available from Year 3 onward:
Alchemy · Flying · Muggles · Runes · Divination · Arithmancy · Astronomy
4 ▪ Local Traditions (concise)
Halha — Solstice torch‑runs that draw down the sun for community renewal.
Akelarre — Basque storm‑dance sabbaths led by goat‑spirit mediators.
Trementinaire — Catalan itinerant herbalism; fir‑sap balms and moon‑cut alpine plants.
Eguzkilore — Sun‑thistle amulets nailed to doors to keep spirits counting till dawn.
Dona d’Aigua — Lake‑bound water women; pact magic for healing and rain‑calling.
Basajaun — Forest giant, teacher of grain and iron; warder of flocks.
Fête de l’Ours — Spring bear‑masque where winter’s wildness is hunted, shaved, and danced into civility.

schools.name: Castelobruxo
schools.description:
1. School Introduction
Castelobruxo sits concealed beneath the emerald canopy deep in the Brazilian Rainforest, its towers and courtyards masked by powerful enchantments. To muggle eyes, it's merely an expansive and strictly protected nature reserve, inaccessible and dangerous. In reality, Castelobruxo ranks among the most prestigious magical institutions worldwide, celebrated for its unparalleled mastery of indigenous Amazonian magic, potion-making, and magical creatures.
2. Historical Note
Founded by Amazonian shamans centuries before European contact, Castelobruxo initially relied on intricate rituals involving sacred flutes and chant magic. Wand-casting arrived only in the late 19th century through exchanges with European wizarding schools, creating a blend of indigenous and Western magical techniques that define its curriculum today.
3. Academic Structure
Years 1 Core: Charms, Defense, Potions, Muggles, Transfiguration, History, Creatures
Electives: Alchemy, Runes, Herbology
Years 2–7 Core: Charms, Defense, Potions, Transfiguration, History, Creatures
Electives: Alchemy, Muggles, Runes, Herbology
4. Occult Traditions Summary
• Yurupary: Sacred flute-based initiation cult. Practices rituals that maintain cosmic balance, fertility, and societal order through secret male initiation rites involving sacred flutes.
• Caraíba: Tupinambá shamanic tradition. Shamans mediate with spirits using chants, tobacco-induced trance, and perform ritual dances for healing, warfare, and ceremonial cannibalism.
• Xapiripë: Yanomami spirit lore. Microscopic luminous spirits aiding shamans through hallucinogenic snuff-induced visions; integral to healing, spiritual warfare, and cosmic balance.
• Acaté: Matsés frog ritual. Hunting and medicinal ritual using giant monkey frog toxin to enhance senses, stamina, and spiritual alignment with the jungle.
• Encantado: Dolphin shapeshifter folklore. Shape-shifting river dolphin spirits blending animal and human worlds, interacting mischievously or seductively with humans, embodying enchantment and mystery.
• Kanaimá: Dark jaguar sorcery. Revenge-focused sorcery involving shapeshifting assassins who ritually kill victims, capturing their souls using animal forms and toxic plants.

schools.description: Crucethu is the only major wizarding school in the middle east. It's located around an oasis in the Empty Quarter that is magically concealed from muggles. The university is known for producing many academics and scholars but often fails to produce a large number of graduates with practical skill.

schools.name: Cuamiatte
schools.description:
Cuamiatte School of Magic
Thaba‑Tseka Highlands, Lesotho — Southern Africa’s premier defensive academy
Overview
Perched on a windswept ridge above 2 800 m, Cuamiatte trains witches and wizards to turn mountain, weather, and starlight into living shields. Every student follows the same six‑subject programme—Charms, Defense, Potions, Transfiguration, History, Astronomy—from first incantation to mastery; no electives are offered.
Philosophy — “The Mountain Yields, the Mountain Guards”
•
Endurance First – Spells, brews, and stances are judged by how long they hold under mountain pressure.
•
Weather as Wall – Students learn to bend wind, cloud, and lightning into layered defenses.
•
Stone‑Rooted Focus – Stable footing and slow, precise wand arcs mirror the immovable basalt beneath their feet.
•
Star‑Timed Readiness – All drills follow the high‑altitude star calendar; no shield rises out of rhythm with the sky.
•
History as Warning – Cliff‑painted spirit guardians and past drought sieges are dissected so future defenders act faster and wiser.
Architecture (Concise)
•
Shield Court – an oval, stone‑flagged arena sunk into the ridge for daily dawn Defense bouts.
•
Storm‑Tower Observatory – a basalt spire with a sliding roof for night astronomy and lightning‑rod charm practice.
•
Rondavel Row – thick‑walled stone classrooms and dorms whose outer faces double as test targets for reinforcement charms.
•
Ferment Caves – cool caverns beneath the ridge where long‑brew potions mature in clay amphorae beside drying racks of high‑veld herbs.
Areas Served
Most students come from Lesotho, South Africa’s highland provinces, Eswatini, and southern Mozambique, with smaller cohorts from Zimbabwe and Namibia seeking elite defensive training.

schools.name: Dobastrias
schools.description:
Overview
Dobastrias is nestled into a natural crevasse in Cyprus, its structure uniquely defined by two wings—each carved into either side of the chasm. Primary walkways, suspended along the cliff face, connect the two halves, while a three‑floor castle is accessed via stairs built into these hanging pathways. Every room is integrated directly into the rugged side of the crevasse, emphasizing both the ancient strength of the earth and the school’s seamless union with its dramatic natural setting.
Integrated Eastern Mediterranean Occult Traditions
At Dobastrias, enduring occult traditions guide every aspect of magical education. These interwoven practices, deeply rooted in ancient Eastern Mediterranean heritage, infuse the curriculum with distinctive regional wisdom:
•
Aphrodite’s Veil:Inspired by ancient Cypriot rites dedicated to Aphrodite, this tradition celebrates transformation through beauty and subtle power. It permeates enchantments and charm work, reminding practitioners that allure and grace are potent sources of magic.
•
Oracular Delphic Insight:Drawing on the prophetic legacies of Mediterranean oracles, this tradition sharpens divinatory arts and historical inquiry. It trains students to interpret omens and discern hidden meanings in the natural and supernatural realms.
•
Labyrinthine Defense:Echoing the intricate mazes and fortifications of ancient citadels, Labyrinthine Defense teaches that safety lies in complexity and subtlety. This tradition informs defensive magic with techniques as elusive and robust as a well‑concealed fortress.
•
Celestial Dialogues:Blending astronomical observation with age‑old celestial lore, this practice asserts that the stars themselves are messengers. It underpins both the study of astronomy and the dark, transformative aspects of magical work by linking cosmic patterns with mythic narratives.
•
Pythagorean Harmony:Merging ancient numeric philosophy with mystical speculation, Pythagorean Harmony reveals a hidden order within the universe. This tradition shapes the study of runes and arithmancy, encouraging students to see magic as the language of sacred geometry.
•
Dynastic Enigma:Rooted in the secretive teachings of Eastern Mediterranean mystery schools, Dynastic Enigma challenges students to uncover the occult forces that have influenced the rise and fall of civilizations. It infuses historical inquiry with esoteric insight, exposing the unseen currents that shape magical society.
Together, these integrated traditions ensure that every lesson at Dobastrias is steeped in the rich, multifaceted occult legacy of the region.
Architecture
Dobastrias’ design is a striking fusion of natural topography and deliberate magical architecture. Carved directly into a deep crevasse, the school comprises two distinct wings positioned on each side of the chasm. Suspended walkways cling to the cliff face, providing the only access between the wings and leading to a three‑floor castle reached by staircases along these precarious paths. Every room is sculpted into the rock, blending seamlessly with the ancient landscape and evoking a timeless, almost otherworldly presence.
Areas Served
Dobastrias attracts a diverse student body from across Cyprus and the broader Eastern Mediterranean region. Its unique location and rich occult traditions appeal to young wizards from coastal towns, island communities, and historical centers steeped in myth and mystery.

schools.name: Durmstrang
schools.description:
Durmstrang School Profile
(Hidden along the fog-bound coast of Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, northern Germany)
1 ▪ School Introduction
Durmstrang’s ramparts rise from a wind-scoured headland that Muggles dismiss as a decommissioned U-boat‐testing bunker. Granite bastions, black-iron runic grills, and a labyrinth of sea caves form a single warded complex whose corridors echo with North-Sea surf. From first year onward, every pupil is expected to master hardship: nights on the battlements, winter drills on frozen inlets, and compulsory study of fate-craft that once guided Vikings and Hanseatic sailors alike. Whispered legend says the school’s tide-gates close forever to any student who breaks an oath of kin-loyalty or traffics with powers they cannot control.
2 ▪ Historical Note
Founded c. 1150 CE by east-Saxon rune-smiths and itinerant völur, Durmstrang began as a seasonal long-hall where river pilots learned galdr charms for safe passage through Baltic storms. When Teutonic crusaders drove pagan priests underground, the hall moved north—first to Rügen’s chalk cliffs, then, after the Thirty Years’ War, to its present sea-cliff redoubt. Each migration wove new northern lore into the curriculum: Sámi trance-drumming, Baltic earth-mother rites, and the outlawed art of útiseta grave vigils. Since 1814 the school has kept an uneasy truce with the German Ministry: officials may inspect the outer dock, but never the Rune Vault or the Hall of Sea-Smoke where oaths are bound.
3 ▪ Academic Structure
Durmstrang admits a single cohort of forty students each year. All five courses are mandatory for all seven years; there are no electives or “soft” options.
Years
Core Courses
1 – 7
Charms · Potions · Perception · Transfiguration · Dark Arts
4 ▪ Occult Traditions (concise overview)
Tradition
One-line definition
Cultural practice
Seiðr
Norse fate-weaving sorcery worked in ecstatic trance.
Völur mount a high seat, chant varðlökur, and “tug” new threads into the web of wyrd.
Galdrastafir
Runic sigil-craft that binds spoken galdr to carved staves.
Rune-smiths etch bone or iron and sing the pattern awake for warding or bane.
Noaidivuohta
Sámi drum-led spirit-journey shamanism.
A noaidi’s soul rides drum-beat maps to retrieve lost fortunes or barter with wind-spirits.
Útiseta
“Sitting-out” night vigil upon grave mounds to seek counsel of the dead.
The seeker keeps motionless till dawn, negotiating secrets with land-wights and ancestors.
Ragana
Baltic witch-seeress path of moonlit shape-flight and fate-charm.
Raganos gather on solstice ridges, blessing milk herds or unleashing storms if slighted.

schools.name: Ep Ansonei
schools.description:
Overview
Ep Ansonei stretches along a slow bend of the Pendjari, its bronze‑tiled roofs and stilted decks mirrored in the water below. The timetable is single‑track and craft‑heavy: every student studies Charms, Runes, Transfiguration, Artificing, History, and Creatures for all seven years. No electives are offered; depth and continuity are the hallmarks of an Ep Ansonei education.
Philosophy – The Six Pillars
Pillar
Daily Practice
Vodun Bocio Craft
From year 1, students carve or cast power‑figures (bocio) and learn safe, ethical spirit‑binding.
Fa / Ifá Symbolic Logic
Monthly palm‑nut casts assign new odu patterns that students must encode into protective runes.
Akan & Yoruba Metal Casting
Lost‑wax bronze work under guild rules; finished pieces become wand‑caps, mask fittings, or creature harness rings.
Gelede & Egungun Mask Metamorphosis
Transfiguration is taught through mask‑work and dance, advancing from voice shifts to full body transformations.
Mami Wata River Stewardship
Charms and Creatures lessons are held on floating platforms; students negotiate with river spirits before every field drill.
Adinkra & Nsibidi Visual Language
All crafted objects carry stamped or carved symbols; stroke order and accuracy are examined as closely as wand form.
Architecture
•
Bronze Quadrangle – open‑air casting yards and rune benches encircle a furnace tower; the heart of Artificing and Runes.
•
Mask Hall – a round, sand‑floored theatre hung with Gelede and Egungun masks for Transfiguration drills.
•
River Decks – stilted pavilions that project over the Pendjari, used for Charms practice and Creatures observation.
•
Griot Walk – a shaded colonnade lined with carved story‑posts where History lectures and evening drum cycles take place.
Areas Served
Primary intake comes from Benin, Togo, Ghana, Nigeria’s southwest, Côte d’Ivoire, Burkina Faso, and Niger, with occasional students from the wider Gulf of Guinea who seek West‑African craft magic at its source.

schools.name: Erehnoll
schools.description:
OverviewPerched on a windswept promontory overlooking Galway Bay, Erehnoll specializes in the Healing Arts. Its white‑washed cloisters ring a central sacred spring, where students master herbal, ritual, and charm‑based cures. Under the guidance of An Seachtú Mac mentors and herbal‑lore masters, apprentices combine dawn‑touch healing, Leigheas Luibheanna plant‑work, and Ortha incantations. Graduates emerge as advanced healers, apothecary masters, or scholarly chroniclers of Irish folk medicine—sought across western Ireland and Celtic communities abroad.
Traditions
•
An Seachtú MacSeventh‑son lineages teach the nine‑stroke dawn‑touch cure, channeling innate restorative virtue through ritual contact.
•
Leigheas LuibheannaIntensive herbology seminars cover local luibheanna (plants), harvesting at auspicious times, preparation of poultices, and working with each herb’s life‑force (féar glas).
•
Tobar BeannaitheFieldwork at holy wells trains students in deiseal circuit rounds, clootie‑tying, and using consecrated waters to cure stubborn ailments.
•
OrthaDaily drills in spoken charm‑cures—rhyming incantations for bleeding, fever, and spirit‑blasts—blend Christian and pre‑Christian elements to command unseen forces.
Architecture
•
Spring‑Cloister Atrium: Central colonnade edged by hawthorn festooned with clooties, through which the sacred well flows.
•
Herb‑Terrace Gardens: Layered stone terraces planted with plantain (slanlus), foxglove, and dandelion—each plot marked by ogham‑inscribed stones.
•
Ortha Hall: Vaulted chamber lined with polished iron fonts where students recite incantations over consecrated water.
•
Healing‑Light Gallery: Ribbon of east‑facing archways floods the infirmary and incantation chamber with dawn’s first rays.
Areas ServedCounty Galway; Mayo; Sligo; Leitrim; Donegal; Celtic‑heritage communities in western Scotland and Newfoundland

schools.name: Glacleaqiax
schools.description:
Glacleaqiax School of MagicCarved into the marble heart of Ultimate Esperanza, Patagonia
OverviewGlacleaqiax is a grand wizarding academy set within an immense Patagonian mountain. Its campus winds through ancient marble caves, where shadowed corridors and vaulted chambers echo centuries of lore. Dramatic outcroppings of the castle burst from the rock, most notably the world-renowned astronomy tower crowning the peak and gazing into the vast southern skies. In Years 1–2, every student is introduced to a core curriculum comprising Flying, Perception, History, Social Skills, Astronomy, and Creatures. From Years 3–7, the focus shifts to core courses in Perception, History, Social Skills, Astronomy, and Creatures, with electives available in Charms, Defense, Potions, Flying, and Transfiguration.
Traditions
Mapuche Cosmovision:Celebrates the deep interconnectedness of community, nature, and ancestral spirit. Its ancient narratives shape History courses and inform ritual gestures in advanced magical theory, ensuring that each lesson in History resonates with the wisdom of the Mapuche.
Tehuelche Celestial Lore:Honors the cosmic dance of the southern skies, where every star is believed to guide destiny. This tradition is the bedrock of Astronomy instruction—linking celestial events to historical cycles—and enriches History with cosmic symbolism.
Marble Echo Mysticism:Rooted in reverence for the marble caves that define the campus, this tradition sees stone as the silent keeper of memory. It informs History through study of ancient inscriptions and lends a mystical perspective to Creature studies, revealing how the local fauna embody the mountain’s storied spirit.
Auroral Alchemy:Harnesses the transformative energy of natural minerals and breathtaking auroras. Its principles underpin both Potions and Transfiguration, teaching that change and refinement are as intrinsic as the shifting light across the mountain peaks.
Granite Sentinel Doctrine:Draws inspiration from the mountain’s unyielding granite to forge powerful defenses. Its tenets guide Defense curriculum, instilling resilience and mastery over protective enchantments that mirror the enduring strength of Patagonia’s rugged landscape.
ArchitectureGlacleaqiax’s campus is a spellbinding blend of natural wonder and deliberate design. The majority of the school is hewn directly into the ancient marble of Ultimate Esperanza, with hidden corridors and vast, vaulted chambers that preserve the lore of millennia. Strategic outcroppings allow natural light to illuminate select halls, culminating in the breathtaking astronomy tower that crowns the mountain’s peak—a beacon that invites students to unlock the secrets of the cosmos.
Areas ServedGlacleaqiax proudly serves a diverse community drawn from across Patagonia and southern South America. Its students hail from indigenous communities, remote frontier settlements, and modern urban centers, each united by a deep reverence for the mountain’s ancient magic and the celestial tapestry overhead.

schools.description: Hogwarts is one of the oldest and most prestigious schools in the wizarding world. The school is located in the Scottish Highlands and is known for producing well-rounded graduates.

schools.name: Iasivia Iebles
schools.description:
Iasivia IeblesNestled along the edge of the Bolivian Salt Flats
OverviewIasivia Iebles is a singular institution renowned for its intensive focus on divination. Set against the vast, shimmering expanse of the Bolivian Salt Flats—where the reflective surfaces are believed to enhance magical sight—the school offers a seven‑year curriculum covering Charms, Potions, Alchemy, Divination, Transfiguration, Herbology, and History. In this environment, every lesson is steeped in the belief that the land itself reveals hidden truths.
Traditions
•
Salar Vision:This tradition draws inspiration from the salt flats’ mirror‑like surfaces, which are said to sharpen sight and awaken intuitive insight. Salar Vision not only underpins the Divination course but also influences Charms and Transfiguration by emphasizing clarity and reflection in magic.
•
Andean Spirit Weave:Rooted in indigenous Andean practices, Andean Spirit Weave celebrates the intimate connection between the people and the natural world. It guides Herbology and History by preserving ancient lore and infusing Potions with the rhythm of local herbal life.
•
Crystalline Alchemy:Inspired by the brilliant mineral formations interlaced among the salt deposits, Crystalline Alchemy teaches the art of transformation. This tradition is key to both Alchemy and Transfiguration, highlighting how raw elements may be refined into magical substances.
•
Salt Seer’s Legacy:A divinatory art unique to these salt flats, Salt Seer’s Legacy uses the reflective quality of salt to foretell the future. Its influence is most potent in Divination, while also lending its perspective to the practice of Charms.
ArchitectureThe campus of Iasivia Iebles is a study in contrasts—its structures echo the otherworldly beauty of the salt flats. Salt-infused stone walls refract sunlight into dazzling patterns across vast open courtyards and meditative terraces. Designed to encourage visionary contemplation, the school’s halls feature large reflective surfaces that enhance divinatory practice. The use of natural light, together with carefully positioned alcoves and open solariums, creates an environment where magic and the landscape blend seamlessly.
Areas ServedIasivia Iebles welcomes students from all over Bolivia and from the surrounding Andean regions. Its diverse community includes inhabitants of highland villages, urban centers, and indigenous groups—all united by a shared belief in the power of the salt flats to reveal hidden truths.

schools.name: Ilvermorny
schools.description:
OverviewIlvermorny perches on Mount Greylock’s western slope as North America’s most prestigious wizarding school. Its red‑brick spires and timbered wings overlook forested ridges and hidden stone rings. By day, pupils perfect high‑altitude flight along cliffside updrafts and brew rare elixirs in sunlit amphitheaters; by night, they bury Witch Bottles in the Cauldron Atrium, chart ley lines in the Dowsing Fields, or convene Séances in the Spirit Chamber. Graduates serve as diplomatic envoys, frontier defense captains, and scholars across the United States, Canada, and Greenland.
Occult Traditions
•
Witch Bottle CraftingWorkshops on filling, consecrating, and burying pin‑and‑urine vessels beneath hearths or thresholds to trap and neutralize hostile magic.
•
Water WitchingDivinatory practice with forked witch‑hazel rods, mapping hidden springs and ley lines to harness subterranean flows in magical work.
•
Planting by the SignsAlmanac‑guided timing of herb gathering and spellcasting according to lunar phases and zodiac positions for optimal potency.
•
Treasure‑Hunting RitesMidnight excavation ceremonies employing silent triangles, protective magic circles, and seer‑stone divination to locate buried troves and their guardians.
•
Spiritualist SéancesCandlelit mediumship circles using table‑rapping and automatic writing to commune with departed spirits and glean otherworldly insight.
•
Stone Circle ObservancesSeasonal gatherings at ancient ring sites where students renew land‑magic alignments through chant, offering, and geomantic patterning.
Architecture
•
Cauldron Atrium: Open‑air courtyard with a central bronze cauldron, ringed by consecration stones for Witch Bottle workshops.
•
Stone Circle Hall: Subterranean gallery displaying campus‑found megaliths and hosting seasonal Stone Circle Observances.
•
Dowsing Fields: Terraced lawns marked with granite monoliths, each calibrated for rod‑guided divination and subterranean mapping.
•
Almanac Library: Light‑filled reading room housing regional almanacs for “Planting by the Signs” research.
•
Treasure Vault: Secure chamber beneath the west wing used to stage and study Treasure‑Hunting Rite reconstructions.
•
Spirit Chamber: Candlelit round hall equipped for Spiritualist Séances, with acoustic enhancements for spirit‑knock phenomena.
Areas ServedUnited States; Canada; Greenland

schools.name: Insosojae
schools.description:
Overview
Insosojae is uniquely modern in its presentation: its campus lies in a nondescript office building in downtown Geoje, South Korea. Wizards enter discreetly through a muggle shop at street level, then simply take the elevator to reach the academic floors above. The setting is practical and unadorned—reflecting a deliberate fusion of contemporary urban life with a hidden, scholarly magical community.
Integrated East Asian Occult Traditions
At Insosojae, a set of interwoven traditions shapes the entire educational experience—ensuring that every subject, from Charms to History, is enriched by authentic East Asian occult heritage:
•
Munjado Wisdom:Drawing on the ancient art of calligraphy and talismanic inscription from the Joseon Dynasty, Munjado Wisdom teaches that every carefully crafted symbol holds latent cosmic energy. This tradition infuses spellcasting with both aesthetic beauty and precise intent.
•
Dangun Protection:Inspired by the mythic narratives and ritual practices surrounding Dangun, the legendary founder of Korea, Dangun Protection emphasizes defensive magic grounded in ancestral rites. It advocates a measured, resilient approach to warding and safeguarding that permeates every defensive spell.
•
Veiled Accord:Rooted in the discreet scholarly circles of late Joseon society, Veiled Accord champions the art of engaging with the non‑magical world. It teaches wizards to observe, integrate, and interact with muggle culture subtly, preserving wizarding secrets while enhancing cultural understanding.
•
Saju Divinatory Arts:Based on the long‑standing practice of Saju (the Four Pillars of Destiny) and related prognostic techniques, Saju Divinatory Arts empowers students to interpret omens and natural signs with disciplined insight, revealing the interplay of fate and free will.
•
Artisanal Enchantment:Reflecting the legacy of hanji craftsmanship and celadon pottery, Artisanal Enchantment blends traditional materials with modern innovation. This tradition drives the creation of magical objects that are both functional and artistically significant, rooted in centuries‑old artisan skills.
•
Dynastic Memory:Grounded in the meticulous historical records of East Asian dynasties, Dynastic Memory calls on students to critically engage with primary texts, oral traditions, and relics. This tradition underpins the History curriculum, instilling a deep respect for the legacies that continue to influence contemporary wizardry.
Together, these traditions provide a cohesive, historically informed framework that enriches every aspect of Insosojae’s curriculum.
Architecture
Insosojae’s campus is refreshingly straightforward—a modern, unembellished office building in downtown Geoje. There is no ostentation in its exterior or layout; the building functions as a discreet hub for wizarding education amidst the urban hustle. Once inside, students take a standard elevator to ascend to the academic floors where classrooms, libraries, and laboratories are thoughtfully arranged. The design is purely functional, emphasizing efficiency and inconspicuousness while still accommodating the magical needs of its occupants through subtle, inbuilt enchantments.
Areas Served
Insosojae serves a diverse student body from across South Korea and neighboring East Asian regions. The school draws aspiring wizards from urban centers, traditional academies, and those keen to bridge the worlds of modern society and ancient magical practice.

schools.name: Kliestiepappio
schools.description:
Overview
Kliestiepappio sits majestically atop Brokopondo Reservoir in Suriname, with wooden bridges spanning its waters to connect various buildings and repurposed barges. Primarily serving students from the Caribbean and the Guianas, the campus is renowned for its vivid integration of natural water elements and culturally steeped traditions. Uniquely, it is one of the few schools worldwide that teaches the Dark Arts alongside traditional wand casting. The setting itself inspires a curriculum where local spirit lore informs every spell and flight.
Traditions
Winti:The Afro‑Surinamese spiritual tradition emphasizing community rituals, ancestral veneration, secret chants, and healing ceremonies. Its influence is woven into Charms through traditional incantations, Creature studies via ancestral spirit lore, and Defense classes with protective warding spells drawn from Winti rites.
Arawak Spirit Lore:Indigenous mysticism that honors nature’s rhythms, transformation cycles, and divination through earth and water. This tradition enriches Transfiguration with motifs of natural metamorphosis, inspires Flying with lessons on wind and water currents, and guides Creature studies by interpreting native fauna through intuitive, earth-bound divination.
Kanaima Binding:A controlled practice of dark magic rooted in regional lore, teaching ethical restraint and the transformation of shadow energy. Kanaima principles are central to the Dark Arts—where structured shadow-binding techniques are mastered—and inform Defense classes with countermeasures grounded in ethical applications of dark magic.
Maroon Mysticism:A syncretic tradition from Maroon communities that emphasizes resilience, communal strength, and transformative renewal. This tradition infuses Charms with empowering oral traditions, guides Transfiguration with themes of renewal and identity, informs Potions through local herbal lore, and shapes Defense strategies inspired by Maroon protective practices.
Architecture
Kliestiepappio’s campus embodies a harmonious blend of natural design and cultural storytelling. Wooden bridges suspended gracefully over the waters of Brokopondo Reservoir serve not only as functional connections between various buildings but also as symbolic links between the realms of tradition. Repurposed barges host classrooms and specialized practice areas, creating intimate environments that echo the fluidity of water and the ever-changing nature of magic. At the heart of the campus stands the Ancestral Pavilion—a central gathering hall, richly adorned with carvings and motifs drawn from Winti and Arawak iconography, where communal rites and scholarly exchanges are held under open skies.
Areas Served
Kliestiepappio primarily caters to students from the Caribbean Islands and the Guianas. This diverse student body is deeply connected to the local occult heritage and regional cultural practices, ensuring that the school remains a vibrant crossroads of magical traditions from across the region.

schools.name: Koldovstoretz
schools.description:
OverviewKoldovstoretz floats atop the frozen expanse of Lake Ladoga, its ice‑carved citadel concealed by ancient spells of concealment and illusion. Founded in the 17th century by exiled court magicians, it blends Russian baroque architecture with frost‑woven embellishments. Students arrive by enchanted ice‑skates or sleigh, passing under onion‑domed towers crowned with crystalline spires. Renowned for its mastery of elemental and frost magic, Koldovstoretz draws the brightest from Russia, Eastern Europe, Scandinavia, and beyond—competing fiercely with Dobastrias to shape the continent’s next generation of sorcerers.
Occult Traditions
•
Domovoi PactBinding household guardian spirits through hearth‑rite offerings and whispered oaths—protecting concealed campuses and familiars.
•
Perun’s Storm RiteStorm‑summoning ceremonies invoking lightning‑charm sequences at midwinter, harnessing Perun’s power for defensive and offensive wards.
•
Koldun BindingFrost‑craft rituals that lock ambient cold into talismans and armor—tempering ice‑forged charms capable of shattering enchanted barriers.
•
Water LibationsSeasonal offerings cast onto the ice in silver bowls to placate lake spirits and ensure safe passage for psychopomp processions and supply convoys.
•
Runic SigilcraftInscribing pre‑Christian Slavic runes on metal, wood, or ice to amplify spellwork—teaching students to merge tradition with modern arcane theory.
•
Psychopomp PassageNocturnal lantern rites guiding ancestral spirits across the frozen lake—training in soul‑navigation and protective escort enchantments.
Architecture
•
Frostspire Turrets: Conical ice‑latticed towers that refract northern lights into the Great Hall by night.
•
Hearthway Gallery: A vaulted corridor lined with eternal braziers—home to Domovoi alcoves and workshop for pact‑binding ceremonies.
•
Storm Atrium: Open‑roof courtyard ringed by storm‑bells and lightning rods used in Perun’s Rite and elemental trials.
•
Icebridge Causeways: Floating crystal pathways connecting lecture halls to living quarters—dissipating in spring and reforged each winter.
Areas ServedRussia; Ukraine; Belarus; Baltic States; Finland; Scandinavia; broader Eastern European magical communities

schools.name: Li De
schools.description:
OverviewPerched on a windswept ridge above the Yarlung Tsangpo valley, Li De draws on millennia of Tibetan artisanship to forge wards and talismans of unmatched potency. Its granite‑walled workshops overlook prayer‑flag‑draped passes, while resident ngakpa lineages tutor students in both hands‑on crafting and visionary ritual. Renowned across the Himalaya for protective amulets that safeguard high‑altitude caravans, Li De blends mountain‑born endurance with subtle‑body alchemy.
Traditions
•
Mdos / NamkhaDaily weaving of colored‑yarn thread‑crosses (namkha) instills cosmic harmony and precision in every knot, anchoring protective spells in material form.
•
GludCrafting and ceremonially dispatching effigy replicas teaches the art of spiritual reciprocity—students learn how symbolic “ransom” offerings restore balance to a stolen or fragmented soul.
•
TummoEsoteric inner‑fire yoga breath‑work channels geothermal heat into the body, empowering students to temper metals and infuse wards with living heat‑energy.
•
Tulpa / Sprul‑paSustained visualization exercises train adepts to generate thought‑forms that animate talismans, sharpening mental focus and intentionality in every artifact.
•
Ro‑LangsCorpse‑binding drills mimic the stiff‑jointed revenants of rural lore, equipping novices with the mantra‑infused techniques needed to sculpt and seal undead‑warding charms.
Architecture
•
Rock‑hewn Atelier: Four cliff‑side workspaces carved into rose‑tinted granite, each crowned by a suspended namkha mobile.
•
Geothermal Furnace Hall: A domed chamber fed by subterranean hot springs, where Tummo‑heated forges temper metal wards.
•
Mandala Skylight: Central courtyard ringed by stained‑glass panels depicting Sprul‑pa mandalas, channeling sunlight onto the crafting benches.
•
Wardstone Façade: Fortified gate flanked by colossal Ro‑Langs‑warding statues—stone effigies imbued with sealing mantras.
Areas ServedTibet Autonomous Region (China); western Sichuan highlands; northern Bhutan; Nepalese trans‑Himalayan valleys; northern Indian Himalayan foothills

schools.name: Magoutokoro
schools.description:
1. School Introduction
Hidden atop the volcanic spire of Minami Iwo Jima, Magoutokoro appears to outsiders as a weather‑beaten radar station left over from Cold‑War war‑games. In truth, lacquered halls grow straight from the island’s basalt, roofed in storm‑hardened ryū‑tiles that glint only for those born to magic. The school’s ethos is strict: knowledge must serve balance. Students whose work tips toward domination simply stop receiving instruction—the sea fog closes, and their names are struck from the rolls.
2. Historical Note
Founded late in the Heian era (c. 1100 CE) by court onmyōji fleeing factional plots, Magoutokoro soon absorbed methods from yamabushi mountain monks and seafaring mediums of the southern islands. During the Meiji reforms the staff refused Ministry oversight, protecting its composite traditions by cloaking the island inside military exclusion zones. Only in 1920 did Magoutokoro accept delegates to the International Confederation, on the condition that its curriculum remain untouched.
3. Academic Structure
No electives; every pupil masters the full course load.
Years
Core Courses
1–7
Defense · Potions · Flying (no set text) · Divination · Dark Arts · Herbology · Arithmancy · History
4. Local Magical Traditions (concise notes)
• Onmyōdō — Yin–Yang diagnostics; five‑phase talismans; shikigami deployment.
• Shugendō — Ascetic mountain practice; waterfall austerities; tengu bargaining.
• Yuta — Ryukyuan trance‑mediumship; ancestor negotiation; tide‑based augury.
• Iyomante — Ainu bear‑sending ceremony; reciprocity with hunting deities.
• Wújiào — Classical Chinese wu‑dance possession; thunder‑drum exorcism.
• Tengrism — Steppe sky‑shamanism; weather petitions; soul‑retrieval rides.
• Makåna — Chamorro cave‑herb sorcery; skull‑oracle healing; banyan pacts.

schools.name: Matureivaveo
schools.description:
Overview
Matureivaveo School of Magic, nestled within the lush, hidden heart of Matureivaveo Atoll, serves as the primary wizarding institution in the vast Oceanic region. Renowned for its specialized expertise in aquatic magic, Matureivaveo uniquely emphasizes the magical control and conservation of marine creatures. Students develop profound skills in marine-related Transfiguration and become experts in handling magical sea beasts. Unlike many institutions, Matureivaveo openly provides structured training in the Dark Arts—specifically targeting disciplined control of dangerous aquatic creatures.
Students study three core subjects annually: Transfiguration, Dark Arts, and Magical Creatures, supplemented each year by electives chosen from Charms, Defense, Divination, and History.
Philosophy
The philosophy of Matureivaveo is guided by three interconnected traditions:
•
Marine Animism and Totemism:Students develop magical bonds with marine life and oceanic spirits, viewing each creature as spiritually significant. This partnership fosters respect, spiritual responsibility, and mastery over marine magical phenomena.
•
Weather Magic and Ritual Control:Ritual discipline is central to Matureivaveo, where precise chants, rites, and ceremonies allow disciplined practitioners to shape, influence, and interpret weather and oceanic conditions, respecting the power and unpredictability of the elements.
•
Tattooing and Symbolic Ritualism:Magical tattoos mark the personal journey and achievements of each student, symbolizing their growth in mana and magical capability. Tattoo rituals reinforce identity, spiritual discipline, protection, and magical specialization, guiding students’ spiritual paths.
Through these guiding beliefs, Matureivaveo teaches that power and harmony arise from discipline, respect for the sacred ocean, and deep spiritual symbolism.
Architecture
Matureivaveo’s campus lies entirely beneath the ocean surface at the heart of the Matureivaveo Atoll, protected by powerful enchantments that repel seawater, creating expansive, breathable magical domes and chambers. Structures are crafted from polished coral stone, iridescent shells, and luminescent aquatic crystals, seamlessly integrated with the surrounding oceanic landscape. Transparent magical barriers provide breathtaking views of the surrounding marine life, reefs, and ocean currents.
Students traverse elegant underwater corridors and spacious open halls illuminated by bioluminescent marine flora, carved with intricate symbolic reliefs depicting sacred marine totems, ritualistic tattoos, and weather symbols. Central ritual chambers open visually onto the vibrant ocean environment, offering constant spiritual connection and reinforcing the school’s philosophy of disciplined communion with marine magic.
Areas Served
Matureivaveo primarily educates students from French Polynesia, Samoa, Tonga, Fiji, Hawaii, and Micronesia, extending its influence throughout Oceania. Occasionally, it attracts students from other regions drawn to its unique focus on oceanic and marine-based magical disciplines.

schools.name: Ojaprui
schools.description:
Overview
Ojaprui is perched high on Lanpo Peak, Nepal, and stands as a beacon of scholarly pursuit in the mystical heights of the Himalayas. The school’s isolated, rugged location inspires students to harness the elemental power of the mountains. Its all‑year core curriculum focuses on Potions, Alchemy, Dark Arts, Herbology, and Artificing, demanding not only academic rigor but also an intimate connection with the environment and ancient Himalayan wisdom.
Traditions
•
Himalayan Resonance:Emphasizes the enduring power of the mountain spirit and the quiet, meditative energy of high altitudes. This tradition guides Artificing as students learn to infuse crafted objects with the elemental force of the peak.
•
Sanskrit Elemental Lore:Draws on centuries‑old rituals and texts from the Himalayan region. It informs both Potions and Herbology by teaching the importance of rare, high‑altitude herbs and mineral waters in magical transformations.
•
Mystic Fire of the Dark Arts:Focuses on harnessing controlled, transformative energy—a tradition rooted in ancient practices that both warn and empower. Its disciplined approach shapes Dark Arts, balancing potential destruction with ethical control.
•
Transmutative Wisdom:Celebrates the ancient art of turning the mundane into the sublime. This tradition is central to Alchemy, where students learn to channel the latent energy of the earth into potent magical transmutations.
Architecture
Ojaprui’s campus is an architectural marvel that harmonizes with its Himalayan surroundings. Carved from local stone and accented with intricate woodwork and prayer flags, the buildings blend traditional Nepalese design with modern magical engineering. High‑arched corridors, open courtyards, and fortified laboratories are nestled along cliffside ledges, while a central meditation hall overlooks the vast expanse below—inviting students to reflect, study, and draw inspiration directly from the mountain’s elemental force.
Areas Served
Ojaprui primarily serves students from Nepal and the greater Himalayan region, including parts of northern India, Bhutan, and Tibet. Its diverse student body is united by a deep respect for the region’s ancient traditions and the formidable power of the high peaks.

schools.name: Raillebron
schools.description:
Overview
Raillebron is a secluded and disciplined magical institution located deep within the untamed wilderness of southwest Tasmania. Known for its strict approach to magical instruction, Raillebron teaches a specialized method called Dreamcasting, a powerful form of spellcraft harnessed through varying degrees of consciousness—from deep dreaming to sharp focus. Students master rigorous magical traditions that emphasize precision, discipline, and profound respect for ancestral knowledge. Unlike other magical academies, Raillebron offers no electives; instead, every student commits to mastering five carefully selected core disciplines: Charms, Transfiguration, Defense, Dark Arts, and Runes.
Philosophy
The core philosophy of Raillebron stresses magical discipline, self-control, and strength through mental focus. Magic is taught not as an art, but as a solemn responsibility. Students are expected to develop exceptional mental discipline, seamlessly transitioning between states of consciousness necessary for Dreamcasting:
•
Dream Casting: Deep unconscious state yielding high magical potency but minimal situational awareness.
•
Trance Casting: Semi-conscious, blending moderate power with reasonable awareness.
•
Focus Casting: Fully alert, allowing precise but lower-powered magic.
Dark Arts are uniquely embraced but strictly regulated, taught to cultivate a profound understanding of ethical responsibility and the consequences of magical actions. Raillebron's graduates are known for exceptional discipline, control, and mastery in intense magical situations.
Architecture
The architecture of Raillebron embodies strength and solemnity. Buildings are constructed primarily of solid, weathered stone, reinforced by magically preserved thatched roofs. Structures are sturdy, austere, and imposing, blending seamlessly with the rugged landscape. Walls and pathways bear intricate rune carvings and symbolic designs that not only reflect magical traditions but serve as guides for navigating the intentionally disorienting layout of the campus. Low magical illumination and narrow corridors encourage introspection, concentration, and discipline among students and staff alike.
Areas Served
Raillebron primarily serves magical students from mainland Australia and New Zealand. Admissions are intentionally selective and limited, favoring disciplined students who demonstrate exceptional potential and commitment to Raillebron's stringent educational standards. Due to the institution's rigorous and specialized approach, it attracts a smaller, highly dedicated student body rather than seeking broad geographical representation.

schools.name: Uagadou
schools.description:
Uagadou School of Magic
Location: Mountains of the Moon, Rwenzori Range, UgandaFounded: Antiquity (pre-dates written records)Specialization: Spirit Mediumship, Divination, Ancestral Magic, and Nature Invocation
Overview
Hidden high among the peaks of the Rwenzori Mountains—concealed by perpetual mists and magical enchantments—Uagadou stands as Africa's largest, most prestigious wizarding institution. Known to wizards as the genuine source of the Nile River, its precise location remains obscured to non-magical observers. Before adopting wand use in the early 20th century, Uagadou’s magic thrived through sophisticated systems of gesture, incantation, and spirit invocation, deeply influenced by ancient initiatory traditions.
Core Philosophies and Magical Culture
Uagadou’s magical education is rooted in profound respect for the intricate relationships between humanity, nature, and spirits. Drawing inspiration from regional esoteric traditions—such as the Bacwezi initiations, Balubaale pantheon worship, and Bakonzo mountain veneration—the curriculum emphasizes understanding and negotiating harmony with unseen forces. Magic at Uagadou isn't viewed merely as technique, but as diplomacy with spirits, ancestors, and the natural world.
Magical Traditions Integrated at Uagadou:
Kubandwa Initiations and Spirit Mediumship
Students learn initiatory rites involving ecstatic drumming and trance-possession, forming covenants with ancestral and heroic spirits (Bacwezi). These rites heal personal and communal discord by channeling messages, diagnoses, and prescriptions from ancestral spirits.
Balubaale and Ancestor Veneration
Balubaale practices instill respect for heroic ancestors, nature deities, and local spirits. Students master rituals, sacred drum rhythms, and taboos, using divination and healing rites at ancestral shrines located around the school.
Bakonzo Mountain Spirits and Nature Communion
Deep respect for Kitasamba and mountain spirits guides students to harmonize with the school's alpine surroundings. Offerings and rituals are performed at sacred waterfalls, caves, and shrines, ensuring protective harmony between students and powerful local spirits.
Nkisi Craft and Magical Artificing
The school's renowned Artificing department creates Minkisi—empowered charms that protect, heal, or bind oaths. Students learn the sacred cosmograms, herbal concoctions, and esoteric rituals of Minkisi, crafting objects that bridge the human and spirit worlds.
Mukanda Initiation and Secret Societies
Advanced students undergo initiatory experiences similar to the Mukanda rites, including seclusion, ritual tests, and spirit masquerades (Makishi). They gain secret knowledge of potent herbal medicines and magical practices that symbolize spiritual rebirth and communal responsibility.
Mbudye Society and Lukasa Memory Boards
At Uagadou, history and divination are intertwined through mnemonic training inspired by Luba kingdom Lukasa boards. Students "read" these intricate devices, gaining the ability to divine future outcomes from past events and maintaining oral magical histories.
Azande Oracles and Witchcraft Detection
Azande techniques are taught to advanced Defense students for identifying and neutralizing dark enchantments (Mangu). Students master divination methods, including simplified adaptations of termite and rubbing-board oracles, to diagnose and cure magical maladies.
Mugiro Cursing and Purification Rituals
Students learn the ethics and responsibility of curse-breaking and purification rites derived from Kikuyu tradition. They master ritual cleansing, herbal cures, and ceremonial language needed to counteract magical curses and restore spiritual purity.
Nilotic Rainmakers and Weather Divination
The Astronomy department instructs students in Nilotic-style weather and celestial divination. Students engage in ceremonial petitions, invoking sky spirits, learning to mediate between communities and the heavens for weather control and agricultural blessings.
Campus and Environment
Uagadou's sprawling campus blends seamlessly into the alpine landscape, with buildings nestled among sacred groves, waterfalls, and rock shelters—each serving as shrines to nature and ancestral spirits. Students inhabit a spiritually charged environment, rich with lore and magical potency. They are taught reverence, diplomacy, and respect toward the mountain’s powerful guardian spirits.

schools.name: Yahrra
schools.description:
Overview
Yahrra is a tiered, river‑laced campus of hardwood platforms, aerial rope bridges, and vine‑wrapped study towers. Courses narrow each year to let students specialize, but Charms remains compulsory for all seven years—the school believes every spellcaster must master negotiation with the rainforest’s watchful spirits. All other subjects gradually shift from required to elective as pupils refine their path.
Year
Core Courses
Elective Options
1
Charms, Defense, Potions, Runes, Transfiguration, Herbology, Arithmancy, Creatures
—
2
Charms, Defense, Potions, Herbology, Arithmancy, Creatures
Transfiguration or Runes
3
Charms, Defense, Potions, Herbology, Creatures
Transfiguration, Arithmancy, Runes
4–5
Charms, Defense, Herbology, Creatures
Potions, Runes, Transfiguration, Arithmancy
6
Charms, Defense
Potions, Runes, Transfiguration, Herbology, Arithmancy, Creatures
7
Charms
Defense, Potions, Runes, Transfiguration, Herbology, Arithmancy, Creatures
Core Traditions Embedded in Every Class
Tradition
How It Lives at Yahrra
Jaibaná Spirit Negotiation
First lesson in every practical subject: request permission from a plant, creature, or river spirit before use. Defense drills end with spirit‑barrier chants learned from Emberá jaibaná.
Nuchu Guardian Carving
Carved wooden figures are activated as Charms; students refine their guardian each year, embedding more complex chants.
Mola Symbol Weaving
Layered cloth panels become portable wards or razor‑sharp curse cloths. Symbol placement rules are taught in Charms (activation) and Runes (spoken‑form design).
Jaguar / Harpy‑Eagle Metamorphosis
Transfiguration electives progress from claw‑tips to full jaguar stride or harpy‑eagle flight for advanced messengers.
Rainforest Venom Alchemy
Potions labs distill frog toxins and curare; Arithmancy calculates safe micro‑doses and lunar potency spikes.
Isthmian Star Navigation
Arithmancy charts “Caiman Stars” and “Hummingbird Path” to time long‑range protection charms and night‑brews.
Congo‑Root Drum Casting
Defensive wards and many Charms are spoken in call‑and‑response rhythm; footwork circles echo maroon Congo society rites.
Architecture (high‑level)
•
River Spine – floating docks for Creatures labs and potion‑ingredient gathering.
•
Canopy Forum – circular platform 25 m up, edged with spirit‑permission totems; serves as Charms arena.
•
Venom Vault – limestone cave kept at constant humidity for long‑ferment draughts.
•
Mola Walk – covered weaving hall where students layer spell‑textiles and practise runic incantation patterns.

schools.name: Zainnur
schools.description:
Zainnur School of Magic
Veiled behind the Tamul Waterfall, Huasteca Potosina, México
Overview
Zainnur occupies a labyrinth of basalt chambers carved into the cliff that frames Tamul Waterfall. A shimmering water curtain hides the main gate; narrow shafts carry sunlight and mist into classrooms and dormitories buried deep in the rock. From Years 1‑4 every student follows the same seven‑course load. In Years 5‑7 only Charms, Defense, and Runes remain compulsory, while four applied subjects become electives.
Years
Core Courses
Elective Options
1 – 4
Charms · Defense · Potions · Muggle Studies · Runes · History · Creatures
—
5 – 7
Charms · Defense · Runes
Potions · Muggle Studies · History · Creatures
Philosophy — Four Living Currents
Current
Cultural Root
What Students Practise
River‑Permission
Huastec water‑offering rites
Every spell or harvest that touches the river starts with a two‑line chant and a drop of honeyed water; without permission, magic weakens.
Cavern‑Echo Discipline
Nahua cave‑underworld lore
Wand strokes and spoken runes must “lock” to the cliff’s natural reverberation; daily drills refine timing to the millisecond.
Storm‑as‑Shield
Tlaloc & Chaac invocations
Defense classes pull falling spray into vertical curtains that ground lightning and blunt shock‑waves.
Silver‑Vein Brewing
Colonial‑era ore‑infusion notes
Potion masters age draughts on mineral drips in abandoned silver tunnels; trace metals and waterfall mist give unmatched resilience.
